En: A memory from another life -- when you were young and fit.
Zh: 来自另一种生活的记忆——那时的你年轻有为，苗条健康。

En: A warm wave of accomplishment washes over your head, as you drop the barbell to the floor. For a moment it feels like you're strong enough to succeed at anything you ever set your mind to.
Zh: 你把杠铃扔在地板上，一股温热的成就感从你的头顶倾泄而下。片刻之间，你感觉自己是那么的强大，只要下定决心，就一定能够成功。

En: Look, Kim, it's a trap. There are no collars on the barbell.
Zh: “看，金，这是个陷阱。这个杠铃上没有夹圈！”

En: A barbell lies on the floor. The colour has worn off its weight plates.
Zh: 地上躺着一只杠铃。夹圈的颜色已经脱落了。

En: There are no collars on the barbell. This is a safety hazard.
Zh: 杠铃上没有夹圈。这是一个安全隐患。

En: You manage to hoist it off the ground, but the barbell feels wobbly and dangerous, your hands slick with sweat. Turns out you're no beast, just an old man with bad form.
Zh: 你设法把它从地上提了起来，但是杠铃感觉有些摇晃，很是危险，你的双手开始出汗打滑。结果你根本不是什么野兽，只是个体型糟糕的老男人。

En: What kind of a bastard would just remove the collars? It should be a felony.
Zh: “是哪个混账玩意把夹圈卸下来的？这绝对是重罪。”

En: A warm wave of accomplishment washes over your head, as you drop the barbell to the floor. For a moment it feels like you're strong enough to succeed at anything you ever set your mind to.
Zh: 你把杠铃扔在地板上，一股温热的成就感从你的头顶倾泄而下。片刻之间，你感觉自己是那么的强大，只要下定决心，就一定能够成功。

En: Conjuring up an inhuman amount of strength, you raise the barbell up in the air. Your biceps tremble, but you're a savage. This is a children's game!
Zh: 你奇迹般地激发出超乎常人的力量，把杠铃举到了空中。你的肱二头肌在颤抖，不过你是一个野蛮人。这不过是小孩子的游戏！

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Hey, but you're still in a ghost house... What if someone heard this? What if they know you're here?!
Zh: 喂，你还在一栋鬼屋里……如果被什么人听见呢？如果他们不知道你在这里呢？！

En: See, see! This place is cursed!
Zh: 看见没，看见没！这个地方被诅咒了！

En: It was never my favourite, either. The lieutenant is obviously handling you.
Zh: “这也从来都不是我的最爱。”警督显然是在应付你。

En: At the station gym, I mean. I prefer running. It clears your head... The lieutenant steps away from the barbell, letting your recover in peace.
Zh: “我的意思是说，在警局的健身房。我更喜欢跑步，能让你清空大脑……”警督走到杠铃旁边，给你留了点安静恢复的空间。

En: You're right, the weights may fall off. Better not touch it then.
Zh: “你说得对，杠铃片可能会掉下来。最好还是别碰了。”

En: It's 60 kg. Your triceps hum at the sight of these weights. Show the world what kind of beast it's dealing with. Lift them.
Zh: 这是60公斤重的杠铃。你的肱三头肌一看到这些重量就发出了不满的声音。让这个世界看看，它要对付的是哪种野兽。把杠铃举起来。

En: Lift the barbell.
Zh: 举起杠铃。

En: Noticed collars.
Zh: 注意到夹圈。

En: Head hurts.
Zh: 头很疼。

En: High-performance weightlifting gloves.
Zh: 高性能举重手套。

En: Coached by Physical Instrument
Zh: 受强身健体指导。

En: Even your body has failed you. It's a miracle you didn't injure yourself.
Zh: 就连你的身体也辜负了你。你没伤到自己简直就是奇迹。

En: Maybe it wasn't such a good idea to smash through that back door...
Zh: 也许砸破那扇后门不是个好主意……

En: It would be a violation of EPIS safety regulations if the gym was still operating. But it isn't. No one's supposed to come here anymore.
Zh: “如果健身房还在运营的话，那就违反了EPIS的安全条例。但是它没有。应该不会有人来这里了。”

En: Whatever, weightlifting is for the intellectually impaired anyway.
Zh: “无所谓了，反正举重是为那些智力受损的人准备的。”

En: Seems like I'm a little out of shape. Or maybe these gloves just suck...
Zh: “看来我的体型有点走样了。或者只是这副手套太垃圾了……”

En: Seems like I'm a little out of shape. Or maybe my palms were just slippery...
Zh: “看来我的体型有点走样了。或者只是我的手掌心太滑了……”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Fuck you, you stupid barbell!
Zh: “去你的吧，该死的杠铃！”

En: Wearing gloves would definitely make it easier, the lieutenant agrees.
Zh: “戴着手套肯定会容易一点，”警督表示赞同。

En: Proper weightlifting gloves would definitely afford a better grip, the lieutenant agrees.
Zh: “合适的举重手套肯定能提供更好的抓力，”警督表示赞同。

En: Weightlifting was never my favourite, either. The lieutenant is obviously *handling* you.
Zh: “举重也一直都不是我的最爱。”警督显然是在*应付*你。

En: Actually, I don't really care about safety hazards.
Zh: “其实呢，我真的不在乎什么安全隐患。”

En: No one does, until they get smashed under the barbell.
Zh: “谁都不在乎，直到他们被压在杠铃底下。”

En: Oh yeah.
Zh: “哦耶。”

En: I'm a true champion!
Zh: “我是真正的冠军！”

En: (Say nothing, revel in the feeling.)
Zh: （什么也不说，沉浸在这种感觉里。）

En: Good technique. The lieutenant nods with approval.
Zh: “技巧很不错。”警督点头表示赞同。

En: The barbell waits patiently on the floor, like a dog for its master.
Zh: 杠铃耐心地躺在地板上等待着，就像一条等待主人的狗。

En: Why does it feel so familiar?
Zh: 为什么感觉这么熟悉？

En: Is this familiar because I'm a weightlifter?
Zh: 这种熟悉的感觉，难道我是个举重运动员吗？

En: No, it's not that... It's the stale smell of rubber, the squeaky sound of sneakers, your bruised knee against the mat. And a whistle... then the feeling is gone.
Zh: 不，不是的……是橡胶那陈腐的味道，运动鞋嘎吱嘎吱的声响，是你青肿的膝盖抵在垫子上的感觉。还有那声口哨……然后这种感觉消失了。

En: It's just a memory.
Zh: 这只是一段记忆。

En: A giant electric mammal awakens. The dead body is going to be safe in the embrace of its cold innards.
Zh: 一只巨大的电动哺乳动物被唤醒了。在它冰冷内脏的环抱之下，这具尸体安全了。

En: Great. The owners of this building will just have to keep paying for the electricity sucked up by this monstrosity a little longer. Let's return to the fridge.
Zh: “很好。这栋建筑的业主只能为这个畸形再多付一些电费了。我们回冰箱那里去吧。”

En: Uh, what are you doing? We need the fridge for the body, you can't just unplug it. He takes the cable and plugs it back in.
Zh: “呃，你在做什么？我们需要冰箱冰冻尸体，你不能就这么电源拔出来。”他拿起电线，又重新插了回去。

En: Two cables are plugged into the breaker box. The red one leads off into the darkness and the black one to the ice cream maker nearby.
Zh: 开关箱里插着两根电线。红色那根通向黑暗之中，黑色那根通往附近的冰淇淋制造机。

En: Unplug the giant red cable.
Zh: 拔掉那根巨大的红色电线。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Plug in the giant red cable.
Zh: 插上那根巨大的红色电线。

En: An electric sizzle! The room is slightly quieter now.
Zh: 一阵电流的嘶嘶声！现在房间变得稍微安静了一些。

En: Somewhere in the dark, a giant electric mammal stirs to life...
Zh: 在这片黑暗中的某个地方，一只巨型电动哺乳动物苏醒了……

En: Rip the red one out! Look how giant and red and inviting it is...
Zh: 把红色那根扯掉！看看它，多么巨大，多么鲜红，多么诱人……

En: Unplug the black cable.
Zh: 拔掉黑色电线。

En: Plug in the black cable.
Zh: 插入黑色电线。

En: Somewhere a machine hums along with the current, happy and contented.
Zh: 在某个地方，一台机器随着电流一起发出嗡嗡的声音，愉悦又满足。

En: Something close to you dies with a soft electric purr.
Zh: 伴随着一阵轻柔的颤动声，离你很近的某个东西沉寂了下来。

En: Two cables are plugged into the breaker box. The red one leads off into the darkness, while the black one seems to be feeding a buzzing little fridge-like thing in the corner.
Zh: 开关箱里插着两根电线。红色那根通向黑暗之中，黑色那根像是在为角落里那个像冰箱一样嗡嗡直响的东西提供能量。

En: Two cables are plugged into the breaker box. The red one leads to the ice bear fridge and the black one to the ice cream maker nearby.
Zh: 开关箱里插着两根电线。红色那根通往冰熊冰箱，黑色那根通往附近的冰淇淋制造机。

En: Two cables are plugged into the breaker box. The red one leads to the ice bear fridge and the black one to some kind of a buzzing fridge-like thing nearby.
Zh: 开关箱里插着两根电线。红色那根通往冰熊冰箱，黑色那根像是在为角落里那个像冰箱一样嗡嗡直响的东西提供能量。

En: Don't rip the red one out -- you'll awaken the ghosts!
Zh: 别把那根红色的电线拔掉——你会唤醒那些幽灵的！

En: Very smart. Opening the lid should be much easier after the ice cream maker has defrosted.
Zh: “很聪明。冰淇淋制造机解冻之后，打开盖子应该就容易多了。”

En: Why did you do that?
Zh: “你为什么要这么做？”

En: It's black, it's not like it's the red one.
Zh: “它是黑色的，感觉不太像红色那根。”

En: Because it's black, the colour of immeasurable cosmos.
Zh: “因为它是黑色的，那是无限宇宙的颜色。”

En: I don't know why I unplugged it, I do things without any reason.
Zh: “我不知道为什么要拔下来，我做事没有任何理由。”

En: Okay. That still doesn't mean you can open the ice cream maker, you know? I bet it's still pretty much impossible...
Zh: “好吧。那也不代表你能打开那台冰淇淋制造机，你知道的吧。我打赌几乎不太可能……”

En: The lieutenant raises his brows, but doesn't say anything. The electric distribution board now has one cable missing.
Zh: 警督扬起眉头，但是一句话也没说。现在配电板上少了一根电线。

En: An electric sizzle! The room is slightly quieter now.
Zh: 一阵电流的嘶嘶声！现在房间变得稍微安静了一些。

En: Both cables are unplugged from the breaker box.
Zh: 开关箱上的两根电线都被拔下来了。

En: Only the red cable is plugged into the breaker box, while the black one lies neglected on the floor.
Zh: 只有红色电线还插在开关箱上，黑色那根被忽视地躺在地板上。

En: Only the black cable is plugged into the breaker box, while the red one lies neglected on the floor.
Zh: 只有黑色电线还插在开关箱上，红色那根被忽视地躺在地板上。

En: Great. The owners of this building will just have to keep paying for the electricity sucked up by this monstrosity a little longer.
Zh: “很好。这栋建筑的业主只能为这个畸形再多付一些电费了。”

En: A giant electric mammal awakens. The dead body is now safe in the embrace of its cold innards.
Zh: 一只巨大的电动哺乳动物被唤醒了。在它冰冷内脏的环抱之下，这具尸体安全了。

En: Very smart. Opening the lid should be much easier after the ice cream maker has defrosted. But we're still going to need a proper prybar.
Zh: “很聪明。冰淇淋制造机解冻之后，打开盖子应该就容易多了。不过我们还是需要一根合适的撬棍。”

En: Maybe you could *paint* something with this coal? Leave a cave painting for future archaeologists... no, that would be stupid.
Zh: 也许你可以用这块煤*涂画*点什么？给未来的考古学家留下一副洞穴壁画……不，这样太蠢了。

En: Still, it's good you have this dirt on you. Real men wear *coal* for make-up.
Zh: 不过，身上沾着这些煤灰挺好的。真男人是用*煤*来装扮自己的。

En: Always has to be the sceptic, this man...
Zh: 这家伙一直都是个怀疑论者……

En: Then what was the chatter you heard?
Zh: 那你听见的吱吱声是什么？

En: This sounded real, not imagined.
Zh: 这个声音很真实，不是想象出来的。

En: The echo is so prominent, it's impossible to discern what the voices are saying. Or what's producing them.
Zh: 回音太过突出，很难分辨出那个声音在说什么。或者是怎么发出来的。

En: Another pathetic yelp sounds off into the vast darkness of the chimney. You're a little embarrassed you produced it.
Zh: 又一声凄惨的叫声在烟囱那无边无际的黑暗中响起。作为声音源头的你都觉得有点窘迫了。

En: Your throat can produce little more than a dry croak. Awkward, since you already had a drink...
Zh: 你的喉咙只能发出一些干涩的嘎嘎声。尴尬，因为你已经喝过一杯了……

En: Your dehydrated, hungover throat can produce little more than a dry croak.
Zh: 除了一阵干燥沙哑的声音，你那干涸又难受的喉咙几乎什么声音也发不出来。

En: He... he... hekkhhhh...
A thick layer of coal dust covers the furnace, colouring it pitch-black.
Zh: 壁炉上盖了一层厚厚的煤灰，把它染成了墨黑色。

En: We should ask the lady in the bookstore what's going on with these curtains.
Zh: “我们应该问问书店那位女士，这些窗帘到底是怎么回事。”

En: A thick layer of coal dust covers the furnace, colouring it pitch-black.
Zh: 壁炉上盖了一层厚厚的煤灰，把它染成了墨黑色。

En: Kim, what is this thing? Is it a furnace?!
Zh: “金，这是什么？是壁炉吗？！”

En: Look inside the furnace.
Zh: 看向壁炉内部。

En: Kick it with your foot.
Zh: 用脚踢它。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Smear your hands with coal.
Zh: 用煤灰把手弄脏。

En: A lush layer of coal now covers your skin, sinking into the wrinkles. Your hands look ancient.
Zh: 现在你的皮肤上覆盖了一层厚厚的煤灰，一直陷入到皱纹里面。你的双手也变得古老起来。

En: You feel the spirit of Ramout Karzai, ancient hero of Graad, pulsing through you. All that's left is to cover your face in war-paint.
Zh: 你感觉到古代的格拉德英雄——哈德拉姆特·卡赛尔的鬼魂，在你的身体里脉动。剩下的就是在你的脸涂上战争油彩。

En: Hadramut Karzai! Smear your cheeks black with coal.
Zh: 哈德拉姆特·卡赛尔！用煤灰把你的面颊抹黑。

En: No, let's not express ourselves. Let's just wipe our hands clean on our pants.
Zh: 不，还是不要自我表达了。在裤子上把手擦干净吧。

En: Three dangerous stripes appear onto your cheeks, telling stories of your wild soul.
Zh: 三道危险的条纹出现在你脸上，诉说着你狂野灵魂的故事。

En: Yes, how grown-up. The furnace agrees.
Zh: 是啊，你可真是个成年人。壁炉表示赞同。

En: A hollow ring echoes through the furnace. Your toe hurts.
Zh: 一阵空旷的响声在壁炉里回荡。你的脚趾头感到有点疼。

En: Looks like it. Looks like an old central furnace used to heat the building. It's connected to the chimney... He opens the door and gingerly peeks inside.
Zh: “看起来是。它像是给建筑供暖的旧中央壁炉。跟烟囱是连在一起的……”他打开门，小心地朝里面看了一眼。

En: What... what are you doing?
Zh: “你……你在干什么？”

En: Yell "Hello!" into the furnace.
Zh: 对着壁炉里面大声“问候！”一下。

En: Warpainted your face.
Zh: 在脸上涂上战争油彩。

En: Kicked the furnace.
Zh: 踢壁炉一脚。

En: Sing from the diaphragm!
Zh: 从隔膜发声歌唱。

En: Wet your whistle.
Zh: 润润嗓子。

En: Called Slipstream SCA again.
Zh: 再打给气流SCA。

En: Helping Soona with research.
Zh: 帮助苏娜做研究。

En: Dresscode: Modus Mullen.
Zh: 着装要求：模式马伦。

En: I am the reincarnation of an ancient Iilmaraan warrior.
Zh: “我是远古伊尔玛战士的化身。”

En: Nothing! (Wipe your face clean.) "Sorry, that was stupid."
Zh: “没什么！”（把脸擦干净。）“抱歉，刚才好像有点太蠢了。”

En: Yes. Yes, it was, the lieutenant nods, before turning back to the furnace.
Zh: “是的。是有点，”警督点点头，然后转回到壁炉前。

En: Please wipe your face clean, officer.
Zh: “请把脸擦干净，警官。”

En: No, you're a proud warrior, keep it.
Zh: 不，你是一个骄傲的战士，留着它。

En: Wipe it clean.
Zh: 擦干净。

En: Thank you, the lieutenant nods. "So, where were we?" He turns back to the furnace.
Zh: “谢谢，”警督点点头。“所以，我们说到哪了？”他再次转回到壁炉前。

En: Hello? you hear a woman's voice answer.
Zh: “你好？”你听见一个女声回答到。

En: Hello! Are you there? Speak to me!
Zh: “喂！你在吗？跟我说话！”

En: This is the Remote Viewers Division! Identify yourself!
Zh: “我来自遥视者部队！报上名来！”

En: This is the police! Who's there?
Zh: “我是警察！你是谁？”

En: You muster all your strength and yell...
Zh: 你鼓足全身的力量大喊……

En: The lieutenant seems relieved as well.
Zh: 警督好像也松了一口气。

En: Then again -- maybe it's worth actually trying something *up there*?
Zh: 还有——也许真值得去*上面*试试呢？

En: Don't wipe it.
Zh: 别擦掉。

En: Kim doesn't trust you.
Zh: 金不相信你。

En: Kim trusts you.
Zh: 金相信你。

En: Looks like this furnace has a face and it's a face of agony.
Zh: 看起来这个壁炉像是有一张脸，一张痛苦的脸。

En: These three stripes give you strength in this dangerous realm. It would be foolish to remove them.
Zh: 在这个危险的领域，脸上那三道条纹给了你力量。擦掉的话就太愚蠢了。

En: You can feel your cheeks turn red under the lieutenant's gaze -- you look like a kid, not a police officer. Even the war-paint can't conceal the embarrassment.
Zh: 在警督的凝视下，你感觉自己脸红了——你看起来像个小孩，而不是个警察。就连战争油彩都难以掩饰这种尴尬。

En: We're exploring this dark place and I need the protection my war-paint affords.
Zh: “我们正在黑暗的地方探索，我需要战争油彩的保护。”

En: And *this* protects you?
Zh: “你说*这个*能保护你？”

En: It gives me confidence and that's all that matters, Kim.
Zh: “它带给我信心，这才是最关键的，金。”

En: Okay, sure. Go ahead. He stubbornly looks away from you and the furnace.
Zh: “好吧，当然。那你随便吧。”他倔强的移开目光，不去看你和那个壁炉。

En: *This* is traditional war-paint. It will grant me safe passage with the spirits that guard this place.
Zh: “*这是*传统的战争油彩。它能让我和守卫在这里的灵魂一起安全通过。”

En: Just pointing out -- this is *not* traditional Semenese war-paint. No idea where you got it.
Zh: 在这里指明一点——这*不是*传统的塞美尼战争油彩。不知道你是从哪里学来的。

En: What a proud warrior! This is how the hunted becomes the hunter in this dark dangerous place.
Zh: 多么骄傲又自豪的战士啊！这样一来，猎物才会在这个黑暗又危险的地方变成猎人。

En: And it's a woman! Of course! The malicious entities are *always* women.
Zh: 是个女人！当然了！那些邪恶的存在*一直*都是女人。

En: I summon the ghost of this Doomed Commercial Area. Answer me, spirit!
Zh: “这片诅咒商业区的幽灵，我在召唤你。回应我吧，幽灵！”

En: Hello! Did you say anything? There's a pause. "I can't hear you, please come upstairs! There's a safety curtain on the second floor, I'll open it!"
Zh: “你好！你刚才说什么了吗？”稍微停顿了一下。“我听不见，请到楼上来！二楼有个防火幕，我这就把它打开！”

En: You hear a low rumble upstairs -- the sound of a curtain being pulled aside.
Zh: 你听见楼上传来一阵低沉的隆隆声——那是窗帘被拉开的声音。

En: Something *breaks loose* in you. A mighty bellow echoes throughout the chimney's depths. The chatter of tiny voices above suddenly cease. Then...
Zh: 有什么东西从你身体里*挣脱*了出来。一阵强而有力的吼叫声在烟囱深处回响。上方细微的声音颤动突然停了下来。然后……

En: You've awakened the entity!
Zh: 你唤醒那个存在了！

En: I summon the ghost of this Doomed Commercial Area. Now begone, vile succubus!
Zh: “这片诅咒商业区的幽灵，我在召唤你。快滚开，邪恶女妖！”

En: Hmm, maybe you should let your voice rest, officer. Try again later.
Zh: “嗯，也许你应该让自己的嗓子稍微休息一下，警官。之后再试。”

En: A lifetime of smoking and drinking will do that to you.
Zh: 一辈子抽烟喝酒的话，你就会变成那个样子。

En: The chatter from the chimney continue on as before. You seem to have made no impression on whatever's *up there*.
Zh: 烟囱里的吱吱声像之前一样继续着。不管*上面*有什么，你似乎没给它留下任何印象。

En: The lieutenant nods, then points upstairs. "After you, officer."
Zh: 警督点点头，然后指着楼上。“你先走，警官。”

En: No one has used it in ages... No signs of any recent fire, only dead rats.
Zh: “已经好多年没人用过了……没有近期发生火灾的痕迹，只有死老鼠。”

En: It's dark and grimy here. In the darkness, you can hear *chatter*. It's coming from above. A voice -- or several voices -- talking to each other, near the smoke chamber upstairs.
Zh: 这里又黑又脏。在黑暗中，你听到一阵*吱吱声*，是从上面传出来的。那个声音——或者说那些声音——就在楼上的烟室附近，彼此交谈着。

En: What are you doing? the lieutenant asks when he sees you climb half-way inside the furnace.
Zh: “你在做什么？”警督看着已经爬到壁炉中间的你，开口问到。

En: I'm hallucinating. Or maybe it's the Entity...
Zh: “我产生幻觉了。或许是那个存在……”

En: I'm hallucinating.
Zh: “我产生幻觉了。”

En: I hear the murderer of the hanged man talking.
Zh: “我听见绞死那个男人的凶手在说话。”

En: I'm not sure, Kim... but I think I can hear someone talking upstairs.
Zh: “我不确定，金……但我好像听到楼上有人说话。”

En: Wait, really? He looks up at the ceiling. "Maybe it's coming from behind those safety curtains we saw upstairs?"
Zh: “等等，真的吗？”他抬头看着天花板。“还记得我们在楼上看见的那些防火幕吗，也许就是从那背后传出来的。”

En: Wait, really? The lieutenant sounds concerned. "Take your head out of the chimney, please. It's not safe."
Zh: “等等，真的吗？”警督听起来有些担心。“请把你的头从烟囱里伸出来。这样不安全。”

En: It feels safe to know that the lieutenant's got your back, now and always.
Zh: 知道警督就在你的身后，让你感觉很安全，一如既往。

En: Wait, really? He looks up at the ceiling. "We should investigate, see if someone's upstairs."
Zh: “等等，真的吗？”他抬头看着天花板。“我们应该调查一下，看看楼上有没有人。”

En: Those voices I heard... Maybe it's the malignant Entity? Plaisance said it lives in a chimney.
Zh: “我听到的那些声音……也许就是那个邪恶的存在？普莱桑斯说它住在一个烟囱里。”

En: You're right, the rooms do look like they're connected. But *malignant Entities* don't exist -- at least not the *supra-natural* kind.
Zh: “你说得对，这些房间看起来确实是相连的。但是*邪恶的存在*并不存在——至少*超自然*那类不存在。”

En: What's happening?!
Zh: 发生什么了？！

En: I'm hallucinating...
Zh: 我产生幻觉了……

En: I hear the murderer of the hanged man talking.
Zh: 我听见绞死那个男人的凶手在说话。

En: I'm getting carbon monoxide poisoning.
Zh: 我要一氧化碳中毒了。

En: Maybe it's coming from behind those safety curtains you saw upstairs? You should investigate...
Zh: 也许是之前在楼上看见的那些防火幕背后传出来的？你应该调查一下……

En: Maybe it's coming from somewhere upstairs? You should investigate...
Zh: 也许是楼上的某个地方传出来的？你应该调查一下……

En: Could it be psychological? Because your physical health is fine.
Zh: 也许是心理作用？因为你的身体健康没什么问题。

En: This sounded real, not imagined.
Zh: 这个声音很真实，不是想象出来的。

En: Or maybe you're just hungover again.
Zh: 又或者，你又是宿醉未醒。

En: There must be another way to wake up whoever is in there... Maybe we can try yelling into the furnace downstairs?
Zh: “不管里面住了什么东西，肯定有其他办法把它唤醒……也许我们可以试试朝楼下的那个壁炉里大叫？”

En: Hello? I yelled, but it didn't work...
Zh: “喂？我喊了，但是没什么用……”

En: Again your voice becomes a raspy croak, mid-sentence. It's silent in the abandoned office building -- no one answers you.
Zh: 你的声音再次变成一阵刺耳而沙哑的叫唤，话也被吞没了一半。废弃的办公大楼里一片寂静——没有人回应你。

En: Sire, may we suggest something? Think of the yelling-into-the-furnace as a *grand performance*. Use your chest voice, not your head voice. Sing from the diaphragm.
Zh: 大人，我能提个建议吗？您就把朝着壁炉里大喊当成是一个*盛大的演出*。使用您的胸音，不要用头音。从隔膜发声歌唱。

En: A sudden bout of self pity comes over you.
Zh: 你突然产生一种自怜的感觉。

En: Perhaps it's not such a bad idea? Try it again, son.
Zh: 也许这主意也不算太糟糕？再试一次，小子。

En: As before, an iron safety curtain curves before your eyes. It covers half the room -- blocking the way into a colossal industrial chimney.
Zh: 就像之前一样，一道铁质的防火幕挡在你眼前，像风箱一样褶曲着。它遮住了半个房间——挡住了通往巨大工业烟囱的道路。

En: There must be another way to wake up whoever is in there... Maybe we should ask the lady in the bookstore what's going on here?
Zh: “不管谁在那里，肯定有其他办法把人叫醒……也许我们应该问问书店的那位女士是怎么回事？”

En: There must be another way to wake up whoever is in there... Maybe you should talk to the lady in the bookstore, ask what's going on?
Zh: 不管谁在那里，肯定有其他办法把人叫醒……也许我们应该跟书店的那位女士聊聊，问问是怎么回事？

En: You took your shot and still missed. Well, you did your best, champ. Shame it wasn't good enough.
Zh: 你尝试过，但还是错过了。不过，你已经尽力了，冠军。忏愧的是，这样还不够好。

En: An iron safety curtain curves before your eyes, folded like a bellows. It covers half the room -- blocking the way into a colossal industrial chimney.
Zh: 一道铁质的防火幕出现在你眼前，像风箱一样褶曲着。它遮住了半个房间——挡住了通往巨大工业烟囱的道路。

En: Knock on it.
Zh: 敲敲它。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: What an odd thing to do.... Nothing happens.
Zh: 这么做也太奇怪了吧……什么也没发生。

En: Knock on it harder.
Zh: 再敲大力一点。

En: Still nothing. No one's home.
Zh: 还是什么都没有。没人在家。

En: Knock *even* harder!
Zh: 敲的*再*用力一些！

En: Those curtains prove to be surprisingly sturdy... Your fist hurts now.
Zh: 这些窗帘真是惊人的结实……现在你的拳头开始疼了。

En: Can you please try to refrain from attacking random things?
Zh: “你能不能尽量克制一下，不要随意攻击什么东西？”

En: This is not random, this is *significant*. I want to see what's on the other side.
Zh: “这可不是随意的，这很*重要*。我想看看另一边有什么。”

En: I can try... (Blow gently on your bruised knuckles.)
Zh: “我可以试试……”（对着青肿的指关节轻轻吹一口气。）

En: Thanks.
Zh: “谢谢。”

En: Why do you think there's something on the other side necessarily? the lieutenant asks, but you can see a spark of curiosity in his eyes.
Zh: “你为什么会觉得对面肯定有东西呢？”警督询问着，但你可以在他的眼神中看到一丝好奇的光芒。

En: I need to find the *malignant entity*, Kim. This is the chimney.
Zh: “我需要找到那个*邪恶的存在*，金。这就是那个烟囱。”

En: The lieutenant sighs. "Ghosts aren't real, didn't your parents tell you that? In any case, there's no way we can get in right now. Let's investigate further."
Zh: 金叹叹气。“幽灵是不存在的，难道你父母没告诉过你吗？无论如何，我们现在是进不去的。之后再来调查吧。”

En: This must be where the *Entity* lives...
Zh: 这肯定就是那个*存在*居住的地方……

En: If this is really an entrance to the chimney then there must be a furnace somewhere as well. Maybe there's another way to get in?
Zh: 如果烟囱真有入口，那这里的某个地方肯定有一个壁炉。也许还有其他进去的办法？

En: There must be another way to wake up whoever is in there... Maybe you should try yelling into the furnace downstairs?
Zh: 不管里面住了什么东西，肯定有其他办法把它唤醒……也许你应该试试朝楼下的那个壁炉里大叫？

En: This is directly above the central furnace in the cellar. The voices probably came from here.
Zh: 这里就是地窖那个中央壁炉的正上方。声音可能就是从这里传出来的。

En: In any case, there's no way we can get in right now. Let's investigate further.
Zh: “无论如何，我们现在是进不去的。之后再来调查吧。”

En: We're inside the chimney of an old central furnace. It's strange, I know. She looks at the ruddy bricks that make up the walls -- even though they've been repainted there are still signs of coal-black soot here and there.
Zh: “我们正处在一个旧中央壁炉的烟囱里。我知道，是有点奇怪。”她看着墙壁上那些红色的砖块——虽然它们已经被重新粉刷过，但到处依然残留着乌黑烟灰的痕迹。

En: You know... this is something the Cafeteria Manager might want to know.
Zh: 你知道……这可能是餐厅经理想知道的事情。

En: Agreed, the lieutenant chips in, "pinball is the worst."
Zh: “同意，”警督插了一嘴，“弹球是最糟糕的。”

En: My advice... She raises her finger. "Don't base your business on a fad -- hypnotism, floriography, triktrak, especially *pinball*..."
Zh: “我的建议……”她竖起手指。“不要把你的生意建立在一时的狂热上——催眠术，花语，双陆棋，特别是*弹球*。”

En: This means the Whirling is part of the *Doomed Commercial Area*. Garte is gonna *love* this...
Zh: “这就意味着飞旋旅社也是*诅咒商业区*的一部分。加尔特一定会*很喜欢*这个的……”

En: Keep it to yourself.
Zh: 自己保密。

En: I don't know this Garte -- but something tells me he won't.
Zh: “我不认识这个加尔特——但是我感觉他不会的。”

En: Anything else I can help you with?
Zh: “还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: As she speaks her bone-like fingers fiddle with a ring. Her bones light, but her hands strong.
Zh: 她一边说，一边用骷髅般的手指摆弄着戒指。她的骨头很轻，但是双手却很强壮。

En: This person means you -- or no one else -- absolutely no harm. She will answer freely and honestly.
Zh: 这个人对你——或者其他任何人——绝对没有任何恶意。她会慷概又诚实地回答你的问题。

En: No falsehoods are present. She's a novelty dicemaker and doesn't have anything to hide. Ask what you need.
Zh: 没有说谎。她是一个新奇骰子匠人，没什么好隐瞒的。需要什么就问什么吧。

En: Loving it. So nifty.
Zh: 我喜欢。多漂亮啊。

En: She's heard of the murder, but did not see it, sire.
Zh: 她听说过凶杀案，但是没有见到，大人。

En: She's not sorry to disappoint you. Informing on someone in a murder investigation would intrude upon her focused existence.
Zh: 让你失望这一点并没有让她感觉到抱歉。在凶杀案调查中告发某人会干扰她专注的生活。

En: It's light here -- but dark in the yard at night.
Zh: 这里很亮——但是晚上院子里很黑。

En: This person values focus above all -- she keeps her sharp sight on the instruments before her.
Zh: 这个人把专注看得比一切都重要——她对面前的工具保持着敏锐的观察力。

En: Do you often work Sunday nights?
Zh: “你经常会在周日晚上工作吗？”

En: It's an odd profession -- making dice for people -- but I like it. And I *prefer* doing this to sitting at home.
Zh: “这是个古怪的职业——帮人做骰子——但是我喜欢。而且我*更喜欢*坐在家里干活。”

En: When she arrived here, there was no room anywhere else... She must've known the other businesses.
Zh: 她来到这里的时候，已经没有其他空间了……她一定知道其他公司。

En: Oh, quite a lot of them spring to mind.
Zh: 哦，想到了很多。

En: Rats scuttle in the dark rooms, under the abandoned blow driers and dusty mannequins, cobwebs cover rotors and radiocomputers alike... so much failure.
Zh: 黑暗的房间里，老鼠在废弃的吹风机和布满灰尘的人体模特下面奔跑，蜘蛛网盖住了转子和无线电脑一样的东西……一眼望去全是失败。

En: You should've known it!
Zh: 你早该知道的！

En: It's not really full of that. No one would store their drugs like that.
Zh: 并不是真的全是那个。没人会像这样储藏毒品的。

En: Poor guy... suddenly you get a feeling that insects are important to the case somehow. It's hard to say why.
Zh: 可怜的家伙……你突然有种感觉，昆虫对这个案子很重要。很难说清是为什么。

En: As she shifts around, you notice several dead flies on the windowsill in front of her. Legs up, they're not moving.
Zh: 她转过身，你注意到她面前的窗沿上有很多死苍蝇。四脚朝天，没有动作。

En: Maybe you could make a sword out of one... No, wait, forget it. It would take too long.
Zh: 也许你可以做一把剑，就用这个……不，等等，还是算了吧。那样太费时间了。

En: They were just props. Why return to them?
Zh: 它们只是道具。为什么要回到它们身上呢？

En: She liked them.
Zh: 她喜欢他们。

En: That's understandable. Fantasies are serious things. The mind is the drawing board of history.
Zh: 这个可以理解。梦想是很严肃的事情。思想就是历史的画板。

En: Something strains her face, before she looks up again:
Zh: 她绷紧了脸，然后再次抬起头：

En: The result is 1. On a 20-sided die.
Zh: 结果是1。在一个20面的骰子上面。

En: The dice is black and filled with little silvery flakes, like snowfall.
Zh: 骰子是黑色，里面满是银色的小白点，像雪花一样。

En: Sounds cool.
Zh: 听起来挺酷的。

En: Scary... but *cool*.
Zh: 有点可怕……但是*很酷*。

En: The temperature has dropped in the cover of the night; you see frost on the windows.
Zh: 夜幕降临，气温下降了；你在窗户上看见霜花。

En: Little sparkling embers under the window...
Zh: 窗户下面有小小的光点在闪烁……

En: Outside it's light. Light scatters from the low hanging cloud cover. There's always the threat of snow.
Zh: 外面有光。光线从低垂的云层中散射而下。下雪的威胁一直存在。

En: Anything else? Another failed business perhaps? I've been here for a long time...
Zh: “还有别的事吗？也许再说一个破产的故事？我在这里待了很久……”

En: His disdain for pinball could not be clearer.
Zh: 他对弹球的鄙视已经清晰得不能再清晰了。

En: If the Whirling is part of the same building then it's part of the Doomed Commercial Area. The darkness of this place is there too...
Zh: 如果飞旋旅社也是同一栋建筑的一部分，那它也属于诅咒商业区的一部分。笼罩在这里的黑暗，那里也有……

En: That's all she has to say on the subject. She *has* been thorough -- and truthful -- as far as we can see.
Zh: 关于这个话题，她只能说这么多了。她*已经*很透彻——而且很真诚——以吾辈目前所见。

En: Plaisance needs to hear about this. Perhaps if you combine your *psychic energies* you'll make sense of the situation.
Zh: 普莱桑斯需要听听这个。也许结合一下你的*心灵能量*，你就能搞清这里的情况。

En: Plaisance is not going to like what you have to tell her.
Zh: 普莱桑斯不会喜欢你接下来要告诉她的话。

En: That's all she has to say on the subject. She's been thorough and truthful as far as we can see.
Zh: 关于这个话题，她只能说这么多了。以吾辈目前所见，她已经很透彻，而且很真诚了。

En: Aside from getting naked, you're not sure what else to do. The building holds no more answers for you.
Zh: 除了裸体，你不知道还能做什么。这栋大楼已经没有你要找的答案了。

En: A tool to get the zipper open?
Zh: 能把拉链拉开的工具？

En: No. The old coal plant that used to be here was subsumed into the new venture, its ruins swallowed up -- yet it has a different address in the heart of the city.
Zh: 不。这里之前的那个火电站被纳入了新企业，它的废墟已经被吞没——不过它在市中心还有一个不同的地址。

En: The bookstore closes starting 21.00. You'll have to do that tomorrow.
Zh: 书店晚上9点关门。你只能明天再去。

En: How did you become a dicemaker?
Zh: “你是怎么成为一个骰子匠人的？”

En: How did I *become* one? It was a business decision. I was a regular jeweller at first, but that's an unfocused field -- with too much competition.
Zh: “怎么*成为*？这是一个商业决策。一开始我是个普通的珠宝商，但是那个领域太分散——竞争太大。”

En: Some of my friends were role-players. They asked me to make some polyhedral dice out of cobalt. That was my first order. I grew it from there.
Zh: “我有些朋友是角色扮演游戏玩家。他们让我帮着用钴做些多面体骰子。那是我的第一单生意，我就是从那里开始发展的。”

En: Do you like role-playing games yourself?
Zh: “你自己喜欢角色扮演游戏吗？”

En: Understood.
Zh: “明白了。”

En: Not especially. I like working with rare materials and a steady pay. And role-players as customers -- they're nice people.
Zh: “不是特别喜欢。我喜欢跟稀有材料一起工作，还喜欢稳定的收入。而且顾客是角色扮演游戏玩家的话——他们人真的挺好的。”

En: She's thankful for the security they provide her.
Zh: 她感激他们给她带来的安全感。

En: Some of those nice people have big bucks to spend on novelty items.
Zh: 有些好人会把大把的钱花在新奇的玩意上。

En: I've grown crafty over the years. You'll like your dice, if you get them from me. Be sure of it.
Zh: “这些年来，我的手艺越来越灵巧。如果是从我手里做出来的骰子，你一定会喜欢的。这一点我很肯定。”

En: I've grown crafty over the years. You'll like your dice, I'm sure of it.
Zh: “这些年来，我的手艺越来越灵巧。你会喜欢你的骰子的，我很肯定。”

En: She doesn't let it show, but there's anger in there -- she doesn't like jewellers. Thinks they're a mob.
Zh: 她没有显露出自己的愤怒——她不喜欢珠宝商。觉得他们是一群暴徒。

En: It feels icy. Just holding this die in your hand sends a jolt of cold down your spine. Through the dark resin, you can make out a nugget of bone hewn from an alligator's jaw.
Zh: 感觉很冰。只是把骰子握在手中，就让你的脊椎感觉到一阵寒意。透过黑色的树脂，你能辨认出一块骨头——来自短吻鳄的下颌骨。

En: Look at it.
Zh: 看着它。

En: Look at them.
Zh: 看着它们。

En: Dazzling! It's like you have a pair of tiny disco balls in your hand.
Zh: 光彩炫目！在你手中就像一对小型的迪斯科闪光灯球。

En: Look at it.
Zh: 看着它。

En: It's a handsome red die with a mix of plant symbols diligently engraved on its faces.
Zh: 这是一个俊俏的红色骰子，表面雕刻着混合的植物图案。

En: Look at it.
Zh: 看着它。

En: There appears to be a small fly embedded within. It's like looking into a lost little world...
Zh: 里面好像嵌入了一只小苍蝇。就像是在看一个小小的失落世界……

En: Creative. The lieutenant looks around the spacious room, its ceiling fading into shadows above.
Zh: “很有创造性。”警督四下看了看这个宽阔的房间，天花板已经融入上方的阴影了。

En: Turns out the business was actually set up as a front for an illicit group that was producing *snuff milieus* -- who would have guessed...
Zh: “结果那家企业其实是为了掩护一个非法集团建立的，就是*鼻烟电台*的出品方——谁能想到呢……”

En: And they never cleaned up the debris either. Now it's just littering the hallway and I have no idea how to get rid of it on my own.
Zh: “而且他们从来都不清扫杂物。现在东西都乱丢在走廊里，我自己一个人也不知道该怎么弄走。”

En: Yeah, don't. She looks down, then up again.
Zh: “是啊，那就不要。”她低下头，然后又抬了起来。

En: Nothing changes in her tone as she says that -- as if it's just another piece of information to lay out for the world.
Zh: 当她说出这句话的时候，口气丝毫没有变化——就好像只是这个世界上又一条普通的信息罢了。

En: What does she mean -- to *get off* on it?
Zh: 她是什么意思——*享受*？

En: Good luck with that. It's not easy catching those perpetrators. Then she lets the thought go.
Zh: “那要祝你好运了。要抓住那些罪犯可不容易。”然后她放弃了这个想法。

En: People do that, she nods. "I believe the ICP got them. They have a special division that deals exclusively with sub rosas."
Zh: “大家是会这么做，”她点点头。“我相信ICP抓到他们了。ICP有个特殊部门，专门处理秘密电台的问题。”

En: In any case, I don't think it's your job to worry about what happens on milieus. I probably shouldn't have even mentioned it -- things like that are better forgotten.
Zh: “不管怎么说，我觉得担心电台里的东西不是你的工作。我可能根本就不应该提它——这种事还是忘掉的好。”

En: Men like that are a curse. The lieutenant is stern.
Zh: “那种人本身就是个诅咒。”警督很坚定。

En: Sure. But Slipstream is history now. All their remaining assets got seized by the bailiffs in '47.
Zh: “当然。不过现在气流已经成为历史。所有剩下来的资产都在47年被法警查封了。”

En: I have no idea why those skis and blades are still lying around in the house... Not much use now, I guess.
Zh: “我不知道为什么房间里还留着那些滑雪板和动叶片……我猜现在应该没多大用了吧。”

En: The usual, I imagine -- that he's been thinking up all kinds of new business plans and can't *wait* to get *started* on them just as soon as he returns... She laughs and turns around in her chair.
Zh: “我想又是老一套吧——他一直在构思各种各样的新商业计划，一回来就*等不及*要马上*开始*……”她笑着，在椅子上转过身来。

En: (Look at your feet.) "Unhonourable..."
Zh: （看着你的脚。）“不光荣……”

En: Maybe. She doesn't really seem to care that much about honour. "But Slipsteam's history now. All their remaining assets got seized by the bailiffs in '47."
Zh: “也许吧。”她好像真的不太在乎荣誉。“不过现在气流已经成为历史。所有剩下来的资产都在47年被法警查封了。”

En: She sounds almost mocking when she says that.
Zh: 她说出这句话的时候，几乎有点嘲弄的意味。

En: That's what I thought. Because when the money started to run out they just began to complain a lot about capitalism. You know, how the markets are rigged to keep out new businesses, and so on.
Zh: “我就是这么想的。因为当资金所剩无几的时候，他们只是开始抱怨资本主义。你懂得，就是市场被操纵了，要排挤新企业之类的，等等等等。”

En: She's right, showing up to work on time is important.
Zh: 她是对的，按时上班很重要。

En: Showing up to work on time is *hard* -- no, scratch that, showing up to work *at all* is difficult (especially if you've been drinking).
Zh: 按时上班很*困难*——不，忘掉它吧，*上班*才叫难（特别是喝醉的时候）。

En: The place was owned by two guys who had some rather *innovative* ideas about marketing. The bear was one of them. Now, ask me about their *other* ideas.
Zh: “那里的所有者是两个小伙子，他们对市场有一些非常*创新*的看法。那只熊就是其中一个。现在，问问我他们的*其他*想法吧。”

En: Sounds like she really didn't like those girls.
Zh: 听起来她真的不太喜欢那些女孩。

En: Sounds like she really doesn't know how to be a female ally!
Zh: 听起来她真不知道怎么成为女性之友！

En: Frittte does the same thing.
Zh: 弗利多也在做同样的事情。

En: You should learn how to *support* other women, not bring them down.
Zh: “你应该学会怎么*支持*其他女性，而不是让她们失望。”

En: I'm not talking just about *women*. There were also *men* -- miniature gorilla-like *boyfriends* loitering near the ice cream stand.
Zh: “我说的不仅仅是*女性*。还有*男性*——那些像缩小版大猩猩的*男朋友*，也会在冰淇淋摊附近瞎晃。”

En: And they already had *the bear*... She closes her eyes as if remembering something painful.
Zh: “而且他们已经有*那只熊*了……”她闭上双眼，好像想起了一些痛苦的回忆。

En: What about the bear?
Zh: “熊怎么了？”

En: It didn't work out?
Zh: “没成功吗？”

En: *The bear*... she repeats, pressing thumbs into her temples, like trying to suppress a headache.
Zh: “*那只熊*……”她一边重复着，一边用拇指按压着太阳穴，像是要止住头痛。

En: As if they didn't already have *the bear*... She closes her eyes as if remembering something painful.
Zh: “就好像他们有*那只熊*还不够一样……”她闭上双眼，好像想起了一些痛苦的回忆。

En: (Nod solemnly.) "It's the market doing its job."
Zh: （郑重地点点头。）“是市场在发挥作用。”

En: Maybe. Because the taxidermist who made the bear definitely wasn't. Doing his job, I mean.
Zh: “也许吧。因为做出熊的那个动物标本剥制师肯定没有。我是说，他尽力了。”

En: Anyway, now you know the story of the fallen ice cream empire. She seems almost sad, finishing the story.
Zh: “总之，现在你知道了冰淇淋帝国覆灭的故事。”说完故事，她看起来几乎有些悲伤。

En: Anyway, now you know the story of the fallen ice cream empire. She seems almost sad, finishing the story. Some dust beams swirl in the morning light. Her eyes follow it idly.
Zh: “总之，现在你知道了冰淇淋帝国覆灭的故事。”说完故事，她看起来几乎有些悲伤。午后的阳光中，几缕灰尘形成的光柱在盘旋着。她的眼睛漫不经心地追随其后。

En: No, it was something else... It was *eerie*.
Zh: 不，是别的东西……*可怕*的东西。

En: It was too real to be just a prank. Either we're dealing with a professional actress, or...
Zh: 它太真实了，感觉不像是恶作剧。要么我们遇到了一个专业女演员，或者……

En: Whatever happened, keep your cool. It's probably better to admit that it was a harmless prank.
Zh: 不管发生什么，你都要保持镇静。也许承认这只是个无伤大雅的恶作剧更好一些。

En: No, it was something else -- something *eerie*... (Narrow your eyes.)
Zh: “不，是别的东西——某种*可怕*的东西……”（眯起眼睛。）

En: Pranks can be eerie. She looks as if she's still convinced it's nothing to be worried about.
Zh: “恶作剧也会很可怕的。”看起来她还是确定根本没什么好担心的。

En: The zipper... it's stuck!
Zh: “拉链……卡住了！”

En: Oh no, not the *zipper*. She rolls over to the other end of her table with her chair, looking for something to grab from the tool box.
Zh: “不，不是*拉链*。”她乘着椅子滑动到桌子的另一头，在工具箱里寻找着什么。

En: Miss, I apologize for my colleague's behaviour. He's still recovering from an *unusual* medical episode...
Zh: “小姐，我为我同事的行为向你道歉。他经历了一场*不寻常*的医疗小插曲，现在还在恢复中……”

En: That doesn't really excuse it, does it.
Zh: 这并不能成为借口，不是吗。

En: Why is *everyone* doing drugs in this place -- even the *taxidermist*?!
Zh: 为什么这里的*每个人*都在吸毒呢——就连*剥制师*也是？！

En: She sighs. "I guess the least I can do is check up on Soona, see how she's doing."
Zh: 她叹了口气。“我猜至少我可以去看望一下苏娜，看看她怎么样了。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: My money's on *electrical anomaly*. I've heard of these. Especially in an old building like this... He looks around.
Zh: “我会押注在*电异常事故*上。这些我之前听过。特别是这么古老的一栋建筑……”他看了看周围。

En: The Wirrâl Untethered die is a variation of a standard role-playing die, only instead of plants it uses motifs of ice and death. And loss, of course.
Zh: “自由威勒尔骰子是标准角色扮演骰子的变种，唯一的差别用冰雪和死亡的图案代替了植物。当然了，还有损失。”

En: That's why people like role-playing games. You can be whoever you want to be. You can try again. Still, there's something inherently violent even about dice rolls.
Zh: “这就是大家喜欢角色扮演游戏的原因。你可以随心所欲地成为任何人。你可以一遍遍地尝试。不过即使是掷骰子，也有某种固有的暴力在里面。”

En: It's like every time you cast a die, something disappears. Some alternative ending, or an entirely different world... She picks up a pair of dice from the table and examines them under the light.
Zh: “就好像你每次锻造骰子的时候，有些东西就会消失。另一种替代的结局，或者是一个完全不同的世界……”她从桌子上抓起一对骰子，拿到灯光下查看。

En: What happened to the gym?
Zh: “健身房怎么了？”

En: I found a strange machine...
Zh: “我发现一台奇怪的机器……”

En: There was a terrifying taxidermied bear in the cellar.
Zh: “地下室有一个超级吓人的熊标本。”

En: There used to be a hair salon here, right?
Zh: “这里以前有一家美发店，对吗？”

En: Actually, I had other questions...
Zh: “其实，我还有别的问题……”

En: Good. I hope it clarified things a bit. What else?
Zh: “很好。希望这样能把事情稍微说的清楚一点。还有别的吗？”

En: What's up with the broken windows?
Zh: “破窗户是怎么回事？”

En: Did someone here make stuffed animals? I saw mounts lying around.
Zh: “有人在这里做毛绒玩具吗？我在周围看到了很多。”

En: I found creepy mannequins...
Zh: “我发现一些很诡异的人体模特……”

En: What's with the rotor blades and skis?
Zh: “动叶片和滑雪板是怎么回事？”

En: Hello, I'm Neha. A bird-like woman sits on a throne of tools, with emerald light shining through her hair.
Zh: “你好，我是妮亚。”一个像鸟儿一样的女人坐在一个工具王座上，发丝间透射出翠绿色的光线。

En: Did you try knocking on my window? I must have missed you, I've been listening to my *milieus*. She taps on her headphones.
Zh: “你是不是敲过我的窗户？我正在听*电台*，肯定是错过了。”她轻轻敲了敲自己的耳机。

En: Why are you asking me about *dice*? (Move on.)
Zh: “你为什么要问我*骰子*的事？”（继续。）

En: Never mind, I'll be back later. [Leave.]
Zh: “没关系，我稍后再回来。”[离开。]

En: I'm a novelty dicemaker, she says. "Tell me the name of your role-playing system and I'll make the die you need. That's why you're here, yes?"
Zh: “我是个新奇骰子匠人，”她说到。“把你在角色扮演系统的名字告诉我，我会帮你做出你需要的骰子。你就是为这个来的，对吧？”

En: It almost looks as if the stones and dice are a natural part of the room, growing out of the shelves like stalagmites.
Zh: 这些石头和骰子仿佛天生就是这个房间的一部分，像石笋一样从架子上长了出来。

En: A 'normal' die? I'm a novelty dicemaker, I don't make 'normal' dice.
Zh: “一个‘普通的’的骰子？我是新奇骰子匠人，我不做‘普通的’骰子。”

En: What do you do then?
Zh: “那你是做什么的？”

En: Polyhedral dice. Dice that have more than four sides. Octahedrons, trapezohedrons, dodecahedrons... But also barrel dice and teetotum balls.
Zh: “多面骰子，超过4面的骰子。八面体，偏六面体，十二面体……不过也做桶形骰子和四方陀螺球体。”

En: I'm looking for something to help with my work.
Zh: “那*你*觉得什么样的骰子符合我的性格呢？”

En: Do you know the *Wirrâl Untethered* setting? I want a die for that.
Zh: “你知道*自由威勒尔*的背景吗？我想要玩那个的骰子。”

En: I want a die for the Wirrâl setting, sweet and basic.
Zh: “我想要适合威勒尔背景的骰子，来个基本款的小可爱就行。”

En: Ah, yes, Fortress Accident. She shakes her head lightly. "It's too bad they never finished their game..."
Zh: “啊，是的，要塞事故。”她轻轻摇摇头。“很可惜，他们从来没能把游戏做完……”

En: That's a classic, yes. A standard role-playing die with plant motifs. Now for the material: Most of my customers opt for an exotic wood -- for this I would suggest East-Semenese Snakewood.
Zh: “那个确实很经典。一枚标准的角色扮演游戏骰子，上面有植物图案。现在说说材料：我的大部分客户会选择外国木材——我会建议选用东塞美尼的蛇纹树。”

En: Actually, I need more time to think about it.
Zh: “其实吧，我需要时间再多考虑一下。”

En: No need to rush. Take a look around, see if anything inspires you. She nods towards the shelves full of precious stones.
Zh: “不用着急。四处看看，有没有什么吸引你的。”她朝着堆满宝石的架子点了点头。

En: I'm thinking something made from alligator jawbone, cast in black resin. The reptile bone is as white as ice and dead as... well... death, she smiles. "For seven reál I could have it ready in eight hours."
Zh: “我在想用短吻鳄的下颚骨做个东西，用黑色树脂浇铸。爬行动物的骨头像雪一样白，而且像……呃……死亡一样了无生气，”她笑了。“只要7雷亚尔，我就能在8小时内交货。”

En: I think I have just the right one for you. She opens the top drawer of her work desk and takes something out -- two polyhedrons, red and blue, are cradled in her palm.
Zh: “我想我这里正好有个适合你的。”她打开工作台顶端的抽屉，把什么东西拿了出来——那是两个多面体，一红一蓝，静静躺在她的掌心。

En: Police colours.
Zh: 警察的颜色。

En: Because I'm a police officer, right?
Zh: “因为我是个警官，对吗？”

En: Uh, I don't get it...
Zh: “呃，我没明白……”

En: You're a police officer, right? Here, catch! She tosses you the dice. "They're a gift from me."
Zh: “你是个警官，对吧？给你，接好了！”她把骰子扔给你。“这是我送你的礼物。”

En: Catch the dice!
Zh: 抓住骰子！

En: Totally overthinking it.
Zh: 完全想多了

En: Dresscode: Extreme performance.
Zh: 着装要求：极致表演。

En: Let them fall on the ground.
Zh: 让它们掉在地板上。

En: Mr. Smooth Moves! You snatch the dice out of the air with one hand, just like you're in a movie.
Zh: 不亏是好身手先生！你用一只手在空中抓住了骰子，就像演电影一样。

En: Two tiny pieces of carved stone drop to the floor and scatter in opposite directions, like pearls from a broken string. The blue one disappears down the pit in the centre of the room...
Zh: 两个小石雕掉落在地板上，散落在两个不同的方向，就像从一条断裂细绳上滚落的珍珠。蓝色那颗消失在房间中央的深坑中……

En: A beautiful woman tossing you a gift? Whatever you do, don't overthink it.
Zh: 一个美丽的女人朝你扔礼物？不管做什么，最好不要想太多。

En: I don't play fetch.
Zh: “我不玩接东西的游戏。”

En: Pick up the red die.
Zh: 捡起红色的骰子。

En: Right. She tries to smile.
Zh: “好吧。”她努力挤出笑容。

En: That one is made of bloodstone with a lapis lazuli inlay. The other one was the inverse. They were a set, you see. But now the set is broken.
Zh: “那颗是血石做的，镶嵌着一颗天青石。另一颗正好相反。你也能看见，它们是一对。不过现在被拆散了。”

En: It's a shame. They might've have brought you luck, and you'll definitely need luck in Martinaise. She closes her desk drawer.
Zh: “真可惜。它们也许能为你带来好运，而且在马丁内斯地方，你肯定是需要一点运气的。”她合上抽屉。

En: Wink at the dicemaker.
Zh: 朝骰子匠人眨眨眼。

En: Say nothing. Act like you've done this a thousand times.
Zh: 什么也不说。表现地好像你已经这样做过1000次。

En: Here, catch! She tosses you the dice. "They're a gift from me."
Zh: “给你，接好了！”她把骰子扔给你。“这是我送你的礼物。”

En: Ah, down the drain, like your career, jokes the dicemaker, her eyes tracking the remaining polyhedron. Then she catches your eye. "I apologize, officer. That comment was unnecessary."
Zh: “啊，付诸东流，就像你的职业生涯一样，”骰子匠人开了个玩笑，眼睛追踪着剩下来的多面体。然后她捕捉到了你的目光。“我很抱歉，警官。那句评论完全没必要。”

En: You'll definitely need luck in Martinaise. She closes her desk drawer. "Was there anything else?"
Zh: “在马丁内斯这种地方，你肯定是需要一点运气的。”她合上抽屉。“还有其他事吗？”

En: What are the odds? The dicemaker gives you a warm smile. "The red one is made of bloodstone with a lapis lazuli inlay and the blue one is the inverse. Think of them as your lucky charm, officer..."
Zh: “这种可能性有多少？”骰子匠人朝你露出一个温暖的笑容。“红色那颗是用血石做的，镶嵌着一颗天青石。蓝色那颗正好相反。把它们当成是你的幸运符吧，警官……”

En: She smiled at you. She smiled! You truly are Mr. Smooth Moves!
Zh: 她冲着你微笑。她笑了！你是货真价实的好身手先生！

En: Leave the die on the floor.
Zh: 不管地上的骰子。

En: Her knuckles pop audibly from her nervous habit of playing with her hands. The soft chatter from the headphones continues unabated.
Zh: 她的指关节发出一阵清脆的响声，显然是来自紧张时掰手指的习惯。耳机那轻柔的颤动声还在继续，丝毫没有减弱。

En: Is she pitying you? Good god, she's pitying you.
Zh: 她是在可怜你吗？我的天，她居然在可怜你。

En: I'm not a dog.
Zh: “我不是一条狗。”

En: I see that.
Zh: “我知道。”

En: And I'm definitely not anyone's bitch.
Zh: “而且我也绝对不是任何人的走狗。”

En: The dicemaker coughs, her hands playing with her ring again. She doesn't say a word.
Zh: 骰子匠人咳嗽了几声，双手又开始摆弄自己的戒指。她一句话也没说。

En: That's right. You'll make your own luck.
Zh: 没错。你会自己创造好运。

En: Not the most original, I know, she smiles, almost apologizing, "but sometimes the obvious choice is obvious because it's best."
Zh: “我知道，不算是最新颖的，”骰子匠人笑了，几乎带着些歉意，“但是有时候，显而易见的选择之所以显而易见，只是因为它是最好的。”

En: You overthink it. Your hands can't agree what to do and the two dice drop to the floor and scatter in opposite directions, like pearls from a broken string. The blue one disappears down the pit in the centre of the room...
Zh: 你想得太多。双手无法决定该怎么做，两只骰子散落在两个不同的方向，就像从一条断裂细绳上滚落的珍珠。蓝色的那颗消失在房间中央的深坑中……

En: You must have me confused with someone else -- I haven't knocked on your window.
Zh: “你一定是把我跟其他人弄混了——我没有敲你的窗户。”

En: Then how did you get inside? By the south entrance? She blinks. "You know what, it doesn't even matter. What matters is that you're finally here. Let's talk dice -- did you have something specific in mind?"
Zh: “那你是怎么进来的？从南边的入口吗？”她眨眨眼。“你知道吗，其实这根本不重要。重要的是你最终来到这里了。我们聊聊骰子吧——你有什么具体的想法吗？”

En: Can I order another die?
Zh: “我还能再订一个骰子吗？”

En: Okay. I'd like to order a die from you.
Zh: “好吧。我要跟你订个骰子。”

En: I'm not interested in buying dice right now. I'm a police officer and I need to ask some questions.
Zh: “我现在没兴趣买骰子。我是一名警官，需要问你几个问题。”

En: Of course, she nods -- this is what she's here for. "Tell me what you have in mind."
Zh: “当然可以，”她点点头——这就是她待在这里的原因。“告诉我，你有什么想法吗？”

En: Just a normal die for me, please. Very *regular*.
Zh: “麻烦给我来个普通的骰子，很*常规*的就行。”

En: Of course, I can see that. I just thought you were a police officer looking for dice... She tilts her bird-like head. "How else can I help you then?"
Zh: “当然了，我能看出来。我还以为你是个在找骰子的警官……”她歪起像小鸟一样的脑袋。“还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Hold on, what do you mean by *milieus*?
Zh: “等等，你说*电台*是什么意思？”

En: What do you know about the man who was lynched behind the Whirling-in-Rags?
Zh: “你知不知道褴褛飞旋背后那个被私刑处死的男人？”

En: Why hasn't her business failed...
Zh: 她的生意为什么没有失败呢……

En: Cast the Cursed Iilmaraan Die.
Zh: 投掷诅咒的伊尔玛骰子。

En: The Remote Viewer.
Zh: 遥视者。

En: Found the 2 mm hole in the world.
Zh: 在世界上发现一个2mm的洞。

En: Anything else, officer?
Zh: “还有什么事吗，警官？”

En: I'm sorry, I don't actually know what die I want...
Zh: “很抱歉，我真的不知道自己想要什么样的骰子……”

En: Do you have any *cursed dice*?
Zh: “你这里有*诅咒骰子*吗？”

En: A star that fell from the firmament? Those cost more than seven reál. Are you sure?
Zh: “从天上坠落的星星？那可不止7雷亚尔了。你确定吗？”

En: Yes, I'm a star myself, a *superstar* -- and superstars don't care about money.
Zh: “是的，我自己就是明星，一个*超级明星*——而且超级明星是不在乎什么钱不钱的。”

En: No, I'm afraid it's not worth it, sorry.
Zh: “不，恐怕不太值得，对不起。”

En: Yes, you definitely have the proper attitude. How about a pair of 100-sided dice? Made of ivory and inlaid with lodestone, a naturally occurring magnet, to complement your magnetic personality.
Zh: “是啊，你的态度确实很端正。那来一对100面的骰子怎么样？用象牙制成，镶嵌天然磁石——那是一种天然形成的磁铁，正好可以补足你这么有吸引力的性格。”

En: What do you mean by *cursed*?
Zh: “你说*诅咒*是什么意思？”

En: Abraq-ad-abra, perished like this world!
Zh: “三位一体，就像这个世界一样毁灭了！”

En: As cursed as this commercial area.
Zh: “类似这个商业区受到的诅咒。”

En: Alright, how about I surprise you? Come back in eight hours with seven reál and I'll give you your *cursed die*.
Zh: “好吧，那我给你个惊喜怎么样？8小时后，带上7雷亚尔回到这里来，我会把*诅咒骰子*交给你。”

En: As cursed as my life.
Zh: “就像我的生活受到的诅咒一样。”

En: Alright. Maybe some other die then?
Zh: “好吧。那不如换个其他骰子吧？”

En: It's a deal. (Order the die.)
Zh: “成交。”（预订骰子。）

En: Actually, I need to think about it first. (Back off.)
Zh: “其实，我需要先考虑一下。”（返回。）

En: Great! See you in eight hours then. She takes a small notebook from her table and writes something down. "Was there anything else?"
Zh: “太好了！那就8小时之后见吧。”她从桌子里拿出一个小笔记本，然后在上面写了点什么。“还有其他事吗？”

En: Maybe you have some other ideas for dice?
Zh: “也许你对骰子还有些其他想法？”

En: Tell me about your most extraordinary die!
Zh: “跟我说说你这里最特别的骰子吧！”

En: Ten reál and I can get these ready in eight hours.
Zh: “10雷亚尔，我会在8小时内准备好。”

En: It's a deal. (Order the dice set.)
Zh: “成交。”（预订一对骰子。）

En: I need to think about it first. (Back off.)
Zh: “我需要先考虑一下。”（退后。）

En: Great! See you in eight hours then. She takes a small notebook from her table and writes something down, her expression pleased. "Was there anything else?"
Zh: “太好了！那就8小时之后见吧。”她从桌子里拿出一个小笔记本，然后在上面写了点什么。“还有其他事吗？”

En: But do magnetic dice even roll properly?
Zh: “但是磁性骰子真的能正常滚动吗？”

En: It's true, magnetic dice are definitely different. When rolled together, the magnetic fields of the pips interact with each other to push and pull on each other as they are rolled. Just like all magnetic personalities do.
Zh: “的确，磁性骰子确实不太一样。一起滚动的时候，它们的磁场会彼此作用，在滚动中互相推拉。就像所有那些性格很有吸引力的人一样。”

En: Pieces of lodestone were used as the first magnetic compasses, from which they derive their other name: *course stones*.
Zh: 天然磁石一开始是用作磁罗盘的，它们也因此得到了另外一个名字：“导航石”。

En: That's a stone for leaders.
Zh: 这是为领导者而生的石头。

En: How about an amber die? she suggests.
Zh: “那琥珀骰子怎么样？”她建议到。

En: Amber, a poor man's gold?
Zh: “琥珀，穷人的金子吗？”

En: No, amber sounds too boring.
Zh: “不，琥珀听起来太无聊了。”

En: The dicemaker laughs. "Who told you that amber was cheap? It's beautiful, really, and has been treasured since the ancient times."
Zh: 骰子匠人笑了。“谁告诉你琥珀便宜的？它真的很美，很久以前就被视为珍宝了。”

En: For seven reál I'll craft you a 13-sided die from a piece of amber with a fossilised insect. It's perfect for those who can't seem to let go of their past.
Zh: “只要7雷亚尔，我就能为你铸造一颗13面的骰子，琥珀材质，里面还有昆虫化石。对于那些不想放下过去的人来说，它是最完美的。”

En: Great! See you in eight hours then. She takes a small notebook from her table and writes something down. "Was there anything else?"
Zh: “太好了！那就8小时之后见吧。”她从桌子里拿出一个小笔记本，然后在上面写了点什么。“还有其他事吗？”

En: It's a deal. (Order the die.)
Zh: “成交。”（预订骰子。）

En: I need to think about it. (Back off.)
Zh: “我需要先考虑一下。”（退后。）

En: It's a deal. (Order the die.)
Zh: “成交。”（预订骰子。）

En: Great! See you in eight hours then. She takes a small notebook from her table and writes something down. "Was there anything else?"
Zh: “太好了！那就8小时之后见吧。”她从桌子里拿出一个小笔记本，然后在上面写了点什么。“还有其他事吗？”

En: I need to think about it. (Back off.)
Zh: “我需要先考虑一下。”（退后。）

En: For seven reál I could have it ready in eight hours.
Zh: “只要7雷亚尔，我就能8小时内交货。”

En: What's a standard role-playing die?
Zh: “角色扮演的标准骰子是什么样的？”

En: It's an *icositetrahedron* -- a 24-sided die that can produce results for a 2-sided, 3-sided, 4-sided, 6-sided, and 12-sided die with a single roll. Technically you can also use it for many other sizes, but you may need to re-roll results.
Zh: “它是*24面体*，一颗24面的骰子，只要丢一次，就能产出2面，3面，4面，6面还有12面骰子的结果。从技术上讲，其他尺寸也是可以用的，只不过你可能需要重掷结果。”

En: It's a deal. (Order the die.)
Zh: “成交。”（预订骰子。）

En: Great! See you in eight hours then. She takes a small notebook from her table and writes something down. "Was there anything else?"
Zh: “太好了！那就8小时之后见吧。”她从桌子里拿出一个小笔记本，然后在上面写了点什么。“还有其他事吗？”

En: Hm... I should maybe get a different one. (Back off.)
Zh: “嗯……也许我该换个别的。”（返回。）

En: What's a *standard* role-playing die?
Zh: “角色扮演的*标准*骰子是什么样的？”

En: It's an *icositetrahedron* -- a 24-sided die that can produce results for a 2-sided, 3-sided, 4-sided, 6-sided, and 12-sided die with a single roll. Technically you can also use it for many other sizes, but you may need to re-roll results.
Zh: “它是*24面体*，一颗24面的骰子，只要丢一次，就能产出2面，3面，4面，6面还有12面骰子的结果。从技术上讲，其他尺寸也是可以用的，只不过你可能需要重掷结果。”

En: Why do you need to cast it in resin?
Zh: “为什么要用树脂浇铸呢？”

En: Untreated bone is porous and prone to chipping. Cast it in something hard like resin, though, and *voila*, it's perfect.
Zh: “未经处理的骨头有很多气孔，用凿的会更容易一些。不过，用树脂类的硬物浇铸的话，*瞧*，它是最完美的。”

En: I'm sorry. I'm a bit overloaded just now so I can only produce one die per customer.
Zh: “很抱歉。我现在有些超负荷了，我只能给每位顾客制造一个骰子。”

En: Oh, it's you again. Did you try knocking on my window before? Are you looking for a die?
Zh: “哦，又是你。你之前是不是敲过我的窗户？你是在找骰子吗？”

En: You're back, the bird-like woman says, looking up from her table. "Did you decide what kind of die you want?"
Zh: “你回来了，”像小鸟一样的女人从桌子上抬起头说到。“你决定好要什么样的骰子了吗？”

En: As you wish. Till next time, she nods, turning back to her table.
Zh: “如你所愿，下次再见，”她点点头，又转回桌子旁边。

En: How can I help you, officer?
Zh: “有什么能帮你的吗，警官？”

En: I came back to pick up my die.
Zh: “我回来取骰子的。”

En: (Give her the money.) "Here you go."
Zh: （把钱给她。）“给你。”

En: Actually, I don't have the money on me right now.
Zh: “其实，我现在身上没有钱。”

En: I don't do orders on credit. Come back when you have the full amount.
Zh: “我这里不赊账的。等你弄到全款之后再回来吧。”

En: (Give her the money.) "Here you go."
Zh: （把钱给她。）“给你。”

En: This was an especially fun set to make, she says as she opens her drawer and hands you a sparkling pair of dice.
Zh: “做这套骰子真的挺有趣的，”她一边说，一边打开抽屉，把一对闪闪发光的骰子递给你。

En: One universal die for *Wirrâl Untethered*, the dicemaker opens her desk drawer and hands you a die.
Zh: “*自由威勒尔*的通用骰子，”骰子匠人打开抽屉，递给你一只骰子。

En: Here's the *cursed* die you ordered, the dicemaker opens her desk drawer and hands you a tiny black sphere with six phrases written on it.
Zh: “这是你预订的*诅咒*骰子，”骰子匠人打开抽屉，递给你一个小黑球，上面写着6句话。

En: One standard Wirrâl role-playing die, the dicemaker opens her desk drawer and hands you a die.
Zh: “一个标准的威勒尔角色扮演骰子，”骰子匠人打开抽屉，递给你一只骰子。

En: Here's your amber die, the dicemaker opens her desk drawer and hands you a die the colour of dark syrup.
Zh: “这是你的琥珀骰子，”骰子匠人打开抽屉，递给你一只深糖浆色的骰子。

En: Mostly they just teach you to swear in different languages, but some of the stations can be quite interesting. I was so absorbed, I must have missed you knocking.
Zh: “大多数时候，他们只是教你用不同的语言在咒骂，不过有些电台就比较有趣。我太过专注，肯定没听见你的敲击声。”

En: Hey, Neha... The curse is real and I figured out why it has spared you.
Zh: “喂，妮亚……那个诅咒是真的，我也弄明白你为什么没有受到影响。”

En: A gust of cold air sweeps through the chimney. The stones and minerals on the shelves rattle as though agitated. For a moment it almost feels as though you're *outside* the building, exposed to the atmosphere...
Zh: 一阵冷风从烟囱里吹了进来。架子上的石头和矿物激动地咯咯直响。有那么一会儿，你几乎感觉自己正站在建筑*外面*，暴露在大气中……

En: You feel nothing. If anything, it's uncomfortably *warm* in here.
Zh: 你什么也没感觉到。如果有的话，那就是里面*温暖*的让人不太自在。

En: What is this?
Zh: “这是什么？”

En: This is not a die.
Zh: “这不是骰子。”

En: It's a die, confirms the dicemaker with a subtle smile on her face. "Try rolling it."
Zh: “这是骰子，”骰子匠人确认到，脸上带着一丝难以捉摸的微笑。“你试着丢一下。”

En: No, I'm not deranged. I don't want to roll a *cursed die*.
Zh: “不，我还没疯呢。我才不想丢*诅咒骰子*。”

En: Wait, you're not scared, are you? The dicemaker starts laughing. "Officer, you don't actually think I put a spell on it, do you?"
Zh: “等等，你不是在害怕吧？”骰子匠人笑了起来。“警官，你不会真以为我在上面施了咒吧？”

En: You throw the ball on the floor and it ends with one of the phrases facing upwards: 'god is indifferent'.
Zh: 你把小球丢到地板上，最后朝上的那一面上写着一句话：‘神明是冷漠的。’

En: I'm not a witch, you know. We live in a rational world. There is no *black magic*.
Zh: “你知道的，我不是什么女巫。我们生活在一个理性的世界。根本没有什么*黑魔法*。”

En: Better safe than sorry.
Zh: “小心驶得万年船。”

En: I've already learned about horrors like *pale* and I can't take any further risks.
Zh: “我已经了解过*灰域*的恐怖，不能再继续冒险。”

En: I'm not *scared*, I just don't *feel* like rolling it.
Zh: “我没有*害怕*，我只是*不想*丢它。”

En: There are *definitely* dark-magiks.
Zh: “这*肯定*是黑魔法。”

En: No, you're a malignant *Entity*. A die-crafting witch.
Zh: “不，你是一个邪恶的*存在*。一个锻造骰子的女巫。”

En: You're right, she simmers down. "The pale is terrible indeed. But I didn't curse the die. I don't believe in curses."
Zh: “你说得对，”她冷静下来。“灰域确实很可怕。但是我没有诅咒这个骰子。我不相信什么诅咒。”

En: So now I'm a witch too. Rhymes well with the whole me-being-a-culprit theme.
Zh: “所以现在我也变成女巫了。与整个*我是罪魁祸首*的主题很押韵呢。”

En: It's a sphere *pretending* to be a six-sided die -- each roll will end with one of the phrases facing up. The die originates in Iilmaraa, where it was used for cleromancy...
Zh: “这是一个*假装*成6面骰子的球体——每丢一次，最后朝上的那面都会印着一句话。这种骰子起源于伊尔玛，在那里是用来做骰子占卜的……”

En: Read the phrases.
Zh: 阅读词句。

En: The phrases read: 'God is indifferent', 'Take all', 'Lose all', '50/50', 'Nothing happens', and 'pale'.
Zh: 词句上写的是：‘神明是冷漠的’，‘全拿走’，‘失去一切’，‘50/50’，‘无事发生’，以及‘灰域’。

En: She looks at the result. "Good. Now roll again, detective."
Zh: 她看着结果。“很好。现在再掷一次吧，警探。”

En: Roll the die again.
Zh: 再掷一次骰子。

En: I don't want to roll again.
Zh: “我不想再掷了。”

En: It lands on exactly the same result -- *god is indifferent* it declares again.
Zh: 它又落在相同的结果上——它再次发出*神明是冷漠的*声明。

En: Why? Neha starts laughing. "You're not scared, officer, are you?"
Zh: “为什么？”妮亚笑了起来。“你不是在害怕吧，警官？”

En: I'm not a witch, mister, and neither is this die cursed, she assures you. "We live in a rational world -- there is no *black magic*."
Zh: “我不是女巫，先生，这个骰子也没有被诅咒，”她向你保证。“我们生活在一个理性的世界——根本就没有什么*黑魔法*。”

En: Right. But it isn't cursed, honestly. I'll tell you what it does.
Zh: “好吧。不过老实说，它真的没有被诅咒。我会告诉你是怎么回事。”

En: Fortress Accident, the radio game studio... She closes her eyes as some remnant of a memory lights up her face.
Zh: “要塞事故，那个无线游戏工作室……”她闭上眼睛，残存的记忆点亮了她的脸庞。

En: Oh boy, the fabled Revachol Ice City -- you're in for a treat here! She smiles and leans closer, hands on her knees, like a stand-up comedian ready to tell a story.
Zh: “天啦，传说中的瑞瓦肖冰城——你一定会喜欢的！”她笑了笑，靠近了一些，双手放在膝盖上，像是一个准备讲故事的单口相声演员。

En: You mean Mr. Fabron, the taxidermist? No, he mostly just did drugs.
Zh: “你是说法布龙先生，那个动物标本剥制师吗？不，他主要是吸毒。”

En: There used to be a fashion atelier here, but I have forgotten the head designer's name... They were doing well for a couple of years, until the insect rights activists came.
Zh: “这里以前有家时装工作室，但是我忘记那个首席设计师的名字了……有几年他们的生意很不错，直到昆虫权益活动家的到来。”

En: They were made by a company called Slipstream. After they 'pivoted' from making rotor blades to skis, their chief executive took off on a 'vacation'... with all their money. She rests her chin on her hand with an impish smile.
Zh: “那是一家名叫气流的公司生产的。就在他们从生产动叶片‘转到’生产滑雪板之后，他们的董事长就‘度假’去了……还带走了所有的钱。”她用一只手托着下巴，脸上露出顽皮的笑容。

En: I was wondering about the Whirling-in-Rags. Is it part of the same building complex?
Zh: “我在想褴褛飞旋。它也是这栋综合建筑的一部分吗？”

En: I saw a name 'East Delta Pinball' on the doorbell.
Zh: “我在门铃上看到一个名字叫‘东德尔塔弹球’。”

En: Right, it used to be a gaming arcade. This is an *ancient* failure -- before my time. I'm not surprised, however.
Zh: “没错，它曾经是一家游戏机室。那是一次*古老*的失败——早在我的时代之前。不过，我也不觉得惊讶。”

En: What do you mean *liberal*? What happened?
Zh: “你说*自由主义*是什么意思？发生什么事了？”

En: I heard that they ran out of money.
Zh: “我听说他们没钱了。”

En: Cool, very cool about the debris -- but what's a *snuff milieu*?
Zh: “妙啊，那些杂物真的挺酷的——但是*鼻烟电台*是什么？”

En: It's a sub rosa radio station that broadcasts real murders, with real victims. Some people pay good money to get off on it.
Zh: “那是一个秘密无线电台，专门广播真实的凶杀案，还有真实的受害者。有些人为了享受，可花了不少钱呢。”

En: Don't worry, the ICP has a separate division that deals exclusively with unlicensed sub rosas, the lieutenant turns to you. "This isn't our problem."
Zh: “别担心，ICP有一个特殊部门，专门针对这些无照经营的秘密电台，”警督转向你。“这不是我们要担心的问题。”

En: But *what* drugs exactly?
Zh: 不过到底是*什么*毒品呢？

En: I need to know what drugs he was doing... for my... police report.
Zh: “我想知道他吸的是什么毒……为了我的……警察报告。”

En: Let it pass.
Zh: 暂且不谈。

En: He got high on some weird taxidermy chemicals... I wouldn't recommend it to anyone. Eventually they caused him to lose control of his bladder. The smell was awful.
Zh: “他嗑上某种奇怪的剥制术化学药品了……我不会推荐给任何人。最后，那些药品让他患上了尿失禁。那股味道真是太糟糕了。”

En: You can almost see it -- a small, sickly old man hunched behind his work desk, his pants stained with old piss, stuffing a sad stiff-legged raccoon dog. The entire scene looks tragic.
Zh: 你几乎可以看见那副景象——一个矮小多病的老男人，蜷缩在自己的工作台后面，裤子上沾满陈年尿渍，手上正填充着一只双腿僵直的可怜狸猫。整个场面看起来悲惨至极。

En: I didn't know insects had any rights -- or activists.
Zh: “我都不知道昆虫还有什么权利——也不知道还有活动家。”

En: Yeah, the atelier didn't know it either. They produced a certain collection that used chitin among the materials. Apparently chitin is made in the Occident, where it's extracted from beetle wings.
Zh: “是啊，那间工作室也不知道。他们发布了一系列作品，使用的材料里面就有甲壳素。很显然，甲壳素是奥西登制造的，是从甲壳虫的翅膀中提取的。”

En: Insect rights activists? What in the name of...
Zh: “昆虫权益活动家？以什么样的名义……”

En: Mhmh -- the atelier produced a certain collection that used chitin among the materials. Apparently chitin is made in the Occident, where it's extracted from beetle wings.
Zh: “嗯——那间工作室发布了一系列作品，使用的材料里面就有甲壳素。很显然，甲壳素是奥西登制造的，是从甲壳虫的翅膀中提取的。”

En: I'm glad that someone took care of the little guys. I like insects. (Nod.)
Zh: “很高兴能有人关照那些小家伙。我喜欢昆虫。”（点点头。）

En: But insects don't have any... brains or... feelings.
Zh: “但是昆虫没有……大脑，或者是……感觉。”

En: Actually, insects *do* have brains, she corrects you. "But yes, I understand what you're saying. I think the protesters took it a little too far."
Zh: “其实呢，昆虫*确实*有大脑，”她纠正了你的发言。“不过，我能理解你的意思。我觉得那些反对者做的有些太过了。”

En: Hm. Really? She looks at the windowsill, where a dead fly is lying on its back, legs curled up in a bowtie. "Anyway..."
Zh: “呃，真的吗？”她看着窗沿，一只死苍蝇正仰卧在上面，腿蜷缩成蝴蝶结的样子。“无所谓了……”

En: And -- naturally -- all the most fashionable tastemakers refused to be seen in chitin from then on. The atelier went bankrupt before they could finish the collection.
Zh: “而且——很自然地——从那时起，大部分时尚弄潮儿开始拒绝穿着有甲壳素的衣服。工作室还没做完那个系列就破产了。”

En: They got what they deserved -- making clothes out of beetle wings is a *terrible* idea!
Zh: “这是他们应得的——用甲壳虫翅膀做衣服实在是个*可怕*的主意！”

En: At least it had some spark to it. Most young designers just combine lace with leather and call it original. She shifts in her simple workwear shirt.
Zh: “至少还是有些才华的。大部分年轻设计师只会组合蕾丝和皮革，然后说成是原创。”她穿着简单的工作衬衫，稍微挪动了一下。

En: I'm sure the bear was doing its best.
Zh: “我相信那只熊已经尽力了。”

En: The bear was scary. Every time I saw that bear I felt *scared*, like it could become alive any moment now.
Zh: “那只熊很吓人。每次我看见它的时候都会*被吓到*，好像它随时可能活过来一样。”

En: The taxidermist who made it said it was his *vision beast*. He said he met his *vision beast* while high on desiccants. He called it 'Megatherion'.
Zh: “做它的那个动物标本剥制师说，这是他的*幻想野兽*。他说自己是在干燥剂嗑嗨了的时候，跟他的*幻想野兽*相遇的。他把它叫做‘超圣兽’。”

En: Maybe. Because the taxidermist who made that bear definitely wasn't. Doing his best, I mean.
Zh: “也许吧。因为做出熊的那个动物标本剥制师肯定没有。我是说，他尽力了。”

En: I killed the bear. (You unplugged it.)
Zh: “我杀了那只熊。”（你把插头拔掉了。）

En: You did *what*? She rolls her swivel chair an inch closer to you, unsure whether she heard you right.
Zh: “你做了*什么*？”她把转椅朝你的方向挪了一英寸，不确定自己是不是听错了。

En: I murdered it. With my own bare hands I laid it to rest. It's my trophy now.
Zh: “我杀了它。赤手空拳把它埋葬了。现在它是我的战利品了。”

En: I unplugged the fridge to help Revachol Ice City cut their losses.
Zh: “我拔掉了冰箱插头，帮瑞瓦肖冰城减少一点损失。”

En: Really? You broke it? What a shame. It was a *terrifying* beast, but I was fond of it.
Zh: “真的吗？你弄坏它了？真可惜。它确实是一只*吓人*的野兽，不过我还挺喜欢的。”

En: But Slipstream is history now. All their remaining assets got seized by the bailiffs in '47. I have no idea why those skis and blades are still lying around in the house...
Zh: “不过现在气流已经成为历史。所有剩下来的资产都在47年被法警查封了。我不知道为什么房间里还留着那些滑雪板和动叶片……”

En: Interesting. What do these transmissions say?
Zh: “有意思。这些传输信号说的是什么？”

En: Megatherion, nods the dicemaker, "a mega wild beast."
Zh: “超圣兽，”骰子匠人点点头。“一种巨大的野兽。”

En: Megatherion?
Zh: “超圣兽？”

En: What's a vision beast?
Zh: “幻想野兽是什么？”

En: It's an imaginary beast that guides you through life...
Zh: “它是一种虚构的野兽，负责为你的人生指引道路……”

En: The horrific necktie tightens around your neck, strangely excited... But it doesn't feel particularly *fun* this time around.
Zh: 那条恐怖的领带在你的脖子上越勒越紧，让你产生了一种奇妙的兴奋感……不过这一次，感觉不是特别*有趣*。

En: Do you ever feel like your vision beast is trying to *blackmail* the fun out of you?
Zh: “你有没有想过，那只幻想野兽其实是想*勒索*你的快乐？”

En: No, officer. I don't have a vision beast.
Zh: “不，警官。我没有幻想野兽。”

En: What's a mega wild beast?
Zh: “巨大的野兽是什么？”

En: ...by telling you to do more drugs, mostly.
Zh: “……主要是让你多吸毒。”

En: Hey, do you think that vision beast could guide me toward some *amphetamines*?
Zh: “喂，你觉得那个幻想野兽能指引我找到*安非他命*吗？”

En: I don't do drugs.
Zh: “我不吸毒。”

En: I do drugs. I've got a vision beast myself.
Zh: “我吸毒。我也有自己的幻想野兽。”

En: I don't have a comment on drugs.
Zh: “我对毒品不发表任何评论。”

En: Understandable. You shouldn't do them, nods the dicemaker. "You're a police officer, after all..."
Zh: “可以理解。你不应该吸毒，”骰子匠人点点头。“毕竟你是一名警官……”

En: (Grab your necktie and mumble.) "Not... now... you beast!"
Zh: （抓住你的领带嘟囔着。）“不……现在不行……你这头野兽！”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: I don't, officer. You should stay away from drugs, and vision beasts...
Zh: “我不这么认为，警官。你应该远离毒品，还有幻想野兽……”

En: Very good, you shouldn't do them, nods the dicemaker. "You're a police officer, after all..."
Zh: “很好，你不应该吸毒。”骰子匠人点点头。“毕竟你是一名警官……”

En: Do you? The dicemaker raises an eyebrow. "Well, good luck keeping it under control..."
Zh: “是吗？”骰子匠人扬起眉头。“呃，那就祝你好运，希望你能控制住它……”

En: I had to kill the bear to *become* the bear.
Zh: “要想*变成*熊，我必须杀了那只熊。”

En: I can see, she nods, pointing to your war-paint, "the stripes."
Zh: “我能看出来，”她指着你脸上的战争油彩，点了点头，“那些条纹。”

En: Those stripes are what keeps me human.
Zh: “那些条纹是让我保持人性的东西。”

En: The bear put up quite a fight.
Zh: “这只熊跟我进行了一场殊死搏斗。”

En: Nothing! (Cough and let go of the tie.)
Zh: “没什么！”（咳嗽几声，松开领带。）

En: I can see, she nods, eyeing you up and down.
Zh: “我能看出来，”她上下打量着你，点了点头。

En: The usual, I imagine -- that he's been thinking up all kinds of new business plans and can't *wait* to get *started* on them just as soon as he returns... Her smile widens, before she sees the lieutenant's face behind you.
Zh: “我想又是老一套吧——他一直在构思各种各样的新商业计划，一回来就*等不及*要马上*开始*……”她的笑容越来越灿烂，直到看见你身后警督的那张脸。

En: I don't care about their other ideas, I just want to hear about the bear. Did the bear work?
Zh: “我不在乎他们的其他想法，我只想听听熊的事。那熊起作用了吗？”

En: Of course not. The bear was terrifying. No one wants ice-cream *guarded* by a hostile apex predator. To make matters worse, the fridge didn't work too well either, and half the ice cream came out malformed and partially melted.
Zh: “当然没有。那只熊太可怕了。有一只充满敌意的顶级掠食者*看守*，谁还想买冰淇淋啊。更糟糕的是，那台冰箱运转的也不是太好，一半的冰淇淋都变得有些畸形，有一部分都化掉了。”

En: Alright, what about the other ideas?
Zh: “好吧，那其他想法呢？”

En: There was really just one, and it involved picking out the prettiest girls in the neighbourhood and paying them 20 cents per hour to man the booth.
Zh: “其实真的只有一个，里面包括挑选附近最漂亮的女孩，付给她们每小时20分钱的报酬去照看货摊。”

En: And by 'man the booth' I mean 'slump behind the counter with a face that could maim you if you ever dared to disturb their bored magazine-browsing.' She leans back, disapproving.
Zh: “说到‘照看货摊’，我的意思是‘瘫坐在柜台后面，脸上还带着一副*如果你胆敢妨碍她们闲得无聊翻杂志的话，就把你搞死搞残*的表情。’”她向后一靠，反对的意思溢于言表。

En: I don't have an opinion on employing teens, I just want to know -- did it work out for the business?
Zh: “我对雇佣青少年没什么意见，我只想知道——这样对生意有帮助吗？”

En: That's an award-winning idea! How else do you choose people around you?
Zh: “这个想法可以得奖了吧！要不还能怎么选周围的人？”

En: What did they expect? Twenty cents per hour is dog's pay. I'm surprised they showed up to work at all.
Zh: “他们到底想干什么？一小时20分是用来买狗的吗。她们还能来上班我都觉得很惊讶了。”

En: I know a girl just like that. She works in Frittte as a cashier and she's not particularly friendly.
Zh: “我认识一个那种女孩。她是弗利多杂货店的收银员，她感觉就不是特别友好。”

En: Employing sulky teenage girls is a widespread practice, yes. Unfortunately they always come in packs -- I'm talking about acne-ridden *girlfriends* and gorilla-like *boyfriends* loitering near the shop. At least that's what happened with Revachol Ice City.
Zh: “雇佣阴沉的年轻姑娘是挺常见的做法，没错。可惜她们经常是打包来的——我说的是那些长满青春痘的*女朋友*，还有长得像大猩猩一样的*男朋友*，在商店附近闲荡。至少瑞瓦肖冰城遇到这种情况了。”

En: It didn't. Occasionally the girls had their friends over, which doubled the amount of hairspray, skin problems, and mood swings behind the counter.
Zh: “没有。这些女孩的朋友时不时会过来，这就让发胶的使用量多了一倍，还有皮肤问题和柜台后面的情绪波动。”

En: Yes, some people spend their whole lives chasing the 'hot one'. She makes quotation marks with her fingers. "You should have joined the gorilla-like boyfriends loitering near the stand, ready to scare off customers and hit on the girls."
Zh: “是的，有些人终其一生都在追逐‘热辣小妞’。”她用手指比了个引号。“你应该加入那群长得像大猩猩一样的男朋友，在货摊附近闲荡，随时准备吓跑顾客，顺便泡妞。”

En: Oh, but they did -- they did show up to work, and not alone. There were also acne-ridden *girlfriends* and gorilla-like *boyfriends* loitering near the ice cream stand.
Zh: “哦，但她们来了——她们确实来上班了，不过不是一个人。一起来的还有她们长满青春痘的*女朋友*，还有长得像大猩猩一样的*男朋友*，在冰淇淋摊附近闲荡。”

En: Eventually Revachol Ice City lost a price war to its rival, Glacé 5000. Glacé 5000 sold caramel sundaes for only five cents a piece. Out of regular fridges.
Zh: “最后，瑞瓦肖冰城在价格战中输给了对手，格拉赛5000。格拉赛5000的焦糖圣代每个只卖5分钱。已经脱离常规的价格了。”

En: How come?
Zh: “怎么会这样？”

En: He said that the bear was his *vision beast*. He said he met his *vision beast* while high on desiccants. He called it 'Megatherion.'
Zh: “他说这是他的*幻想野兽*，自己是在干燥剂嗑嗨了的时候，跟他的*幻想野兽*相遇的。他把它叫做‘超圣兽’。”

En: Anyway, now you know the story of the fallen ice cream empire. She seems almost sad, finishing the story. Some dust beams swirl in the afternoon air. Her eyes follow it idly.
Zh: “总之，现在你知道了冰淇淋帝国覆灭的故事。”说完故事，她看起来几乎有些悲伤。午后的阳光中，几缕灰尘形成的光柱在盘旋着。她的眼睛漫不经心地追随其后。

En: Normal people don't have vision beasts. Only drug-addled madmen like the taxidermist do.
Zh: “普通人都没有幻想野兽。只有像动物标本剥制师那样吸毒成瘾的疯子才会有。”

En: That was a hypothetical question.
Zh: “这是个假设性的问题。”

En: Of course they don't. I'm a normal person and I don't have a vision beast.
Zh: “当然不会了。我是个普通人，我就没有幻想野兽。”

En: Right, says the dicemaker. She doesn't look entirely convinced.
Zh: “没错，”骰子匠人回答到。她看起来并没有完全信服。

En: What about horrific neckties? Do normal people have horrific neckties?
Zh: “那可怕的领带呢？普通人也有可怕的领带吗？”

En: Neckties? Her eyes move to the piss-coloured cloth tied around your neck. "I guess they do sometimes, officer... But I don't understand how it's relevant to our discussion."
Zh: “领带？”她的目光移动到你脖子周围那条尿色的布料上。“我猜有时候会有吧，警官……但是我不明白，这跟我们讨论的话题有什么关系。”

En: The taxidermist who made it said it was his *vision beast*. He said he met his *vision beast* while high on desiccants. He called it 'Megatherion.'
Zh: “做它的那个动物标本剥制师说，这是他的*幻想野兽*。他说自己是在干燥剂嗑嗨了的时候，跟他的*幻想野兽*相遇的。他把它叫做‘超圣兽’。”

En: Very cool, but I don't want to hear about bears anymore. Let's move on.
Zh: “挺酷的，不过我不想再听那只熊的故事了。我们继续说别的吧。”

En: That bear was one mighty mammal. The taxidermist who made it said it was his *vision beast*. He said he met his *vision beast* while high on desiccants. He called it 'Megatherion.'
Zh: “那只熊是一个强大的哺乳动物。做它的那个动物标本剥制师说，这是他的*幻想野兽*。他说自己是在干燥剂嗑嗨了的时候，跟他的*幻想野兽*相遇的。他把它叫做‘超圣兽’。”

En: Oh, I totally understand.
Zh: “哦，我完全能够理解。”

En: The taxidermist who made it said it was his *vision beast*. He said he met his *vision beast* while high on desiccants. He called it 'Megatherion.'
Zh: “做它的那个动物标本剥制师说，这是他的*幻想野兽*。他说自己是在干燥剂嗑嗨了的时候，跟他的*幻想野兽*相遇的。他把它叫做‘超圣兽’。”

En: That's horrible. Were they arrested?
Zh: “真是太可怕了。他们被逮捕了吗？”

En: Wait, people do that?
Zh: “等等，大家会这么做吗？”

En: I believe the ICP got them. They have a special division that deals exclusively with sub rosas.
Zh: “我相信ICP抓到他们了。ICP有个特殊部门，专门处理秘密电台的问题。”

En: It's the copopedia reporting in: ICP stands for International Collaboration Police.
Zh: 小百科为您报道：ICP代表国际联合刑警。

En: Honestly, I think it's quite funny. I think he's still sending out holiday transmissions from Touloula or Tioumoutiri or Khasht-Kor, or wherever he is.
Zh: “老实说，我觉得还挺有意思的。我觉得他还在发送节日讯号，从陶尔洛拉，提欧穆特里，卡希科尔，或者随便什么地方。”

En: That's illegal -- running off with company's money like that. Why hasn't he been arrested?
Zh: “这是违法的——像这样卷走公司的钱逃跑。为什么他没有被逮捕呢？”

En: Sure, it's illegal, but it's not exactly anything *extraordinary* in business. Besides, Slipstream is history now. All their remaining assets got seized by the bailiffs in '47. I have no idea why those skis and blades are still lying around.
Zh: “当然，这是违法的，但在商界，这算不上是很*离奇*的事情。再说，现在气流已经成为历史。所有剩下来的资产都在47年被法警查封了。我不知道为什么房间里还留着那些滑雪板和动叶片。”

En: And you know how all kinds of political movements are *big* in the Occident. The activists shut down the biggest chitin suppliers, which of course caused the price to skyrocket.
Zh: “你也知道，奥西登国家的各种政治运动都会搞得很*大*。活动家们关闭了最大的甲壳素供应商，自然就导致价格一路猛涨。”

En: They had some kind of an accident with the backup copy, right?
Zh: “他们的备份拷贝好像出事故了，对吗？”

En: An accident? I wouldn't know anything about that. I just heard they ran out of money and couldn't get the project done on time.
Zh: “事故？这我就不知道了。我只听说他们没钱了，没办法按时完成项目。”

En: Money and time, she nods. "The usual."
Zh: “时间和金钱，”她点点头。“还是老一套。”

En: The usual. They ran out of money and couldn't get the project done on time.
Zh: “还是老一套。他们的钱用完了，没办法按时完成项目。”

En: What went wrong?
Zh: “出什么差错了？”

En: Well, I did hear them talking at times... She looks at the hallway, as if she can still hear them chit-chat behind her curtains on a cigarette break.
Zh: “呃，我有时会听到他们说话……”她看着走廊，仿佛还能在窗帘背后抽烟休息的时候，听见他们的闲谈。

En: In the end they just didn't get it done. They didn't have enough willpower to produce something *truly historic* -- and to show up to work on time.
Zh: “最后他们根本没办法完成项目。他们没有足够的意志力去生产一个*真正历史性*的东西——也没办法准时上班。”

En: Not the wisest decision: You would have lost all your savings. She tosses a pair of dice on the table; one of them stops near the edge of the metallic desk.
Zh: “这可不是最明智的决定：你可能会失去所有积蓄的。”她朝桌子上掷出一对骰子；其中一个停在金属桌面的边缘。

En: You're right, they should have just tried harder. They had everything needed to succeed.
Zh: “你说得对，他们应该再努力一些。他们已经拥有成功所需要的一切。”

En: Still, not everyone is going to make it. She tosses a pair of dice on the table; one of them stops near the edge of the metallic desk. "That's the nature of the game."
Zh: “不过，不是所有人都能成功的。”她朝桌子上掷出一对骰子；其中一个停在金属桌面的边缘。“这就是游戏的本质。”

En: Why would Slipstream SCA have a hundred-years-old recording as their doorbell message? It doesn't make any sense.
Zh: “为什么气流SCA会用一份几百年的录音当做门铃信息呢？这一点也说不通啊。”

En: Start taking off your clothes. You need to *connect*.
Zh: 开始脱衣服。你需要*连接*。

En: Leave it at that.
Zh: 不去管它。

En: Excuse me, what are you doing? The dicemaker stares at you while you start to fuss with your pants.
Zh: “不好意思，你在做什么？”骰子匠人盯着你开始摆弄自己的裤子。

En: She has begun to idly clean one of her carving tools with a dirty kerchief, the tool's sharp edge shining in the light of her desk lamp.
Zh: 她用一面肮脏的手帕徒然地擦拭着自己的雕刻工具，工具锋利的边缘在台灯的照耀下闪闪发光。

En: Is it just me or is it hot in here?
Zh: “只是我这么觉得，还是这个地方变热了？”

En: It's part of this special *technique* I have.
Zh: “这是我特殊*技巧*的一部分。”

En: Didn't we already talk about this? she asks, as the wind continues to seep in through the cracks in the old chimney.
Zh: “我们不是已经说过了吗？”她询问着，风不断地从旧烟囱的裂缝中渗透进来。

En: It's because you're not *in* the same building as the others. This isn't *technically* the Doomed Commercial Area.
Zh: “那是因为你*所在*的建筑跟其他人的不一样。从*技术层面*上讲，这里并不是诅咒商业区。”

En: What are you talking about? she says, shaking her head. "My address is exactly the same -- Rue de Saint-Ghislaine 10."
Zh: “你在说什么呢？”她摇头说到。“我的地址完全是一样的——圣吉莱纳路10号。”

En: This doesn't make any sense. She looks around the makeshift nest that she has carved out for herself, bewildered.
Zh: “这样根本说不通。”她四下打量着自己雕刻出来的临时小窝，样子有些困惑。

En: No, I don't feel *hot*. We're in a derelict smokestack. If anything then it's *cold* here!
Zh: “不，我没有感觉到*热*。我们在一个荒废的烟囱里。如果有什么感觉的话，那也应该是*冷*啊！”

En: Can you please employ this special *technique* somewhere else? I work here and my work requires concentration. Half-naked people don't help with that. She turns away, so that you can zip up your pants again.
Zh: “那能请你去别处使用这种特殊*技巧*吗？我要在这里工作，而且我的工作需要高度集中。半裸的人可帮不上忙。”她转过身以便你拉上拉链了。

En: Never mind, that was a terrible idea. (Do up your pants.)
Zh: “没关系，这确实是个很糟糕的主意。”（穿好你的裤子。）

En: Undressing in a stranger's place of business? Yes, it was -- an unbelievably bad idea. She trembles, as if trying to shake it off.
Zh: “在一个陌生人的营业场所脱衣服？是啊，没错——这确实是个让人难以置信的坏主意。”她颤抖着，像是要摆脱什么。

En: I'm trying to lower my body temperature.
Zh: “我是在尝试降低自己的体温。”

En: And *why* would you do that?!
Zh: “那你*为什么*要这么做呢？！”

En: Let's say I have my own methods... Unusual methods.
Zh: “这么说吧，我有自己的方法……不寻常的方法。”

En: Are you saying my business was spared because of a *technicality*? Where is this coming from?
Zh: “你是说，我的生意不受影响是因为一个*技术性细节*吗？这又是哪门子的说法？”

En: I'm just using logic -- it's windy in here. You probably haven't properly sealed the chimney crown. (Point above.)
Zh: “我只是在运用逻辑思维——这里经常刮风。你可能没有把烟囱顶封好。”（指向上方。）

En: Hmm, she says, looking up. "You're right, I should take a trip to the roof once the snow is gone."
Zh: “嗯，”她抬起头仰望，说到。“你说得对，等雪一停，我马上去屋顶看看。”

En: Except I've weighted the die. When you try rolling it, you realize that each time it gets you exactly the same result -- *God is indifferent*. This is our curse.
Zh: “我只是给骰子加重了。当你掷出它的时候，你会发现它每次都会给出相同的结果——*神明是冷漠的*。这就是我们的诅咒。”

En: This is mega cool, but can I get one that actually works?
Zh: “这也太酷了吧，我能要个真正能用的吗？”

En: Thanks, I'll keep it.
Zh: “谢谢，我会留着它的。”

En: It's even worse than she says. God is dead -- We live in a forsaken age.
Zh: 比她说的还要糟。神明已死——我们生活在一个被遗忘的年代。

En: You're saying that the end result doesn't matter.
Zh: “你是说最终结果不重要。”

En: In the face of death -- no, not really.
Zh: “面对死亡的话——不，不完全是。”

En: What a relief.
Zh: “真是解脱了。”

En: That's so depressing.
Zh: “那也太压抑了。”

En: You were the one who asked for a cursed die, she points out.
Zh: “要诅咒骰子的可是你，”她挑明了。

En: It is, isn't it? There's something liberating about knowing it's just we humans, no all powerful forces guiding us, watching us, *judging* us.
Zh: “确实如此，不是吗？知道只有人类的时候有种解放的感觉，没有全能的力量引导我们，看着我们，*评判*我们。”

En: I don't agree with you.
Zh: “我不同意你的看法。”

En: You don't? she raises her brows.
Zh: “是吗？”她扬起眉头。

En: As long as there is mankind, there is also meaning.
Zh: “只要还有人类，就会有意义。”

En: It matters as long as we're alive.
Zh: “只要我们还活着，就很重要。”

En: Yes, but how will mankind last?
Zh: “是的，但是人类该怎么延续呢？”

En: Don't answer that, actually. Meaning is whatever you want to make of it. That's the nature of meaning.
Zh: “说实在的，还是不要回答了。意义是你自己想要赋予的。这就是意义的本质。”

En: Can I get a normal version of this die? One that isn't modified to land on a single result?
Zh: “能不能给我来个这骰子的普通版？没有修改过，不会每次都是同一个结果的？”

En: I guess I'll keep the die then.
Zh: “那我还是留着这个骰子吧。”

En: Good luck, officer, she says with a mischievous smile, before turning back to her table.
Zh: “祝你好运，警官，”她淘气地笑着说到，然后又转回到桌子旁边了。

En: No, I don't have another die. You'll have to do with this one.
Zh: “不，我没有其他骰子了。你只能用这个。”

En: I'll keep that in mind. Thanks for the die.
Zh: “我会记下来的。谢谢你的骰子。”

En: Doesn't really fly with my vibe, too morbid.
Zh: “跟我的气场不是太般配，太病态。”

En: Sorry, I don't have another one ready. You were the one who asked for a cursed die.
Zh: “抱歉，我没有其他现成的骰子。要诅咒骰子的人可是你。”

En: I guess you can find some temporary solace in that, yes.
Zh: “我猜你确实能从中找到一些短暂的安慰，没错。”

En: Can I get a normal version of this die? One that isn't modified to land on a single result?
Zh: “能不能给我来个这骰子的普通版？没有修改过，不会每次都是同一个结果的？”

En: Still, thanks for the die.
Zh: “不过，还是要谢谢你的骰子。”

En: Not in this die, rest assured. It isn't cursed. I can tell you what it does.
Zh: “这个骰子可没有，放心。它没有被诅咒。我可以告诉你是怎么回事。”

En: So you confess?
Zh: “你就这么承认了？”

En: I am nothing if not consistent.
Zh: “如果不是始终如一的话，我什么都不是。”

En: Fantastic. Can the witch *Entity* tell you what the cursed die does then?
Zh: “太棒了。那巫师能告诉*存在*这个诅咒骰子是做什么用的吗？”

En: Yes, it's better to know how the evil works.
Zh: “是的，最好知道邪灵是怎么工作的。”

En: No, I don't want to know, it's too dangerous.
Zh: “不，我不想知道，实在是太危险了。”

En: Yes, I've got my warpaint protecting me from the evil. (Point to your face.)
Zh: “是的，我的战争油彩可以保护我不受邪灵侵害。”（指指自己的脸。）

En: Too bad, I'm going to tell you anyway.
Zh: “很遗憾，反正我怎么都要告诉你的。”

En: Oh, now I'm evil, too -- lovely.
Zh: “哦，现在我也是邪灵了——真好。”

En: Cast the cursed die.
Zh: 投掷诅咒骰子。

En: Oh, it's you. She turns around. "I'm surprised to see you here this late. What can I help you with?"
Zh: “哦，是你。”她转过身来。“我真是有点吃惊，居然这么晚还能见到你。有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Oh, it's you again. Are you looking for a die?
Zh: “哦，又是你。你是想要骰子吗？”

En: Hello again. She turns around. "You must really like walking through the dark. What can I help you with?"
Zh: “又见面了。”她转过身来。“你一定很喜欢在黑暗中行走吧。有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Yes, a milieu is like a call-in station. You need a two-way radio to access one. That's why I have these. She pats the headphones on the table.
Zh: “是的，电台就像是一个呼叫中心。要接入的话，需要一台收发报机。所以我有这些东西。”她拍了拍桌子上的耳机。

En: Could this be the malicious Entity? Perhaps it's wise to go along with this *masquerade* for now...
Zh: 难道这就是邪恶的存在吗？也许暂时附和这个*伪装*会比较明智……

En: So, what kind of die are you looking for?
Zh: “所以，你在找什么样的骰子呢？”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Maybe. I'm not really sure why I'm here, honestly.
Zh: “也许吧。老实说，我也不知道自己为什么会来这里。”

En: No, I was looking for something else... (Squint your eyes mysteriously.) "*Answers*."
Zh: “不，我在找别的东西……”（神秘兮兮地眯起双眼。）“*答案*。”

En: Sure, I like role-playing games and I need some dice.
Zh: “当然，我喜欢角色扮演游戏，而且我需要骰子。”

En: Very good, she says. "My rate is 10 reál per set, unless you want something really unusual..."
Zh: “很好，”她说到。“我的价格是一对10雷亚尔，除非你要的东西不太一般……”

En: How strange, she says. "Well, if you're interested, my rate is 10 reál per set, unless you want something really unusual..."
Zh: “真奇怪，”她说着。“如果你感兴趣的话，我的价格是一对10雷亚尔，除非你要的东西不太一般……”

En: Take a look around and see if there's any particular stone you want to use. The walls around her are covered with rows of precious stones and minerals.
Zh: “到处看看吧，有没有什么你特别想用的石头。”她周围的墙面上堆满一排排的宝石和矿物。

En: Answers? How strange. These days people only come to me for dice and role-playing games...
Zh: “答案。真奇怪。最近大家都是为了骰子和角色扮演游戏来找我的……”

En: I'm not sure how helpful I'll be, but go ahead and ask. The walls around her are covered with rows of precious stones and minerals.
Zh: “我不确定自己到底能帮你多少，不过还是问吧。”她周围的墙面上堆满一排排的宝石和矿物。

En: How did that community project work out? (Conclude.)
Zh: “那个社会项目进展如何？”（总结。）

En: And who was Artemitep?
Zh: “阿特米普是谁？”

En: Maybe that's what Cuno needs -- a community-centric boxing club.
Zh: “也许这就是坤诺需要的——以社区为中心的拳击俱乐部。”

En: Judging from the kids I've met so far, it didn't really work, did it? (Conclude.)
Zh: “从我目前见过的小孩来判断，其实真的没什么用，对吧？”（总结。）

En: A kind man... from Zsiemsk. I heard he had some trouble with the law when he was younger, and that's why he wanted to start the gym, as his way of giving back.
Zh: “一个好人……从西姆斯科来的。我听说他年轻的时候好像触犯过法律，所以才想到开一家健身房，作为回馈社会的方式。”

En: Hm, Cuno. Her eyes narrow in the dim light. "Who's Cuno?"
Zh: “嗯，坤诺。”昏暗的灯光下，她眯起双眼。“坤诺是谁？”

En: He's sort of the king around here.
Zh: “他有点像这附近的小霸王。”

En: He's a little ginger gremlin who likes to defile dead bodies.
Zh: “他是个姜黄色的小麻烦精，喜欢玷污尸体。”

En: Indeed, who *is* Cuno... Your guess is as good as mine.
Zh: “确实，坤诺*是*谁……我也不知道。”

En: Oh, you mean the kid with the sailor's mouth. Yes, I've heard him yelling profanities in the backyard... She looks out of the window, her face reflecting back in the dark.
Zh: “哦，你是说那个嘴很脏的小鬼。我听见他在后院大声说脏话……”她看向窗外，黑暗将她的面容反射回来。

En: I think it will take more than a gym to help that kid.
Zh: “要帮助那种小孩的话，一家健身房怕是不够的。”
