En: They certainly took their work very seriously. Even if they seemed to be... chronically *liberal* with their schedules.
Zh: “他们对待自己的工作确实很认真。虽然他们好像……对日程安排有种惯性的*自由主义*风格。”

En: Ice... death... loss... sounds like you.
Zh: 冰……死亡……损失……听起来很像你的风格。

En: That sounds like me.
Zh: “听起来挺像我的风格。”

En: I had a feeling. She smiles, but says nothing more.
Zh: “我感觉到了。”她笑了笑，但是不再言语。

En: Come on now. Be a little creative.
Zh: “来吧。再有创意一点。”

En: What are the odds? The dicemaker winks back at you. "The red one is made of bloodstone with a lapis lazuli inlay and the blue one is the inverse. I believe these dice are your lucky charm, officer..."
Zh: “这种可能性有多少？”骰子匠人也朝你眨眨眼。“红色那颗是用血石做的，镶嵌着一颗天青石。蓝色那颗正好相反。我相信这些骰子会成为你的幸运符，警官……”

En: She winked back at you! Can you believe it? You truly are Mr. Smooth Moves!
Zh: 她居然也朝你眨眼了！你能相信吗？你是货真价实的好身手先生！

En: Now, was there anything else?
Zh: “现在，还有其他事吗？”

En: I met one of their investors. He said they lacked the will to finish anything.
Zh: “我见过其中一个投资人。他说他们缺少完成任何事情的意志力。”

En: Ha, the salty investors... Something green sparkles in her eyes. "Well yes, they couldn't get the project done on time and thus lost the funding."
Zh: “哈，搞笑的投资人……”她的眼睛里闪烁着绿色的光芒。“是啊，他们没有按时完成项目，所以失去了资金援助。”

En: That's too bad. I would have supported them -- the project looked great.
Zh: “真是太糟糕了。要是我应该会支持他们——这个项目看起来棒极了。”

En: Ah, of course they left it plugged in. Even in death the bear is costing them money.
Zh: “啊，他们当然不会拔掉电源了。就算是死，那只熊也在花他们的钱。”

En: Excellent. That will be 10 reál for one set of magnetic dice.
Zh: “太棒了。一对磁性骰子10雷亚尔。”

En: Very good. That will be seven reál for one custom die.
Zh: “很好。一个定制骰子是7雷亚尔。”

En: Sure! She laughs. "Now can the *Entity* explain what your cursed die does?"
Zh: “当然！”她笑了。“现在*存在*是不是可以解释一下，你的诅咒骰子是做什么用的？”

En: It has a different address in the heart of the city.
Zh: “它有个不同的地址，在市中心。”

En: And what technique is that?! She squints at you, a little astonished at what she's hearing. You still haven't gotten your zipper open.
Zh: 她眯起眼睛看着你，对自己听到的东西有些吃惊。“那又是哪门子技巧？！”你的拉链还没拉开呢。

En: Okay, hear me out: Sometimes I get these *feelings*...
Zh: “好吧，听我说完：有时候，我会有些*感觉*……”

En: You know what? Forget it. It was a bad idea. (Do up your pants.)
Zh: “你知道吗？算了吧。这不是个好主意。”（扣上你的裤子。）

En: Officer, this isn't how *mature* adults deal with their *feelings*.
Zh: “警官，*成熟*的成年人是不会这样处理他们的*感觉*的。”

En: No, not like that. It's like I can feel the *wind* or, I don't know, the *air pressure*, and then the air *tells* me things.
Zh: “不，不是这样的。就好像我能感觉到*风*，或者，我也不知道，是*气压*，然后空气会*告诉*我一些事情。”

En: The air pressure told you to take your pants off in my studio?
Zh: “所以是气压告诉你在我的工作室里脱裤子？”

En: Yes, exactly.
Zh: “是的，没错。”

En: No, no, I'm not explaining this very well.
Zh: “不是，不是，我也解释不好。”

En: *Now* you feel something, the cold damp of shame washing over you.
Zh: *现在*你感觉到某种东西，羞愧的寒潮席卷了你的全身。

En: Can we please just forget this ever happened? (Do up your pants.)
Zh: “我们能不能彻底忘掉这件事？”（穿好你的裤子。）

En: *Gladly*. The dicemaker turns away so that you can do up your pants.
Zh: “我很*乐意*。”骰子匠人转过身去，这样你就能穿好裤子。

En: Officer! The lieutenant exclaims sharply.
Zh: “警官！”警督尖声惊叫。

En: Miss, I apologize for my colleague's behaviour. He's still recovering from an *unusual* medical episode...
Zh: “小姐，我为我同事的行为向你道歉。他经历了一场*不寻常*的医疗小插曲，现在还在恢复中……”

En: A wise and noble beast, guiding you toward the land where the streets are paved... with drugs!
Zh: 一头聪明而又高贵的野兽，引导你走向那条大道……铺满毒品的大道！

En: Maybe this wise and noble beast can guide you toward those *amphetamines* you've been craving.
Zh: 也许这头聪明而又高贵的野兽可以引导你找到你渴望的*安非他命*。

En: Even you can probably do better than that.
Zh: 就算是你，也许都能做得更好。

En: So you're telling me that you have a doorbell there -- which one?
Zh: “所以你说你这里也有门铃吗——哪一个？”

En: So you're telling me that the doorbell with the empty name card was yours?
Zh: “所以说那个空白名牌的门铃是你的？”

En: The one with an empty name card, it's the last one in the list. As I said, it's quite useless right now, it doesn't work yet.
Zh: “名牌是空的那个，就是清单上的最后一个。我说过，现在它没什么用，暂时还没好呢。”

En: The doorbell with the empty name card must belong to her then.
Zh: 门铃上那个空名牌应该就是她的了……

En: That's right, I haven't even written my name there -- as I said, it's quite useless right now, it doesn't work yet.
Zh: “没错，我还没在上面写名字呢——我说过，现在它没什么用，暂时还没好呢。”

En: She looks like she doesn't really believe you. "It can't be true. They don't work here anymore, they've been gone for *years*."
Zh: 看来她并没有真正相信你。“这不可能是真的。他们早就不在这里工作，*几年前*就搬走了。”

En: Are you sure it was Slipstream SCA? Was it a *woman*? Maybe it was Plaisance from the bookstore...
Zh: “你确定是气流SCA吗？是一个*女人*吗？也许是书店的普莱桑斯……”

En: First, it wasn't Plaisance, I know her and would have recognized her voice.
Zh: “首先，那个不是普莱桑斯，我认识她，应该能认出她的声音。”

En: She said she was from Tricentennial Electrics.
Zh: “她说她来自三生电气。”

En: Alright. But did this person say anything? She still sounds sceptical.
Zh: “好吧。但是那个人说什么了吗？”她听起来还是有些怀疑。

En: Tricentennial Electrics? There's a moment before she recognizes the name.
Zh: “三生电气？”她隔了一会儿才想起这个名字。

En: It used to be a major electric company one hundred years ago. Are you sure it wasn't just some kids playing a prank on you?
Zh: “那是一百年前一家大电气公司的名字。你确定不是熊孩子在跟你开玩笑吗？”

En: Hey, where are we anyway? What is this place? (Look around the room.)
Zh: “话说，我这是在哪儿？这是什么地方？”（环顾这个房间。）

En: We're inside the chimney of an old central furnace. It's strange, I know. She looks at the ruddy bricks that make up the walls -- even though they've been repainted there are still signs of coal-black soot here and there.
Zh: “我们正处在一个旧中央壁炉的烟囱里。我知道，是有点奇怪。”她看着墙壁上那些红色的砖块——虽然已经被重新粉刷过，但到处依然残留着乌黑烟灰的痕迹。

En: But when I arrived here, all the other rooms were taken, so I had to build myself a makeshift home. Besides, I don't really have to pay any rent here, so that's a plus.
Zh: “但是我来到这里的时候，其他房间都被占用了，所以我只能自己临时搭了一个小窝。再说了，我在这里也不用付租金，这也是个加分项。”

En: Do you know what happened to other tenants? Everyone else is gone.
Zh: “你知道其他租客发生什么事了吗？其他人都不在了。”

En: More or less. She adjusts the yellow scarf that covers her hair. "Are you interested in anyone specific?"
Zh: “多少知道一点吧。”她调整了一下头上的黄头巾。“你对谁特别感兴趣吗？”

En: I've heard this place is cursed. Did you know that people call it the Doomed Commercial Area?
Zh: “我听说这个地方被诅咒了。你知道大家都叫这里诅咒商业区吗？”

En: It didn't. If anything, it made the youth situation in Martinaise even worse. At some point, someone started a rumour that the punching bag downstairs was full of *amphetamines*.
Zh: “不怎么样。如果说有什么的话，它只是让马丁内斯青少年的处境更加糟糕了。在某一时刻，有人开始散播传闻，说楼下的拳击吊袋里面装满了*安非他命*。”

En: Eventually the Coalition took away the funding and the club went bankrupt. This was a few years ago. It's gotten much more peaceful around the plaza ever since.
Zh: “最后，联盟撤走资金，俱乐部破产。这已经是几年前的事了。从那以后，广场周围就变得更加安静。”

En: A strange thing happened when I tried calling a company named Slipstream SCA -- someone answered.
Zh: “我试着打给一家名叫气流SCA的公司，然后发生了一件很奇怪的事——有人回应了。”

En: I don't want hear about this sick shit anymore.
Zh: “我不想再听到这些恶心的事。”

En: Good call. She lets the thought go.
Zh: “说得好。”她放弃了这个想法。

En: No, this used to be a coal plant. (Touch the safety curtains.) "You're in a chimney of *another* building."
Zh: “不，这里以前是一个火电站。”（触摸防火幕。）“你在*另一栋*建筑的烟囱里。”

En: No one's really ever safe from the failure.
Zh: “没有人能真正避免失败。”

En: Rest assured, no psychic attacks will reach you here.
Zh: “放心，你在这里不会受到灵异袭击的。”

En: Actually, it's only your workshop that's protected. You should still do something to defend your person.
Zh: “其实，只有你这家工作室是受到保护的。不过你还是应该做点什么来保护自己。”

En: Don't let her become complacent! She still needs to ward *her soul* against the evil forces!
Zh: 别让她太得意！她还需要保护*自己的灵魂*，不受邪恶力量的侵害！

En: I've heard the stories, she nods, as the wind howls in from the furnace shaft above. "But I don't think those stories are true."
Zh: “我听说过一些故事，”她点点头，风从头顶的上风坚井中呼啸而来。“但是我不觉得那些故事是真的。”

En: Plaisance is the one who sent me. She's convinced that the place is swarming with malicious energies.
Zh: “普莱桑斯派我来的。她确信这个地方充满了邪恶能量。”

En: Plaisance, the bookshop lady? She raises her brows. "I've heard that her business is doing rather well. Have the *energies* spared her somehow?"
Zh: “普莱桑斯，书店的那个女士吗？”她扬起眉头。“我听说她的生意挺不错的。难道是那个*能量*放过她了？”

En: Plaisance is a wise woman. She has Semenese trinkets protecting her against the curse.
Zh: “普莱桑斯是个聪明的女人。她用塞美尼饰品保护自己不受诅咒影响。”

En: Alright. But it's not just the bookstore that's still up and running. What about the Whirling-in-Rags? Some people say it's part of the building complex.
Zh: “好吧。不过也不是只有书店还在运营啊。那褴褛飞旋呢？有人说它也是这栋综合建筑的一部分。”

En: Actually, the bookstore isn't doing that well. There are hardly any customers and she has to exploit her own daughter to keep the company going.
Zh: “其实，书店的状况并不理想。基本上没有多少顾客，为了维持运营，她甚至剥削了自己的亲生女儿。”

En: Ah, the Semenese trinkets, of course... She nods as if this explains everything.
Zh: “啊，塞美尼饰品，当然了……”她点点头，仿佛一切都解释清楚了。

En: You're right, the Whirling doesn't really look like it's cursed.
Zh: “你说得对，飞旋旅社看起来确实不像被诅咒了。”

En: I don't know why the bookstore hasn't gone bankrupt yet -- that's what I'm here to investigate.
Zh: “我不知道书店为什么还没有破产——这就是我来这里调查的原因。”

En: The curse is just biding its time before it strikes again. Sooner or later everyone will fail -- even her.
Zh: “诅咒只是在等待时机，好发动下一次袭击。迟早所有人都会失败的——就连她也逃不过。”

En: Yes, but it's still a separate building. The malicious energies can't reach there.
Zh: “是的，但它还是在一栋隔开的建筑里。邪恶能量够不到那里。”

En: And then there's me... She sighs, looking at her messy work table. All kinds of tools lie there scattered, from knives to carving files to wire cutters.
Zh: “然后就是我……”她叹了口气，看着眼前乱糟糟的工作台。各种各样的工具凌乱地散落着，从刀具、雕刻锉刀到剪线钳，应有尽有。

En: No, the Whirling isn't doing well either: its waitress just took off and customers have trouble paying bills.
Zh: “不，飞旋旅社的状况也不太好：那里的女服务员刚离职，顾客也付不起账单。”

En: I've been here for 14 years, selling novelty dice to role-playing enthusiasts. Not exactly a million reál business idea, yet somehow I've survived despite the talk of malicious energies. Strange, isn't it?
Zh: “我在这里待了14年，把新奇骰子卖给角色扮演游戏爱好者。这并不是一个价值百万雷亚尔的商业构想，不过我还是顶住了你说的那股邪恶能量，生存了下来。有点奇怪，不是吗？”

En: I yelled to summon the succubus plaguing the Doomed Commercial Area and you were the one who answered.\n
Zh: “我大声召唤折磨这片诅咒商业区的女妖，然后你应答了。”

En: I yelled to summon the ghost of the Doomed Commercial Area and you were the one who answered.
Zh: “我大声召唤诅咒商业区的幽灵，然后你应答了。”

En: Role-playing games? You know role-playing games: Wirrâl, Wirrâl Untethered, um, what else...
Zh: 角色扮演游戏？你知道角色扮演游戏：威勒尔，自由威勒尔，呃，还有什么……

En: Role-playing games? You know Wirrâl. Does that count?
Zh: 角色扮演游戏？你知道威勒尔。这算吗？

En: Role-playing games? You know the one made by Fortress Accident. Does that count?
Zh: 角色扮演游戏？你知道要塞事故做的那个。这算吗？

En: Oh, yes -- you mean the kid with the sailor's mouth? Yes, I've heard him yelling profanities in the backyard... She looks out of the window; it's oddly quiet there at the moment.
Zh: “哦，是的——你是说那个嘴很脏的小孩？我听见他在后院大声说脏话……”她看向窗外；此时此刻，那里出奇的安静。

En: They were an interesting bunch. We talked about role-playing systems every now and then. Once I even saw two of them get into fisticuffs over Wirrâl...
Zh: “他们是一群很有趣的家伙。我们经常会谈论一些角色扮演游戏系统的问题。我曾经看到两个人因为威勒尔而大打出手……”

En: Well, showing up to work on time *is* incredibly hard.
Zh: “呃，按时上班*确实*挺困难的。”

En: And so is producing something extraordinary. Her eyes wander to the shelves full of die prototypes and discarded models.
Zh: “生产出某种超凡脱俗的东西也是如此。”她的眼睛在架子上游荡，上面堆满骰子原型和废弃的模型。

En: I think I found the *actual* source of the curse.
Zh: “我想我找到诅咒*真正的*源头了。”

En: Did you know the East Delta Pinball was once the Whirling?
Zh: “你知不知道飞旋旅社的前身就是东德尔塔弹球？”

En: East Delta Pinball -- is that now the Whirling?
Zh: “东德尔塔弹球——现在的飞旋旅社吗？”

En: Yes. I've heard it used to be a gaming arcade. This is an *ancient* failure -- before my time. I'm not surprised, however.
Zh: “是的。我听说那里曾经是一家游戏机室。那是一次*古老*的失败——早在我的时代之前。不过，我也不觉得惊讶。”

En: My advice... She raises her finger. "Don't base your business on a fad -- hypnotism, floriography, triktrak, especially *pinball*."
Zh: “我的建议……”她竖起手指。“不要把你的生意建立在一时的狂热上——催眠术，花语，双陆棋，特别是*弹球*。”

En: Yes. I guess so. The arcade is an *ancient* failure -- before my time. I'm not surprised, however.
Zh: “没错，我猜也是。那家游戏机室是一次*古老*的失败——早在我的时代之前。不过，我也不觉得惊讶。”

En: She's got a direct view to the backyard. You should interrogate her about the lynching.
Zh: 她可以直接看到后院。你应该询问她私刑的事。

En: Plaisance was right... There's an entity living in the chimney! You should ask her about the curse.
Zh: 普莱桑斯是对的……烟囱里确实住着一个存在！你应该问问她诅咒的事。

En: Does it mean she's *unlawfully* occupying a part of the house? Shameful!
Zh: 这是不是意味着她*非法*占用了房子的一部分？真可耻！

En: Nothing, really. I didn't know him.
Zh: “什么都不知道，真的。我不认识他。”

En: Who cares about the dead body? Ask her about the curse already!
Zh: 谁在乎那具尸体啊？问问她诅咒的事！

En: I have more questions about this building.
Zh: “关于这栋建筑，我还有些问题。”

En: It wasn't merely a gym, it was *Artemitep's Boxing Club* -- a community project created to steer at-risk youths away from drugs and crime.
Zh: “它不仅仅是一个健身房，它是*阿特米普拳击俱乐部*，一个社区项目，旨在引导高危青少年远离毒品和犯罪。”

En: Yes, I think it was called Androgynous Orlando or something similar. They weren't a big hit around here -- turns out that working class men don't like genderless haircuts. They're scared of that word.
Zh: “是的，我觉得好像是叫双性奥兰多或者之类的名字。他们在这里不是很受欢迎——工人们不太喜欢无性别的发型。他们很害怕那个词。”

En: I'm listening.
Zh: “我在听。”

En: Hm, what's a snuff milieu?
Zh: 嗯，鼻烟电台是什么？

En: Trust me, you don't want to know.
Zh: 相信我，你不想知道的。

En: Anything else?
Zh: “还有别的事吗？”

En: I found the building's intercom, but it's not working.
Zh: “我找到了这栋建筑的对讲机，但是没有用。”

En: You wouldn't like it either. The others would laugh at you.
Zh: 你也不会喜欢这个的。其他人会笑话你的。

En: A bit of experimenting every now and then isn't bad.
Zh: 偶尔尝试一下也不算很糟糕。

En: It's not about the haircut, it's about the *confidence*.
Zh: 这跟发型无关，跟*自信心*有关。

En: What's wrong with a bit of experimenting? The customers should have been more open-minded.
Zh: “稍微尝试一下怎么了？顾客应该思想再开放一些。”

En: I would *never* let anyone androgynous touch my hair, not even if my life depended on it.
Zh: “我*永远*不会让任何双性人碰我的头发，就算我就靠它活着也不行。”

En: I guess I'm a simple man, I don't really have any opinions on hair styles.
Zh: “我猜我是个简单的男人，我对发型真的没什么意见。”

En: You're right, it looks like you haven't let anyone near your hair in a long time. She tilts her head, looking at your shaggy mane.
Zh: “你说对，看起来你已经很久没让人碰过你的头发了。”她歪着头，看着你乱糟糟的胡子。

En: I guess it just wasn't the time yet. She tucks a strand of hair under the headscarf.
Zh: “我猜只是时机不对。”她把一缕头发塞到头巾下面。

En: She's right -- when was the last time? You should get an un-androgynous crew cut.
Zh: 她说得对——上次理发是什么时候？你应该理一个完全不双性的平头。

En: Me neither. I just want it off my face. She tucks a strand of hair under her headscarf.
Zh: “我也不在乎。只要不挡住我的脸就好。”她把一缕头发塞到头巾下面。

En: This is still just a theory, but hear me out: I think I know why your business hasn't failed.
Zh: “这还只是个理论，但是听我说：我觉得我已经知道你的生意为什么还没有失败了。”

En: And what, does it mean that I'm safe from failure?
Zh: “什么，那是不是意味着我不会失败？”

En: Does it mean that I'm safe from failure?
Zh: “那是不是意味着我不会失败？”

En: She starts laughing, her fingers trying to rub away the exhaustion from her face.
Zh: 她笑了起来，试着用手指拂去脸上的疲惫。

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: (Look away.) "I want to talk about something else now."
Zh: （转移目光。）“我现在想聊点别的了。”

En: Do you know what this is? She raises her hand to reveal a piece of metal shining on her index finger.
Zh: “你知道这是什么吗？”她举起手，露出食指上闪烁的金属。

En: A lucky charm? A Semenese ward?
Zh: “一个幸运符？一个塞美尼守护？”

En: Your lucky charm? Some kind of a ward?
Zh: “你的幸运符？某种守护？”

En: It looks like a regular piece of jewellery to me.
Zh: “在我看来，就是普通的珠宝首饰。”

En: I don't care what it is, I only came to tell you about why you haven't failed yet.
Zh: “我不在乎它是什么，我来只是为了告诉你，为什么你的生意还没有失败。”

En: It's a mourning ring, she replies. "I made this when my first company failed. It was a small jewellery shop right here in the East Delta Commerce Centre, built with the little I inherited from my parents..."
Zh: “这是一枚纪念戒指，”她回复到。“我的第一家公司破产的时候，我把它做出来了。那是一间小小的珠宝店，就在东德尔塔商业中心，是用我从父母那里继承的一点点遗产开的……”

En: I drove it into the ground within a year. I didn't have what you would call a *viable* business plan.
Zh: “不到一年，我就把它给毁掉了。我没有你们口中*可行的*商业计划。”

En: See? The curse is real! I bet you didn't run this little jewellery shop from the protective depths of the chimney?
Zh: “看见了吗？诅咒是真的！我敢打赌，你的那家小珠宝店，应该没有开在这个有防护作用的烟囱深处吧？”

En: Why are you telling me this?
Zh: “你为什么要告诉我这些？”

En: No, you're right, I didn't. She laughs again, but it sounds rather small and sad.
Zh: “不，你说得对，我没有。”她又笑了出来，但是这次声音很小，很悲伤。

En: It wasn't just the jewellery shop either. I always thought that it was just the world, that you were supposed to try again and again until you finally succeed.
Zh: “它也不仅仅是一家珠宝店。我一直以为这就是世界，你应该一次又一次地尝试，直到最终取得成功。”

En: It's a mourning ring, she replies, talking over your voice. "I made this when my first company failed. It was a small jewellery shop right here in the East Delta Commerce Centre, built with the little I inherited from my parents..."
Zh: “这是一枚纪念戒指，”她的回复盖住了你的声音。“我的第一家公司破产的时候，我把它做出来了。那是一间小小的珠宝店，就在东德尔塔商业中心，是用我从父母那里继承的一点点遗产开的……”

En: And now you're telling me, what? She closes her eyes. "That it was all because I didn't run my little shops and ventures from a dump inside an abandoned chimney?"
Zh: “现在你又告诉我，什么，”她闭上双眼，“都是因为我没有把那家小店和公司开在一个废弃烟囱的垃圾堆里？”

En: Don't call it a dump, you've made it nice and cosy here.
Zh: “别叫它垃圾堆，你把这里布置的挺好的，很舒适。”

En: I'm sorry. I don't know what to say. It's entropy.
Zh: “抱歉，我不知道该说什么。这是熵，反映的是体系的混乱程度。”

En: Yes. Coincidence is all that safeguards us.
Zh: “是的。只有巧合才能保护我们。”

En: Yeah. She stares out of the window, not really hearing your words. "Or maybe it's the entire world that's cursed? It's such a precarious place. Nothing ever works out the way you wanted."
Zh: “是啊。”她凝视着窗外，并没有听到你的话。“或许整个世界都被诅咒了？这是个多么危险的地方啊。从来没有什么是按照你希望的方式运作的。”

En: But anyway... thanks for sharing your theories, officer. She gives you a tired smile.
Zh: “但是不管怎么说……谢谢你能分享自己的理论，警官。”她朝你露出一个疲惫的微笑。

En: You mean the curse that I'm spared of because I live outside its immediate reach?
Zh: “你的意思是说，诅咒放过我，是因为我住在它够不着的地方？”

En: I thought we agreed that the curse isn't real.
Zh: “我以为我们都赞同那个诅咒不是真的。”

En: Yes, well... about that.
Zh: “是的，呃……那个。”

En: It's a bit more complicated than I first thought it would be.
Zh: “这比我刚开始想的还要复杂。”

En: I'm listening. She leans back in her chair, arms crossed across the chest.
Zh: “我在听。”她靠回到椅子上，双臂交叉放在胸前。

En: (Just say it.) "There's a two-millimetre hole in reality located in a church on the other side of the canal. I think it may be related to pale."
Zh: （直接说吧。）“现实里有一个2毫米的小洞，就在运河对面有一座教堂。我觉得它可能跟灰域有关。”

En: Excuse me? She sits up, visibly agitated. "A two-millimetre hole in reality? This can't be true."
Zh: “什么？”她坐起身来，明显有些焦虑。“现实里有一个2毫米的小洞？这不可能是真的。”

En: I'm afraid it is, ma'am. Soona Luukanen-Kilde, the former lead programmer of Fortress Accident, made the discovery.
Zh: “恐怕是的，女士。苏娜·卢凯伦-吉尔德，要塞事故的前任主程序员，就是她发现的。”

En: I'm afraid it is. Soona Luukanen-Kilde, the former lead programmer of Fortress Accident, made the discovery.
Zh: “恐怕是的。苏娜·卢凯伦-吉尔德，要塞事故的前任主程序员，就是她发现的。”

En: Soona is involved in this? She appears to take this in while the chatter from her headphones continues unabated...
Zh: “苏娜也参与了？”她似乎还在消化这个消息，但耳机里的颤动声还在继续，丝毫没有减弱……

En: Now that I think about it... It sounds too preposterous to say out loud.
Zh: “现在这么一想……听起来好像有点太荒谬，都不好意思大声说出来。”

En: What? You came all the way up here to tell me that you *can't* tell me because I won't like the answer?
Zh: “什么？你大老远跑上来就是为了告诉我，你*不能*告诉我是因为我不会喜欢那个答案？”

En: So it's even worse than I thought. It's not just the commercial area that's cursed, it's *the entire world*.
Zh: “所以比我想象的还要糟糕。看来不是这个商业区被诅咒了，而是*整个世界*。”

En: She looks outside the window where the night has fallen on the yard.
Zh: 她看着窗外，夜色降临在院子里。

En: She looks outside the window where daylight has filled the yard.
Zh: 她看着窗外，院子里充满阳光。

En: What? No, I wouldn't go as far to say that the entire world is doomed.
Zh: “什么？不，我不会说什么整个世界都被诅咒了。”

En: It's what I have preached the whole time -- and no one listened.
Zh: “这就是我一直在宣扬的——但是没有人听。”

En: Don't worry about it. You've been doing fine so far. I'm sure it's nothing.
Zh: “别担心。目前为止你一切都还好。我肯定这不算什么。”

En: You mean considering the fact that the world is collapsing on my porch? She gives you a rueful smile and takes a look around...
Zh: “你的意思是，考虑到世界正在我的走廊上崩溃的事实？”她朝你露出一个悲哀的笑容，然后看了看周围……

En: Just Martinaise? She gives you a rueful smile and takes a look around...
Zh: “只有马丁内斯？”她朝你露出一个悲哀的笑容，然后看了看周围……

En: In any case, thank you for stopping by. It's good to have an answer, even if I can't claim to understand it fully.
Zh: “无论如何，谢谢你能来拜访我。找到答案是好事，虽然我不能说自己已经完全理解了。”

En: A 'curse' is something superstitious, but a two-millimetre hole in reality? She gives you a rueful smile. "We all know what it means -- it's pale."
Zh: “‘诅咒’是迷信的东西，但是现实里有2毫米的小洞？”她朝你露出一个悲哀的笑容。“我们都知道这意味着什么——那是灰域。”

En: Wait... how do you explain what happened to all those companies then?
Zh: “等等……那你怎么解释那些公司发生的事情？”

En: It's just capitalism. She shrugs. "We only hear about tales of success, so it's often surprising to realize how many ventures actually fail."
Zh: “这就是资本主义。”她耸耸肩。“我们只听说过成功故事，所以经常会很吃惊地意识到：实际上到底有多少企业破产。”

En: Hold on, the Whirling is part of the Doomed Commercial Area?!
Zh: “等等，飞旋旅社也是诅咒商业区的一部分吗？”

En: You could say so. Both houses were built at the same time and under the East Delta Commerce Centre project.
Zh: “可以这么说。两栋房子都是同一时期建成的，而且都属于东德尔塔商业中心项目。”

En: It's because she's in cahoots with the demons!
Zh: 那是因为她跟恶魔有勾结！

En: Maybe it's just because she's so talented that she's been able to woo the curse?
Zh: 也许只是因为她才智过人，所以能够成功逃过诅咒？

En: Plaisance thinks it's because you're the *source* of it. A malignant entity.
Zh: “普莱桑斯觉得因为你就是诅咒的*根源*。是那个邪恶的存在。”

En: It's because you're competent, and dedicated to your craft. The curse doesn't affect people like you.
Zh: “是因为你能力出众，而且专注于自己的手艺。诅咒不会影响你这样的人。”

En: The curse will get to you, have no doubt about it. It just doesn't care about earthly time frames.
Zh: “毫无疑问，诅咒也会降临到你头上。它只是不太关心地球的时间范围。”

En: I'll be the first to admit there are many inconsistencies in this so-called 'curse'.
Zh: “我会成为第一个承认所谓的‘诅咒’有很多矛盾之处的人。”

En: The dicemaker erupts in laughter. "Is that what you think, officer? That the curse is real?"
Zh: 骰子匠人突然大笑起来。“你是这么想的吗，警官？那个诅咒是真的？”

En: Malignant *entity*, what does that even mean? The dicemaker laughs. "Some kind of sorceress? What about you, officer? Do you think I'm the *malignant entity*?"
Zh: “邪恶*存在*，那到底是什么意思啊？”骰子匠人笑了。“某种女巫吗？你怎么看，警官？你觉得我是那个*邪恶存在*吗？”

En: The dicemaker erupts in laughter. "What, so the curse only affects people with poor work ethics? What you're describing isn't a *curse*, it's *capitalism*."
Zh: 骰子匠人大笑出声。“什么，所以诅咒只会应验在那些职业道德很差的人身上吗？你说的那个不是*诅咒*，那叫*资本主义*。”

En: I was just about to ask... She leans forward in her chair to study your face. "What do *you* think? Do you think the curse is real?"
Zh: “我刚想问……”坐在椅子上的她向前倾身，打量着你的脸。“*你*怎么想的？你觉得诅咒是真实存在的吗？”

En: The jig is up! The she-demon knows you've uncovered her true identity.
Zh: 完蛋了！那个女恶魔知道你揭露了她的真实身份。

En: Honestly, I'm still not sure... This world is a puzzling place.
Zh: “老实说，我还是不太确定……这个世界本身就是个让人困惑的地方。”

En: I think it still might be *you* that's causing this. (Narrow your eyes -- very mysteriously.)
Zh: “我觉得可能是*你*造成的。”（眯起眼睛——神秘兮兮的）

En: I'm starting to see that there is no curse, only business decisions and natural market fluctuations.
Zh: “我开始觉得并没有什么诅咒，只是商业决策和自然的市场波动。”

En: Exactly. She pinches the root of her nose. "Truth is always so disappointingly mundane and boring."
Zh: “没错。”她捏了捏鼻根。“真相永远都是令人失望的，平平无奇，了无生趣。”

En: But I'm glad we got this sorted out. Anything else I can help you with today?
Zh: “但是很高兴我们能把整件事搞清楚。今天还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Is it now? She squints at you from her desk. "I've always thought it's a rather mundane and boring place with no supra-natural surprises in sight."
Zh: “现在变成这样了吗？”桌子后的她眯起眼睛看着你。“我一直觉得这是个相当平凡又无聊的地方，没有什么看得见的超自然力量带来的惊喜。”

En: Well, if you ever find a way to explain all those *inconsistencies* in the curse, let me know. She turns back to her work.
Zh: “呃，如果你能解释那些诅咒中的*矛盾*之处，记得告诉我。”她转回去工作了。

En: Mm, so I'm the grand dragon in the cave... She seems mildly entertained by this suggestion. "Might I ask what supports this claim?"
Zh: “嗯，所以我是盘踞在洞穴里的巨龙……”你的暗示似乎给她带来了些许的乐趣。“那我能问下，有什么东西能支持这种说法吗？”

En: There is something ghostly about you, *ethereal* even... (Squint your eyes suspiciously.)
Zh: “你身上有一种幽灵般的感觉，甚至可以说是*虚无缥缈*……”（怀疑地眯起双眼。）

En: I don't have time for explanations, not amidst all those *visions* I'm getting.
Zh: “我没时间解释，特别是我还身处于*幻觉*之中的时候。”

En: You're the last man standing. You must be responsible.
Zh: “你是最后一个没有倒下的人。你肯定要负责。”

En: No, it's the troubled colour of her energetic vibrations!
Zh: 不，这是她积极共鸣显露出的不安色彩！

En: No, I mean it -- your energetic vibrations are off. I sense tragedies in your past, darling.
Zh: “不，我是认真的——你的积极共鸣关闭了。我能感觉到你过去的悲剧，小宝贝。”

En: *Ethereal*? She throws her head back and laughs. "Thank you, but I think it's just the lighting."
Zh: “*虚无缥缈*？”她后仰着头，笑了。“谢谢，不过我觉得那只是灯光。”

En: Let it go.
Zh: 随它去吧。

En: Of course you do. She listens to your antics with a forbearing smile on her face. "Show me a person in Martinaise who *doesn't* have any tragedies in their past and then we'll talk."
Zh: “当然是了。”她看着你滑稽的举动，脸上带着宽容的微笑。“跟我说一个在马丁内斯*没有*悲惨过去的人，然后我们再聊。”

En: I *am* responsible for making sound business decisions, yes, but not for my fellow tenants' misfortune.
Zh: “我*会*对做出合理的商业决策负责，是的，其他那些租客的不幸可不赖我。”

En: Of course. She pinches the root of her nose. "How convenient."
Zh: “当然。”她捏了捏鼻根。“这样多方便呢。”

En: Well, if you ever find a way to explain all those *inconsistencies* in the curse then let me know. She turns back to her work.
Zh: “呃，如果你能解释那些诅咒中的*矛盾*之处，记得告诉我。”她转回去工作了。

En: Oh my, she says in mock despair. "I've revealed myself!"
Zh: “我的天，”她假装绝望地说到。“我暴露自己的身份了！”

En: You better call the exorcists.
Zh: “你最好找驱魔人过来。”

En: I believe the name you're looking for is the Remote Viewers Division.
Zh: “我相信你要找的名字是遥视者部队。”

En: I don't have to call anyone, I'm a ghostwhisperer myself.
Zh: “我不用叫任何人，我自己就是鬼语者。”

En: It is not my job to intervene in matters supra-natural, for I am merely a police officer.
Zh: “我的工作不是插手超自然事件，因为我只是一个警官。”

En: What's that? A union for clairvoyants? They have unions for everything nowadays.
Zh: “那是什么？预言者联盟吗？现在什么都有联盟。”

En: No, it's a special division in the police for investigating supra-natural activities.
Zh: “不，那是警察系统的一个特殊部门，专门调查超自然活动的。”

En: She just stares at you for a moment with an astonished look on her face, blinking her dark eyes.
Zh: 她吃惊地盯了你一会儿，眨巴着黑色的眼睛。

En: It's not real, ma'am. He's making it up.
Zh: “这不是真的，女士。他瞎编的。”

En: Yes, I figured. She turns back to you.
Zh: “是啊，我想也是。”她转向你。

En: This is not going very well, is it -- word wise?
Zh: 事情进展的不太顺利，不是吗——机灵鬼？

En: Who cares about the dead body? We might be dealing with a *malignant entity* here!
Zh: 谁在乎尸体啊？我们这里可能正在对付一个*邪恶存在*啊！

En: The lieutenant looks at his notebook, then the woman under the large window...
Zh: 警督看了看自己的笔记，然后看向大窗户下的那个女人……

En: Your window looks directly onto the courtyard. You're saying you didn't see or hear *anything* unusual last Sunday evening?
Zh: “你的窗户能直接看到后院。你说上周日晚上，自己没有看见或者听见*任何*不寻常的动静吗？”

En: I'm sorry, detective, but -- as you know -- I usually have my headphones on when I'm working. It shuts out most of the daily ruckus behind my window.
Zh: “很抱歉，警探，但是——你也知道的——我工作的时候一般都带着耳机。它隔绝了窗户背后每天绝大部分的喧闹声音。”

En: Seriously? You've got a great view of the courtyard and you didn't see or hear *anything* Sunday night?
Zh: “真的吗？你这里能把后院看得很清楚，但是你却说上周日晚上*什么*也没看见，没听见？”

En: Well, that settles that, then.
Zh: “呃，那就算了吧。”

En: What do you mean by the 'daily ruckus'?
Zh: “你说‘每天喧闹的声音’是什么意思？”

En: And you never took your eyes off the work to look out of the window?
Zh: “难道你从来没有把注意力从工作上移开，偶尔看看窗外吗？”

En: I see. Thank you for your answers. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我明白了。多谢你的回答。”（总结。）

En: I might have, she admits, "but in this case all I would have seen is my own reflection staring back from the darkness."
Zh: “也许有过，”她承认到，“但是在这种情况下，我唯一能看见的，只有自己的倒影在黑暗中回望着我。”

En: It's really hard to make anything out in the yard when it's dark outside. Besides... I rarely get up to look out the window when I'm in the zone.
Zh: “外面很黑的时候，真的很难辨认出院子里的东西。再说……我专注的时候，几乎不会起身去看窗外。”

En: Well, there's always something going on in the Whirling's backyard... She stops to try and come up with an example.
Zh: “呃，飞旋旅社的后院总是琐事不断……”她停顿下来，试着想出个例子。

En: During daytime there are usually those kids... And lately I've been seeing a lot of drunk workers hanging about. Must be because of the strike.
Zh: “白天一般都是那些小孩……最近我看见很多醉酒的工人在瞎晃。一定是因为罢工的事吧。”

En: She looks up at the window... Pale light comes in. "But I never saw anyone during that fateful Sunday night, I'm afraid."
Zh: 她抬头看向窗户……苍白的灯光照射进来。“但是在那个宿命的周日夜晚，恐怕我谁也没看见。”

En: She nods.
Zh: 她点点头。

En: It's a shame about those windows. I'm not even going to ask about that milieu...
Zh: “窗户的事真是太可惜了。我甚至都不打算问你电台的事了……”

En: They seemed to believe they were historical individuals on some *grand* quest.
Zh: “他们似乎相信自己是历史人物，肩负着某种*重大的*历史使命。”

En: From what I've seen so far the project *did* look quite impressive...
Zh: “以我目前所见，这个项目*确实*挺让人印象深刻的……”

En: Really? They must have been on a gigantic ego trip.
Zh: “真的吗？他们一定是踏上了无限自我膨胀的旅途。”

En: Yes, but when the money started to run out they just began to complain a lot about capitalism. You know, how the markets are *rigged* to keep out new businesses, and so on.
Zh: “是的，当资金所剩无几的时候，他们只是开始抱怨资本主义。你懂得，就是市场被*操纵*了，要排挤新企业之类的，等等等等。”

En: Indeed, what were the other ideas?
Zh: 确实，还有什么其他想法吗？

En: Oh, right! She rubs her forehead; her scarf has left a faint line on her dusky skin. "I hope you didn't try to ring me."
Zh: “哦，对了！”她揉了揉前额；头巾在她黯淡的皮肤上留下一道浅浅的印子。“希望你没按过我的门铃。”

En: My advice... She raises her finger. "Don't base your business on a fad -- hypnotism, floriography, triktrak, especially *pinball*."
Zh: “我的建议……”她竖起手指。“不要把你的生意建立在一时的狂热上——催眠术，花语，双陆棋，特别是*弹球*。”

En: You could say so. Both houses were built at the same time and under the East Delta Commerce Centre project. That explains why you can call the Whirling from the intercom, albeit I doubt that anyone responds.
Zh: “可以这么说。两栋房子都是同一时期建成的，而且都属于东德尔塔商业中心项目。这就解释了你为什么能用对讲机呼叫飞旋旅社，尽管我怀疑不会有人回应。”

En: I have a few more questions about the building.
Zh: “关于这栋建筑，我还有些问题。”

En: Sure, I'm listening.
Zh: “当然了，我在听。”

En: Actually... the Slipstream SCA mystery might be a *recording*. I called again later and got exactly the same message.
Zh: “其实……气流SCA的秘密可能是一个*录音*。我稍后再播一次，看看是不是完全相同的信息。”

En: You're right, I probably just got made fun of.
Zh: “你说得对，也许我只是被人整了。”

En: It may have been some sort of... rare electrical anomaly.
Zh: “这可能是某种……罕见的电异常事故。”

En: The kids these days... She shakes her head. "We were just one of them and now they're terrorizing us. No solidarity."
Zh: “现在的小孩啊……”她摇摇头。“我们只是他们中的一员，现在他们反倒恐吓起我们来了。一点都不团结。”

En: A prank is more likely, no? The kids these days... She shakes her head. "We were just one of them and now they're terrorizing us. No solidarity."
Zh: “更有可能是恶作剧，不是吗？现在的小孩啊……”她摇摇头。“我们只是他们中的一员，现在他们反倒恐吓起我们来了。一点都不团结。”

En: I'm still convinced it was nothing more than some elaborate prank.
Zh: “我还是相信这不过是个精心安排的恶作剧。”

En: I have more questions about the intercom.
Zh: “关于这个对讲机，我还有些问题。”

En: I'm pretty sure it still doesn't work, but sure, what do you want to know?
Zh: “我肯定它还是不能用，不过当然了，你还有什么想知道的吗？”

En: Oh, this one's a mess. She sighs. "There used to be a company that promised to repair windows 24 hours a day. What could go wrong with this one, right?"
Zh: “哦，这个真是一塌糊涂。”她叹了口气。“曾经有一家公司，承诺会每天24小时帮人维修窗户。这个会出什么问题呢，是吧？”

En: Time has come to face the Source. Fear not, for the forces of the universe are supporting you in this psychic quest.
Zh: 是时候面对源头了。不要害怕，因为宇宙的力量会支持你完成这次的超自然任务。

En: But it doesn't: this is merely a sarcastic response.
Zh: 但是并没有：这只不过是一个讽刺的回应。

En: I think none of those doorbells work, including mine. I'm still in the middle of connecting the wires -- sorry about the confusion.
Zh: “我觉得没有一个门铃是好用的，包括我的。我还在连接线路呢——抱歉给你添麻烦了。”

En: If you ever find a way to explain all those *inconsistencies* in the curse then let me know. She turns back to her work.
Zh: “呃，如果你能解释那些诅咒中的*矛盾*之处，记得告诉我。”她转回去工作了。

En: Another seagull passes by... It's getting cold standing here, staring at the silent call box.
Zh: 又一只海鸥飞过……站在这里越来越冷，你凝视着沉默的电话亭。

En: It must have been some kind of... faulty wiring. We should maybe stop playing with this doorbell, it looks ancient.
Zh: “这肯定是某种……接线故障。我们还是不要摆弄门铃了，它看起来挺古老的。”

En: Here come the bad vibes again. Relax. Distance yourself from it.
Zh: 糟糕的感应又来了。放松。让自己远离它。

En: FOR THREE HUNDRED YEARS I HAVE BEEN HERE. VOLATILE AND LUMINOUS. MADE OF SODIUM AND RAIN.
Zh: 我在这里待了整整三百年。反复无常，清晰明亮。钠和雨水构成了我。

En: IF YOU WANT ME YOU CAN FIND ME ON THE BEACH, DANS LE DERNIER BAL.
Zh: 如果你想要我的话，就来海滩找我，在最后的那场舞会上。

En: Her words are too cold to comprehend. She smells of sodium lights and rain streaks on car windows. Eyes like pilot lights watch your shape in dark hallways, guttering.
Zh: 她的话语太过冰冷，让人无法领会。她闻起来是钠灯和車窗上雨滴的味道。她的眼睛像指示灯，注视着黑暗走廊里你的身影，忽明忽暗。

En: So... The strange, alien thought pattern ends. The lieutenant cuts in, inspecting the intercom.
Zh: “所以……”那种奇怪又异常的思维模式结束了。警督插了进来，检查着对讲机。

En: It was a recording trapped in the circuitry. From some ancient tenant. This sometimes happens. Shall we conclude here? We have other mysteries to solve.
Zh: “这是一段卡在电路系统里的电话录音。应该是很久以前某个租客留下的。这种事情偶尔会发生。这里完事了吗？我们还有其他谜题要破解呢。”

En: Something *weird* just happened to me.
Zh: “我身上刚刚发生了一些很*奇怪*的事情。”

En: What is happening to me?
Zh: “我这是怎么了？”

En: (Just hang up the call.)
Zh: （挂断通话。）

En: Don't take this the wrong way, but -- during our short stint working together -- *something weird* is almost always happening to you.
Zh: “不要误会，但是——在我们这段短暂的合作期间——你身上总是会发生*奇怪*的事情。”

En: That *is* true.
Zh: 这*倒是*真的。

En: The strange and alien thought pattern ends. You're left staring at the electronic doorbell.
Zh: 那种奇怪又异常的思维模式结束了。你留在原地，盯着电子门铃。

En: I have no idea. I just know that my room's window is broken and needs to be dealt with.
Zh: “我不知道。我只知道我房间的窗户破了，需要处理一下。”

En: I see. The lieutenant doesn't ask more.
Zh: “我明白了。”警督不再问了。

En: Hmm... this button looks new. I wouldn't be surprised if it hasn't been connected yet. Is it the dicemaker's? He takes a step back, inspecting the other names on the list.
Zh: “嗯……按钮看起来挺新的。如果还没接好的话，我一点也不会觉得惊讶。这是骰子匠人的吗？”他退后一步，检查着清单上的其他名字。

En: Wait, is she...?
Zh: 等等，难道她……？

En: Wait, a recording trapped in the circuitry?
Zh: “等等，你是说一段录音被困在电路里了？”

En: Mhmh. I don't have time to explain it to you right now. Maybe some time later... He looks at the sea, almost wistful.
Zh: “嗯。我现在没时间跟你解释。稍后再说吧……”他看向大海，几乎可以说充满渴望。

En: And then it hits you.
Zh: 然后，你突然明白了。

En: Just a recording... and here I thought...
Zh: “只是一个录音……我还以为……”

En: An old call box with a matrix of push buttons lists all the companies in the East Delta Commerce Centre.
Zh: 一个老旧的公共电话亭，上面有一个按钮矩阵，列出了东德尔塔商业中心的所有公司。

En: Main Hall, Building A.
Zh: “大堂，A栋。”

En: 24h Window.
Zh: “24小时窗户”

En: Emma's Fashion Atelier.
Zh: “艾玛时装工作室”

En: REVACHOL ICE CITY.
Zh: “瑞瓦肖冰城”

En: Main hall, building B: WHIRLING-IN-RAGS.
Zh: “大堂，B栋：褴褛飞旋”

En: East Delta Pinball (Entrance from building B).
Zh: “东德尔塔弹球（请从B栋进入）”

En: ARTEMITEP's Boxing for... (The rest is destroyed.)
Zh: “阿特米普拳击……”（剩下的部分已经被破坏掉了。）

En: ARTEMITEP's Boxing for Young Athletes & Gym.
Zh: “阿特米普青年运动员拳击&体操”

En: Fabron's Taxi... (The rest has been burned off.)
Zh: “法布龙出租車……”（剩下的部分被烧掉了。）

En: Slipstream SCA.
Zh: “气流SCA”

En: Fortress Accident SCA.
Zh: “要塞事故SCA”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: All you hear is static, but no one answers the call.
Zh: 你只能听到电流的声音，但是没人接电话。

En: Silence. No one's home at Fortress Accident.
Zh: 沉默。要塞事故没人在。

En: You wait for a minute or two, but all you get from the call box is silence -- no one answers the call.
Zh: 你等了一两分钟，但从电话亭里只得到了沉默——没人接电话。

En: An off-key melody starts playing after you ring the doorbell... Then a woman picks up the receiver...
Zh: 你按下门铃，一阵走调的旋律开始响起……然后一个女人拿起了听筒……

En: Cuno! Please stop calling here! Grown-ups don't have time for your stupid games.
Zh: “坤诺！别再打过来了！成年人没时间陪你玩那些愚蠢的游戏。”

En: You ring the doorbell, but nothing happens.
Zh: 你按响门铃，但是什么也没发生。

En: Looks like someone has melted half the plastic off with a lighter -- the doorbell doesn't work anymore.
Zh: 看来有人用打火机熔掉了半边塑料——门铃没用了。

En: Andro-Orlando Hair SCA.
Zh: “安德罗·奥兰多发型SCA”

En: Silence. No one answers the doorbell.
Zh: 沉默。按了门铃但是没人应答。

En: Silence. No one answers your call.
Zh: 沉默。没人回应。

En: Nothing happens after you ring the doorbell... They don't want to talk to you.
Zh: 你按响门铃，但是什么也没发生……他们不想跟你说话。

En: (Empty card.)
Zh: （空白卡片）

En: This button looks new, but someone has removed the name card. Nothing happens when you try to ring it.
Zh: 按钮看起来很新，但是有人拿走了名牌。当你试着按响它的时候，什么也没发生。

En: You ring the doorbell, but no one answers.
Zh: 你按响门铃，但是没有人应答。

En: Pardon? This is not Cuno. Who *is* this?
Zh: “请原谅？你不是坤诺。请问你*是谁*？”

En: She thinks you're the gremlin child! What would he say to this?
Zh: 她觉得你是那个熊孩子！不知道他会怎么说？

En: You're going to just stand there and *take it* from a doorbell?
Zh: 你就打算站在那里，*承受*一个门铃吗？

En: No way. I should beat the spark into it. (Punch the doorbell.)
Zh: 不可能。我应该给它打出点火花。（用拳头去捶打门铃。）

En: No, I'll show you how a gentleman treats a doorbell! (Kiss the doorbell.)
Zh: 不，我会让你看看，一个绅士是怎么对待门铃的！（亲吻门铃。）

En: I should just leave the doorbell alone.
Zh: 我应该不去管那个门铃。

En: The doorbell agrees. The entire call box looks relieved.
Zh: 门铃表示赞同。整个电话亭看起来都放松了不少。

En: You're now kissing the doorbell. The corroded metal of the intercom is cold, unattainable; not un-familiar. The doorbell remains silent.
Zh: 你亲吻了门铃。对讲机冰冷的金属已经生锈，难以触碰，却有种莫名的熟悉。门铃依然没有发出任何声音。

En: Like a cat's whiskers, your moustache feels it first, followed by your lips: the corroded metal of the intercom, cold, unattainable. Now you're kissing the doorbell. The doorbell remains silent.
Zh: 就像猫的胡须一般，你的胡子先感受了一下，然后是嘴唇：对讲机那锈蚀的金属，冰冷而又高不可攀。你正在亲吻一个门铃，但它却依然沉默不语。

En: Your fist crashes into the metal, pure physical vengeance. It feels good, at first, and then it starts to hurt.
Zh: 你的拳头砸进金属里，纯粹的物理性报复。一开始感觉很好，然后就开始疼了。

En: Khm... says the lieutenant. "That's unsanitary." (It probably is.)
Zh: “呃……”警督说到。“这样有点不卫生。”（有这个可能。）

En: You just keep on beating those things, don't you?
Zh: “你就打算继续这样砸下去，是吗？”

En: When was the last time you kissed?
Zh: 你上次亲吻是什么时候？

En: Congratulations, you've destroyed Artemitep's Boxing Club's doorbell. It's not working anymore.
Zh: 恭喜，你毁掉了阿特米普拳击俱乐部的门铃。现在它没用了。

En: Who's Cuno? This is the police, please open the door.
Zh: “坤诺是谁？我是警察，请把门打开。”

En: This is the police, please open the door.
Zh: “我是警察，请把门打开。”

En: Oh, I'm sorry, officer, I thought you were... There's a spot of static that overrides her words.
Zh: “哦，抱歉，警官，我还以为你是……”一阵微弱的电流干扰了她的话语。

En: ...but the doorbell is broken and the bookstore shouldn't even be on the list anymore, so I can't help you. Please don't call here again. Thanks.
Zh: “……但是门铃坏了，书店甚至都不在清单上，所以我帮不了你。请别再打来了。谢谢。”

En: Please don't do that! Doorbells are not toys, and this one isn't even working properly. Please don't call us again, thank you.
Zh: “请不要这么做！门铃不是玩具，而且它甚至都不能正常工作。请别再打过来，谢谢。”

En: A single beep indicates that the line has gone dead.
Zh: 你听见一个哔声，这代表线路已经挂断。

En: Hmm... this button looks new. It's probably not connected yet. He takes a step back, inspecting the other names on the list.
Zh: “嗯……按钮看起来挺新的。可能还没接好。”他退后一步，检查着清单上的其他名字。

En: This time a pleasant female voice answers: "Thank you for calling 24h Window Company. This is an automated message. Are you experiencing a window-related emergency? During non-business-hours, please call 0-0-7-2-5-4-7-7-6-5-1. The streets are mean, but we're *always* there for you."
Zh: 这次是一个和蔼可亲的女声：“感谢您拨打24小时窗户公司。这是一条语音消息。您是否遇到了与窗户有关的紧急状况？非工作时间，请拨打0-0-7-2-5-4-7-7-6-5-1。街道无情人有情，我们会*永远*陪伴在你左右。”

En: Kim, do we have an emergency?
Zh: “金，我们遇到紧急状况了吗？”

En: No emergencies, thank you. (Look at other buttons again.)
Zh: “没有紧急状况，谢谢。”（再次看向其他按钮。）

En: No, not a window-related one.
Zh: “不，跟窗户没有关系。”

En: Twelve name cards on the call box read:
Zh: 电话亭上有12张名牌，写着：

En: Kim, what's Revachol Ice City?
Zh: “金，瑞瓦肖冰城是什么？”

En: I don't care what it is.
Zh: 我不在乎它是什么。

En: No idea, he shrugs. The three-worded business name stands cryptic among the others:
Zh: “不知道，”他耸耸肩。相比其他名字来说，这个由三个词组成的公司名称特别模糊：

En: (Try to impersonate Cuno.) "Fuck off, pig, I'm Cuno! I don't give a shit, let me in."
Zh: （试着模仿坤诺。）“滚开，猪头，我是坤诺！我他妈才不管呢，快放我进去。”

En: What an ominous name for a hair salon... Doesn't bode well for anyone's hair.
Zh: 对于一家美发店来说，这名字还真是有些不太吉利……对于任何人的头发来说，都不是什么好兆头。

En: Just seeing the words "Andro-Orlando" gets your hackles up. It's very existence is a *threat* to your masculinity, to say nothing of your hair.
Zh: 只是看到“安德罗·奥兰多”这个名字就让你怒气冲冲。对于你的男子汉气概来说，它的存在就是一个*威胁*，更别提你的头发了。

En: Try again, you didn't press it hard enough.
Zh: 再试一次，你按的不够用力。

En: Press again.
Zh: 再按一次。

En: Ignore it.
Zh: 忽略它。

En: That's only half-true. The streets *are* mean, but they're not there for you.
Zh: 这只说对了一半。这些街道*确实*很无情，但他们也不会陪伴在你左右。

En: She sounds almost antique, as if her voice was being played off an old wax cylinder. Her receiver must not be working properly.
Zh: 她的声音很古老，就像是用旧蜡制圆筒播出来的一样。她的听筒一定是坏了。

En: They have failed.
Zh: 他们失败了。

En: Yes, I am the destroyer.
Zh: “是的，我就是毁灭者。”

En: Say nothing. You've already taught the doorbell a lesson.
Zh: 什么也不说。你已经给门铃好好上了一课。

En: The lieutenant sighs but says nothing. He looks up at the building.
Zh: 警督叹了口气，但什么也没说。他抬头看着这栋大楼。

En: No, I have explicitly stated that I am not the destroyer.
Zh: “不，我已经明确声明过，我不是毁灭者。”

En: Wait! But what happened to Slipstream SCA? There's no Tricentennial Electrics on the list.
Zh: 等等！那气流SCA出什么事了？清单上没有三生电气。

En: Hold on, Tricentennial Electrics? I thought I was calling Slipstream SCA.
Zh: “等等，三生电气？我还以为自己打给气流SCA了。”

En: Yes, hello, can you let me inside the building please?
Zh: “是的，你好，请问能让我进来吗？”

En: Sorry, I don't actually have any business here.
Zh: “抱歉，其实我在这里没什么事做。”

En: Slipstream SCA.
Zh: “气流SCA”

En: Wax cylinder.
Zh: 蜡制圆筒。

En: Slipstream SCA.
Zh: 气流SCA。

En: Radio ghost.
Zh: 无线电幽灵。

En: Guided by the Cuno.
Zh: 坤诺引导。

En: Punched it.
Zh: 砸它。

En: Kissed it.
Zh: 亲吻它。

En: No, hold on. The last thing you need in your life is more hysteric emotions. Forget it. Find something else to do.
Zh: 不，等等。你生活中最不需要的就是更歇斯底里的情绪。忘了它吧。找点别的事情做。

En: Actually, I *do have* a window-related emergency. (Look at Whirling's second floor.)
Zh: “其实，我*确实*遇到了跟窗户有关的紧急情况。”（看向飞旋旅社二楼。）

En: Yes, I've been meaning to ask about that... What happened?
Zh: “没错，我一直想问这个……发生什么事了？”

En: Some crook threw a shoe into my window.
Zh: “有个混账朝我的窗户扔了一只鞋。”

En: I threw my shoe out the window.
Zh: “我把鞋子扔到窗外了。”

En: Some crook threw a shoe into my window -- and forced a bottle of Commodore Red on me.
Zh: “有个混账朝我的窗户扔了一只鞋——然后还强迫我喝了一瓶海军准将红朗姆。”

En: In any case, I'm afraid you won't get any help here. He looks up to the towering ghost house. "This place is deserted."
Zh: “总之，恐怕你不会在这里得到任何帮助。”他抬头望着幽灵般高高耸立的房子。“这地方已经荒废了。”

En: A SHOE WAS DEFENESTRATED!
Zh: “一只鞋被扔出窗外了！”

En: This was no mere act of vandalism -- this was a defenestration!
Zh: 这不仅仅是故意毁坏他人财物的行为——这是直接抛出窗外了啊！

En: They don't want to talk to you.
Zh: 他们不想跟你说话。

En: Plaisance, it's me! Please open the door, I'm trying to get in.
Zh: “普莱桑斯，是我！请开门，我想进去。”

En: Sorry, I was just trying random doorbells, I didn't have anything important.
Zh: “抱歉，我只是随便按了下门铃，没什么重要的事情。”

En: Please just stop calling here, I don't have time to deal with this. Thank you.
Zh: “请不要再打过来了，我没时间管这个。谢谢。”

En: There's no one there to answer your call. You are beyond help.
Zh: 没人回应。你已经无能为力了。

En: You hear static from the intercom speaker. It sounds as if someone has picked up the receiver, but isn't saying anything.
Zh: 你听见对讲机扬声器发出的电流声。感觉像是有人拿起了听筒，但是什么也没说。

En: Hello? Is anyone there?
Zh: “喂？有人在吗？”

En: Hang up the call.
Zh: 挂断。

En: You can almost hear them breathe.
Zh: 你几乎能听见他们的呼吸声。

En: My god...
Zh: “我的天……”

En: I see. There is an impenetrable expression on the lieutenant's face.
Zh: “我明白了。”警督脸上露出令人费解的表情。

En: This is the police, I need you to let me in because I heard there's a fridge in the cellar.
Zh: “我是警察，听说地下室有个冰箱，我需要你放我进去。”

En: You hear the same waiting room music, but no one picks up the receiver anymore...
Zh: 你听见相同的等候室音乐，但是再也没有人拿起听筒了……

En: Yes, hello, this is Tricentennial Electrics. This is a woman's voice, crackling and fragile through the static.
Zh: “是的，你好，这里是三生电气。”一个女人的声音通过电流传了出来，噼里啪啦的，显得很脆弱。

En: Have you come to place an order?
Zh: “您是打来订购的吗？”

En: Who is this? I've been inside this building, but I didn't see anyone from Tricentennial Electrics. This place is deserted.
Zh: “你是谁？我已经进来了，但是没有看见任何三生电气的人。这地方已经荒废了。”

En: (Lie.) "Yes, I've come to place an order."
Zh: （撒谎。）“没错，我是来下订单的。”

En: Sorry?
Zh: “怎么了？”

En: It's you. My god, I didn't think I would hear your voice again...
Zh: “是你。我的天，我还以为再也听不到你的声音了……”

En: It's a woman and she knows you! Your heart beats faster.
Zh: 是一个女人，而且她认识你！你的心跳开始加速。

En: She must be mistaking you for someone else.
Zh: 她一定是把你跟其他人搞混了。

En: Who cares that you don't remember her? Just go along with it.
Zh: 谁在乎你记不记得她？随它去吧。

En: No, something's wrong here... are you sure she's talking to *you*?
Zh: 不，感觉有些不对……你确定她是在跟*你*说话吗？

En: Do we know each other?
Zh: “我们认识吗？”

En: Who are you? *Where* are you?
Zh: “你是谁？你在*哪*？”

En: Khm-khm... (Stand up straighter.) "Yeah, it's *me*. Here I am again, crawling back into your life."
Zh: “咳咳……”（挺直腰板。）“没错，是*我*。我又来了，卑微地重新回到你的生活。”

En: Michel, just please... She stops and you can hear her breathe heavily. Her breath distorted by ancient static.
Zh: “米歇尔，拜托……”她停了下来，你能听见她沉重的呼吸声——因为古老的电流显得有些失真。

En: Sounds like a ghost. Wind blows through your clothes and you feel detached from your surroundings. Inside the building -- a cold memory hangs.
Zh: 听起来像是一个幽灵。风吹起你的衣衫，你感觉与周围的环境逐渐分离。在这栋建筑的某个地方——有一段冰冷的记忆挥之不去。

En: Why did you even call? I don't understand... You've been gone for months. She sounds like she's shaking. "I thought you didn't care."
Zh: “你为什么要打过来？我不明白……你已经走了好几个月。”她听起来像是在颤抖。“我还以为你一点都不在乎。”

En: Hold on, tell me what's going on. What did I do?
Zh: “等等，告诉我发生了什么。我做了什么？”

En: She sounds like she's about to cry.
Zh: 她听上去像是要哭出来了。

En: Ever since I came to work here it's been different... as if my mind's been wiped clean...
Zh: “自从我来这里工作以后，一切都不同了……就好像我的脑袋被清空了一样……”

En: Forget about what?
Zh: “忘记什么？”

En: Listen, I'm going to hang up now.
Zh: “听着，我要挂断了。”

En: Michel? My name's not Michel. I'm Harry.
Zh: “米歇尔？我不叫米歇尔。我是哈里。”

En: Hello?
Hang up the call.
Zh: 挂断通话。

En: She doesn't answer.
Zh: 她没有回答。

En: The cold is deep under your skin, as if you were talking to someone who's a hundred years away. Somewhere inside the building water is flooding the cellar floor.
Zh: 寒冷深深地埋藏在你的皮肤底下，你感觉自己像是在跟一个100多年前的人说话。就在这栋建筑的某个地方，水淹没了地下室的地板。

En: Oh god, please don't cry.
Zh: “天啦，请不要哭。”

En: You press the number sign on the keypad that terminates the call. Twelve name cards on the call box read:
Zh: 你按下键盘上的井号，终止了这次通话。电话亭上有12张名牌，写着：

En: The lieutenant exchanges a look with you.
Zh: 警督跟你交换了一下眼色。

En: Hey, are you still there?
Zh: “喂，你还在吗？”

En: You said it was nice. What's so nice about forgetting?
Zh: “你说这样很好，但是忘记到底有什么好的？”

En: Silence. The only thing you can hear now is static and waves, washing ashore on the bay.
Zh: 沉默。现在你唯一能听到的只有电流声，还有海浪拍打岸边的声音。

En: Alright, it's a good-bye then. (Hang up the call.)
Zh: “好吧，那就再见了。”(挂断通话。)

En: Plaisance, it's me! I'm investigating the Doomed Commercial Area, just like we discussed!
Zh: “普莱桑斯，是我！我正在调查诅咒商业区，我们之前讨论过的！”

En: Of course I care. It's just that I've been going through some tough time.
Zh: “我当然在乎了。我只是经历了一段比较困难的时间。”

En: A spot of static overrides her words; when she speaks again it sounds like she's submerged:
Zh: 一阵电流声盖住了她的话语；当她再次开口的时候，听起来她就像是在水下一样：

En: ...It's so nice.
Zh: “……这样真的很好。”

En: What just happened?
Zh: “刚才出什么事了？”

En: Fuck! (Hang up the call.)
Zh: “该死！”(挂断通话。)

En: I get it, you don't want to talk to me. No one *ever* wants to talk to me.
Zh: “我明白的，你不想跟我说话。*从来*就没人想跟我说话。”

En: Another seagull passes by... It's getting cold standing here, staring at the silent call box.
Zh: 又一只海鸥飞过……站在这里越来越冷，你凝视着沉默的电话亭。

En: I don't know what happened either. The lieutenant inspects the doorbell. "We should probably stop playing with this thing."
Zh: “我也不知道是怎么回事。”警督检查着门铃。“也许我们应该别碰这玩意了。”

En: Whatever she says, it can't hurt you. You're a different person now. Stronger, healthier and...
Zh: 不管她说了什么，都伤不到你。现在的你已经不同往日。更强壮，更健康，而且……

En: Alright, maybe not *healthier*, but it's a *bonus* that you've drunk so hard you can't remember any of your past relationships. Oblivion's the ace in your corner.
Zh: 好吧，也许没有*更健康*，但是喝太多导致记不起过去任何一段感情，其实是一种*奖励*。遗忘是你手中的王牌。

En: Call her again. (Press the doorbell to Slipstream SCA.)
Zh: 再打给她。（按下气流SCA的门铃）

En: There's a light buzz as you press the doorbell, waiting for her to answer the call. It's cold outside, and you can hear the wind blowing into the speaker.
Zh: 你按下门铃，一阵嗡嗡声响起，你在等待她的回应。外面很冷，你可以听见风吹进扬声器的声音。

En: Yes, hello, this is Tricentennial Electrics. It's the same voice you heard before. "Have you come to place an order?"
Zh: “是的，你好，这里是三生电气。”是你之前听到过的声音。“您是打来订购的吗？”

En: A strange, metallic taste fills your mouth, as you stare at the intercom.
Zh: 你盯着对讲机，口腔里突然充满一阵奇怪的金属味道。

En: There's the static again, whispering like a seashell pressed against the ear.
Zh: 又是电流声，就像紧贴在耳边的海螺一样，对你轻声低语。

En: Hi, it's me again. I wanted to talk to you.
Zh: “喂，又是我。我想跟你说说话。”

En: Last time we talked there was some confusion. You got me mixed up with someone else.
Zh: “上次我们谈话的时候有点混乱。你把我跟其他人搞混了。”

En: Can you let me in please? I need to get inside this building.
Zh: “你能放我进来吗？我需要进到建筑里面去。”

En: My god...
Zh: “我的天……”

En: Please don't hang up. I just want to explain myself.
Zh: “请不要挂断。我只是想解释一下。”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: Before you can finish your sentence the voice continues speaking: "It's you..."
Zh: 你还没有说完，那个声音又开始说话了：“是你……”

En: Is this some kind of joke?
Zh: 这是在开玩笑吗？

En: Wait, are you repeating your words?
Zh: “等等，你是在重复自己的话吗？”

En: Didn't you already say that the last time we talked?
Zh: “上次我们谈话的时候，你不是已经说过了吗？”

En: Michel, just please... Even her breathing, the way her voice drops when she finishes the sentence sounds exactly the same.
Zh: “米歇尔，拜托……”就连她的呼吸，说完一句话之后尾音消退的方式，全都一模一样。

En: Why did you even call? I don't understand... You've been gone for months, she continues. "I thought you didn't care."
Zh: “你为什么要打过来？我不明白……你已经走了好几个月。”她听起来像是有些颤抖。“我还以为你一点都不在乎。”

En: It doesn't matter what I say, you're just going to continue, right?
Zh: “我说什么不重要，你打算就这么继续下去，对吗？”

En: Do you think it's *funny*, deceiving a police officer?
Zh: “你觉得这样*好玩*吗，欺骗一名警官？”

En: Just stop it already! Stop repeating your words!
Zh: “快停下来！别再重复你说过的话了！”

En: Ever since I came to work here it's been different...
Zh: “自从我来这里工作以后，一切都不同了……就好像我的脑袋被清空了一样……”

En: The voice from the intercom doesn't answer, but you can hear her breathing. Wind blows into your microphone again, crackling and echoing in the box.
Zh: 对讲机里的声音没有回应，但你能听到她的呼吸声。风再次吹进你的话筒，噼里啪啦的声音在电话亭里回荡。

En: Is it over? Can we talk now?
Zh: “说完了吗？现在我们可以谈谈吗？”

En: Thank you. Now tell me, who's responsible for this *joke*?
Zh: “谢谢你。现在请告诉我，这个*笑话*是谁搞出来的？”

En: Your hands are getting cold and your breathing becomes visible, forming small silvery puffs in the air.
Zh: 你的手变得冰凉，呼吸也变得清晰可见，在空气中形成一团银色的烟雾。

En: You're a recording.
Zh: “你是一份电话录音。”

En: (Hang up the call.)
Zh: (挂断通话。)

En: You press the number sign on the keypad that terminates the call. Twelve name cards on the call box read:
Zh: 你按下键盘上的井号，终止了这次通话。电话亭上有12张名牌，写着：

En: She tries again not to cry. And *still* doesn't succeed completely. Her quiet sobs sound old and distant, as if her voice is being played off a wax cylinder.
Zh: 她再次尝试抑制住哭泣。结果*还是*没有完全成功。她安静的啜泣声古老又遥远，像是用旧蜡制圆筒播出来的一样。

En: Go ahead, ancient recording. Cry then.
Zh: “来吧，古老的录音。哭吧。”

En: Why does it still feel like it's *my* fault?
Zh: “为什么还是感觉像是*我的*错一样？”

En: Real or not, your mirror neurons react: it feels painful to be listening to this.
Zh: 不管是不是真的，你的镜像神经元做出了反应：听到这个让你感觉到痛苦。

En: Anger boils up in your chest...
Zh: 怒气在你胸中聚集……

En: Her sound melts into the static from a long-distance phone call. From time to time you can hear people talking in the distance, but can't make out any words.
Zh: 她的声音逐渐融化在长途电话的电流声中。你有时候也会听见远处有人说话的声音，但却一个词也辨认不出来。

En: This is where you hung up the call the last time, but the recording is still going.
Zh: 上次你就是在这里挂断电话的，但是录音还在继续。

En: Keep listening.
Zh: 继续听。

En: A phone rings in the office with an old-fashioned chime and someone walks by in a pair of heels. The static is like a warm blanket wrapped around the sounds.
Zh: 一间拥有老式乐钟的办公室里，电话声响起，有人穿着一双高跟鞋走过。电流声就像一张温暖的毛毯，把那些声音包裹了起来。

En: Is anyone there?
Zh: “有人在吗？”

En: No one replies, but the static grows stronger like rainfall. Then a female voice speaks out, completely different from the one before. Glorious and *total* somehow. Crawling inside your head.
Zh: 没人回应，只有电流的声音越来越强，仿佛落雨一般。然后一个女人的声音响起，跟之前的完全不一样。不知怎么的，听起来美妙又*完整*。在你的脑海里缓缓爬行。

En: It's so nice to be able to finally forget.
Zh: “总算是能忘记了，真是太好了。”

En: My god, I didn't think I would hear your voice again.
Zh: “天啦，我还以为再也听不到你的声音了。”

En: As if my mind's been wiped clean...
Zh: “就好像我的脑袋被清空了一样……”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: It's so nice.
Zh: “这样很好。”

En: It's so nice to be able to finally forget about you.
Zh: “总算能忘记你了，真好。”

En: East Delta Commerce Centre? This must be the name of the Doomed Commercial Area!
Zh: 东德尔塔商业中心？这肯定是那个诅咒商业区的名字！

En: Perhaps... The web is comprised of radio stations. All lead back to one red heart, titled: The Game Master Frequency. A note says: 'This one can listen in on any station it wants?!'
Zh: 也许……网络是由无线电台组成的。全部都指向一颗红色的心脏，名字叫：游戏管理员频率。一张便条上写着：“它可以随心所欲地窃听所有电台？！”

En: A conductor for the hundreds of story threads that pass through the Game Master's Frequency.
Zh: 上百个故事线的导体，全都要通过游戏管理员的频率。

En: The leader of a massive on-air game built by these people.
Zh: 它就是这些人创造的大型实况游戏的统领。

En: All of this gone, left unrealized.
Zh: 这一切全都消失了，最终没能实现。

En: There's no way a little basement studio working *here* could pull anything like this off.
Zh: 一间开在*这种地方*的小地下工作室，是绝对不可能做出这种东西的。

En: This old fireplace is covered in lines drawn in blue and red marker, the mesh spreading over the stone like blood vessels on alabaster skin. It looks ghostly and strangely ancient.
Zh: 这个古老的壁炉被蓝色和红色的马克笔线条覆盖着，网格线遍布在石头上，就像雪白皮肤下的血管。看起来形同鬼魅，有种不可思议的古老感觉。

En: Radio frequencies for what?
Zh: 什么的无线电频率？

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: The whole thing resembles Kedran mosaic tiles. Very Pisantic.
Zh: 这些东西跟肯德拉的地砖很相似，很有皮桑缇克的风格。

En: Unclear. It looks like a cardiovascular system, split into veins and capillaries. Very advanced.
Zh: 不清楚。看起来像是心血管系统，分裂成静脉和毛细血管。很先进。

En: Hold on, how do I know what Kedran mosaic tiles are supposed to look like?
Zh: 等等，我怎么知道肯德拉的地砖长什么样？

En: History classes. Students with their textbooks open, studying the roots of our civilization. Those aquarelle-blue tiles looked beautiful in the sun.
Zh: 历史课上。学生面前是翻开的课本，正在学习我们文明的根源。那些水彩蓝色的瓷砖在太阳下显得格外美丽。

En: What am I looking at?
Zh: 我看到的是什么？

En: Radio frequencies, it seems. UKV123.6, UKV123.7, UKV123.9... some written notes too. Sparse and cryptic.
Zh: 好像是无线电频率。UKV123.6，UKV123.7，UKV123.9……还有一些手写的注释。稀少又晦涩。

En: Wait, who's the Game Master?
Zh: 等等，游戏管理员是谁？

En: Looks like a surveillance programme...
Zh: 看起来像是一个监控程序……

En: If it's a game, then who's playing?
Zh: 如果这是个游戏的话，那都有谁在玩呢？

En: This must be an elaborate piece of art.
Zh: 这肯定是一份精心制作的艺术品。

En: So we're dealing with something medical here?
Zh: 所以说我们现在是在处理医学问题了？

En: Whoever decides to call in to a call-in station, it looks like.
Zh: 看起来像是那些决定打给呼叫中心的人。

En: Someone very important.
Zh: 很重要的人。

En: Of course... (Narrow your eyes.) The anatomy of *the curse*.
Zh: 当然……（眯起眼睛。）对*诅咒*的解剖。

En: You think so? The web is comprised of radio stations. All lead back to one red heart, titled: The Game Master Frequency. A note says: 'This one can listen in on any station it wants?!'
Zh: 你这么觉得吗？网络是由无线电台组成的。全部都指向一颗红色的心脏，名字叫：游戏管理员频率。一张便条上写着：“它可以随心所欲地窃听所有电台。”

En: My god... The lieutenant leans closer, his finger tracking the maddening rhizome. "It's as if the *less money* they had, the more ambitious their project became."
Zh: “我的天……”警督靠近了一些，手指追踪着这令人发狂的根茎。“看来他们手上的*钱越少*，项目反而变得越来越有野心。”

En: A diagram for summoning some time-forgotten being? The symbols seem very esoteric.
Zh: 用来召唤某种被时间遗忘的存在？这些符号看起来非常深奥。

En: They must have had *massive* airwidth. These things don't come cheap.
Zh: 它们的频宽肯定*非常大*。这种东西可不便宜。

En: A list of names under the stations suggests people across *six isolas* would be playing: Mundi, Insulinde, Katla, Graad, Samara, and even Iilmaraa.
Zh: 电台下有一张清单，显示出了横跨*六大洲*的游戏玩家：蒙迪，伊苏林迪，卡特拉，格拉德，萨马拉，甚至还有伊尔玛。

En: What else? (Squint at the lines.)
Zh: 还有什么？（眯着眼看这些线条。）

En: Why do you say that?
Zh: “你为什么这么说？”

En: Nothing. It's just lines on marble, an echo from times long gone. No one has used the fireplace in ages.
Zh: 没什么。只是大理石的纹路，许久之前的回音罢了。已经很久没人用过这个壁炉了。

En: The schedule. He nods at the calendar on the chalkboard, wiping his marker-stained fingers clean. "I know *doom* when I see it -- the company was running out of funding."
Zh: “计划表。”他朝着黑板上的日程表点点头，把沾着马克笔印的手指擦拭干净。“当我看到*诅咒*的时候就明白了——这家公司的资金快用完了。”

En: Mhm. The cost of airwidth alone must have been huge.
Zh: “嗯。单说这个频宽的花费都不是个小数目。”

En: Exactly. He nods at the calendar on the chalkboard, wiping his marker-stained fingers clean against his jacket. "This schedule -- I know *doom* when I see it. The company was running out of funding."
Zh: “没错。”他朝着黑板上的日程表点点头，在夹克上擦了擦沾着马克笔印的手指。“这份计划表——当我看到*诅咒*的时候就明白了——这家公司的资金快用完了。”

En: What does this mean -- a rifle here?
Zh: “这是什么意思——这支步枪？”

En: This should cheer you up.
Zh: 这应该能让你高兴起来。

En: It means there are firearms -- albeit inoperable -- still lying around in Martinaise. It's an interesting coincidence, I would say. Might come in useful in the future.
Zh: “这就意味着还有枪支——虽然不能用了——散落在马丁内斯各处。要我说，这是个有趣的巧合。未来也许能用得着。”

En: He likes this find.
Zh: 他喜欢这个发现。

En: It means there are firearms -- breechloaders -- still lying around in Martinaise. This would have been good in our case against Ruby for Titus. Too late for that now, but still -- might be useful down the road...
Zh: “这就意味着还有枪支——后膛枪——散落在马丁内斯各处。我们在提图斯面前指控露比的时候，这个其实能派上用场的。虽然现在已经太迟了，不过——以后也许也能用得着……”

En: It means there are firearms -- breechloaders -- still lying around in Martinaise. This could prove useful in our case against Ruby for Titus. Men are easily swayed by the sight of a rifle.
Zh: “这就意味着还有枪支——后膛枪——散落在马丁内斯各处。如果我们要帮提图斯对付露比，这个应该能派上用场的。人看到步枪的时候很容易动摇。”

En: What does this mean -- a rifle here?
Zh: 这是什么意思——这支步枪？

En: It means there are firearms -- albeit inoperable -- still lying around Martinaise. This is an interesting coincidence. Could prove useful in some way. You don't know *how* yet, but it might be useful down the road…
Zh: 这就意味着还有枪支——虽然不能用了——散落在马丁内斯各处。这是个有趣的巧合。也许在某些方面能派上用场。你还不知道该*怎么用*，不过未来可能会派上用场。

En: It means there are firearms -- front loaders -- still lying around Martinaise. This would have been good in our case against Ruby for Titus. Too late for that now, but still -- might be useful down the road...
Zh: 这就意味着还有枪支——后膛枪——散落在马丁内斯各处。我们在提图斯面前指控露比的时候，这个其实能派上用场的。虽然现在已经太迟了，不过——以后也许也能用得着……

En: This is a good find. Really justifies coming down here and looking around.
Zh: 这是个很好的发现。来这里看看真的很值当。

En: It means there are firearms -- front loaders -- still lying around Martinaise. This could prove useful in our case against Ruby for Titus. Men are easily swayed by the sight of a rifle...
Zh: 这就意味着还有枪支——后膛枪——散落在马丁内斯各处。如果我们要帮提图斯对付露比，这个应该能派上用场的。人看到步枪的时候很容易动摇……

En: Could the murder weapon we're looking for be similar?
Zh: “我们要找的凶器会跟这种类似吗？”

En: It's the same *type* of weapon, yes. A breechloader. An interesting coincidence that we should find something so similar. But I'm afraid our search for the real murder weapon must continue.
Zh: “是相同*类型*的武器，没错。一支前膛枪。能找到如此相似的物件确实是个有趣的巧合。不过，恐怕我们寻找谋杀凶器的任务仍将继续。”

En: Could the murder weapon we're looking for be similar?
Zh: “我们要找的凶器会跟这种类似吗？”

En: For that we would have to take a closer look at the bullet you found, officer. I wouldn't read too much into this, yet -- what we have discovered here is an *inoperable antique*.
Zh: “这样的话，我们需要再仔细研究一下你找到的那枚子弹，警官。我还不会想太多，不过——我们在这里发现的东西只是*不能用的古董*。”

En: Isn't that the same gun that fired the fatal bullet?
Zh: “就是这种枪射出那枚致命子弹的吗？”

En: It's the same *type* of weapon, yes -- a breechloader. But it's not the same gun. This one's been out of order for *years...* I'm afraid our search for the real murder weapon must continue.
Zh: “是相同*类型*的武器，没错——前膛枪。不过不是同一支枪。这一支*几年前*就已经不能用了……恐怕我们还要继续寻找真正的凶器。

En: This is definitely the same make of gun as the one that fired the fatal bullet in the murder case. It's not the murder weapon itself, but still... you should have it, just in case.
Zh: 这支枪的型号跟凶杀案里射出那枚致命子弹的武器绝对是一样的。它虽然不是凶器本身，不过……为了以防万一，你还是拿着吧。

En: A few bricks have fallen off, revealing a compartment behind the wall. It's too dark to see in.
Zh: 有些砖块掉了下来，墙后的一个隔间露了出来。里面太黑了，什么也看不见。

En: Where are we? (Look around the secret room.)
Zh: “我们在哪？”（四下打量这个秘密房间。）

En: Look, there's a hole in the wall. (Shine light on the hidden compartment.)
Zh: “看，墙上有一个洞。”（把光打在隐藏的隔间上。）

En: Look, there's a hole in the wall. (Point at the hidden compartment.)
Zh: “看，墙上有一个洞。”（指着隐藏的隔间。）

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: So there's a hole in the wall.
Zh: 所以墙上有个洞。

En: Where am I?
Zh: 我在哪？

En: Seems like an old bunker from the Revolutionary period. Look at all those rifles... He points up, at the rifles under the ceiling. "Must be an old weapons cache."
Zh: “看来是大革命时期的一座旧碉堡。看看这些步枪……”他指着天花板下的步枪。“肯定是个旧军火库。”

En: There is, yes. And there also appears to be something *inside* the hole... Interesting. Do you want to take a look?
Zh: “没错，是的。而且洞*里面*好像还有东西……有点意思。你想看一眼吗？”

En: Okay, I do... (Look inside.)
Zh: “好吧，我想……”（看看里面。）

En: No, you do the honours. (Back off.)
Zh: “不，这份荣誉还是留给你吧。”（退后。）

En: Your hand reaches deep into darkness and spider webs, rummaging around. You find rusty rifles, hidden away...
Zh: 你的手伸入蜘蛛网和黑暗深处，四下翻找着。你找到了隐藏起来的生锈步枪……

En: Sure, he nods and steps closer. "Okay, here's some more rifles... None of these seem to be working. And oh..." His hand reaches in, rummaging in the dark...
Zh: “当然，”他点点头，然后靠近一步。“好吧，里面还有更多步枪……不过好像都不能用。还有……”他的手伸了进去，在黑暗中摸索着……

En: There is. Inside it's nothing but gloomy blackness. Better not stick your hand inside!
Zh: 有的。里面除了阴郁的黑暗之外什么也没有。最好别把手伸进去！

En: Inside a secret room infested with rats, spiders, and woodlice.
Zh: 在一个满是老鼠、蜘蛛和潮虫的秘密房间里。

En: Seems like an old weapons cache from the Revolutionary period.
Zh: 看起来像是大革命时期的一个旧军火库。

En: An old Belle-Magrave rifle -- that's *rare* to find in such good order. Seems to no longer be functional, but still -- rare. Here...
Zh: “一把老式的贝莱-马格雷步枪——能找到保存这么好的真的很*罕见*。虽然好像不能用了，但还是——很罕见。这里……”

En: It's yours, officer. He hands you the gun. "You found this place."
Zh: “它是你的了，警官。”他把枪递给你。“是你发现这个地方的。”

En: Inspect the rifles.
Zh: 检查步枪。

En: Most of them are rusty and inoperable like the rest, but one catches your eye -- a bolt action model with a fine wood stock. In better cosmetic order than the others.
Zh: 大部分都生锈了，跟其余那些一样没用，但是有一支吸引了你的眼球——栓式枪机，还有上等的木质枪托。外观看起来也比其他那些要好。

En: Rifles, Kim!
Zh: “步枪，金！”

En: The lieutenant steps closer, curious: "Are these any good?"
Zh: 警督靠近一些，有些好奇：“这些能用吗？”

En: This old Belle-Magrave looks okay... (Take the rifle.)
Zh: “这把老式的贝莱-马格雷看起来还不错……”（拿起步枪。）

En: This one looks nice. (Take the rifle.)
Zh: “这把看起来不错。”（拿起步枪。）

En: That's a rare sight. Seems to no longer be functional, but still -- a beautiful thing in its own way. The lieutenant whistles as he admires the rifle. His eyes are gleaming.
Zh: 警督观赏着步枪，吹了个口哨。“这可真是副罕见的景象。好像已经不能用了，不过——还是有自己独到的美。”

En: An old Belle-Magrave, from the Revolution, the lieutenant notes with approval. His eyes are gleaming. "Seems to no longer be functional, but still -- a beautiful thing, in its own way."
Zh: “一把老式的贝莱-马格雷步枪，大革命时期的。”警督点头赞同。他的眼睛闪闪发光。“看起来已经不能用了，不过——还是有自己独到的美。”

En: Stick your hand inside the hole.
Zh: 把手伸进洞里。

En: Never mind.
Zh: 没关系的。

En: Your hand reaches deep into darkness and spider webs, rummaging around. You find a number of rusty rifles, hidden away... Most of them are ugly and inoperable, but a single rifle catches your eye. It's a bolt-action with a fine wood stock.
Zh: 你的手伸入蜘蛛网和黑暗深处，四下翻找着。你找到一些隐藏起来的生锈步枪……大部分都是又丑又不实用，不过其中有一支抓住了你的眼球。那是一支栓式枪机，还有上等的木质枪托。

En: An old Belle-Magrave rifle, Revolutionary-era. Prized for its reliability more than it's accuracy. The rest are probably Belle-Magraves too, just too damaged to be identified.
Zh: 一支老式的贝莱-马格雷步枪，是大革命时期的。相比精准度来说，它的可靠性更受推崇。其余的可能也都是，只是损坏的太严重，已经辨认不出来了。

En: Take the rifle.
Zh: 拿走步枪。

En: Leave it there.
Zh: 就放在那里吧。

En: A fit service weapon for a fit officer. Too bad it doesn't work -- it looks like it's been out of order for years.
Zh: 好枪就要配好警官。可惜它已经不能用了——看起来已经坏了很多年。

En: History is best left where it lies. The rifle doesn't work anyway.
Zh: 历史最好被留在原来的地方。再说了，这支步枪也不能用。

En: Wait, what, really? You're not going to look inside a secret compartment behind the wall? Okay, officer... *Okay*.
Zh: 等等，什么，真的吗？你居然不打算看一眼墙后的秘密隔间里有什么？好吧，警官……*好吧*。

En: Take it! You're a police officer. Police officers carry guns.
Zh: 拿走它！你是一名警官。警官都是要配枪的。

En: An old Belle-Magrave rifle, Revolutionary-era. Prized for its reliability more than it's accuracy. The rest are probably Belle-Magraves too, just too damaged to be sure.
Zh: 一支老式的贝莱-马格雷步枪，大革命时期的。相比精准度来说，它的可靠性更受推崇。其余的可能也都是，只是损坏的太严重，已经辨认不出来了。

En: Look inside the refrigerator.
Zh: 看看冰箱里面。

En: Just what we were looking for -- let's see what's inside.
Zh: “正好是我们要找的东西——看看里面有什么吧。”

En: The bear looks oddly realistic. Is it taxidermy?
Zh: 熊看起来异常逼真。难道是动物标本？

En: Examine one of the ice cream wrappers in there.
Zh: 检查里面的一张冰淇淋包装纸。

En: A friendly cartoon bear smiles back at you from a glossy cellophane wrapper. It looks nothing like the fridge.
Zh: 光滑的玻璃包装纸后面，一只友好的卡通熊正朝你微笑。它跟冰箱看起来一点都不像。

En: But where's the filament memory? It's not here...
Zh: 但是纤维内存在哪？它不在这里……

En: The paper still smells of vanilla and chocolate.
Zh: 包装纸还残留着香草和巧克力的味道。

En: Good question. It looks like an ice cream fridge. The lieutenant reaches for one of the wrappers. He studies it in the light.
Zh: “这是个好问题。看起来像是一台冰淇淋冰箱。”警督拿起其中一张包装纸，在灯光下研究了起来。

En: Good question. It looks like an ice cream fridge. The lieutenant reaches for one of the wrappers. He tries to study it in the darkness.
Zh: “这是个好问题。它看起来像是一台冰淇淋冰箱。”警督拿起其中一张包装纸，试着在黑暗中研究它。

En: Somewhere in the past it's summer. Five-year-old Fifette lets go of her mother's hand, change jingling in her pockets, as she hops towards the ice cream stand right across the plaza.
Zh: 那是过去的某个夏天。5岁大的菲费特松开母亲的手，一蹦一跳地跑向广场对面的冰淇淋摊，口袋中的零钱叮当作响。

En: As she makes her way to the market stall the girl starts crying: a ferocious ice bear is guarding the fridge! Its paws raised to ward off any potential customers.
Zh: 女孩跑到街市档位的时候，突然哭了起来：一只恶狠狠的冰熊正守在冰箱前面！它扬起爪子，想拦住所有潜在的顾客。

En: Her mother rushes to soothe her, but Fifette doesn't want ice cream anymore, she just wants to go home. The ice bear stares at them as they leave the plaza. A gust of wind flies one of the wrappers right past the sobbing girl...
Zh: 母亲冲过去安抚她，但是菲费特已经不想要冰淇淋了，她只想回家。冰熊盯着她们一路离开广场。一阵风卷起一张包装纸，正好从哭泣的女孩身边飞过……

En: So they tried to sell ice cream from this hypercarnivore?
Zh: “所以他们打算用这个超级食肉动物卖冰淇淋吗？”

En: I know, says the lieutenant, "what an unfortunate marketing choice."
Zh: “我知道，”警督说到，“真是个不幸的营销选择。”

En: The bear's eyes are dead and empty, the corpse inside the fridge slowly rotting... The power has been cut off.
Zh: 熊的眼睛死气沉沉、空洞无神，冰箱里面的尸体正在慢慢腐烂……电源已经被切断了。

En: The bear's eyes are still glowing red -- it's guarding over the freezing corpse hidden inside its belly.
Zh: 熊的眼睛依然在发着红光——它正守卫着肚子里隐藏的那些冰淇淋包装纸。

En: Hey, I can't see the off-site copy anywhere... (Check the shelves again.)
Zh: “喂，我好像没看到场外拷贝啊……”（再次检查架子。）

En: Someone must have taken it... What does the note say?
Zh: “肯定是被人拿走了……便条上写的是什么？”

En: This is clearly *the* fridge. It looks big enough for *two* corpses.
Zh: “这显然就是*那台*冰箱了。看起来大的能放下*两具*尸体。”

En: This must be the fridge I was told about. But is it big enough for the corpse?
Zh: 这肯定就是他们告诉我的冰箱。不过不知道够不够大，能不能放尸体呢？

En: The fridge is plugged in now. Will you help me with the body, Kim?
Zh: “现在冰箱插上了。你能帮我搬下尸体吗，金？”

En: He studies the fridge. "It's certainly an eccentric choice, but it *is* big enough. And cold enough, too..."
Zh: 他研究着冰箱。“这确实是个反常的选择，不过它*确实*足够大。而且也足够冷……”

En: The lieutenant studies the fridge. "It's an eccentric choice... but it definitely fits the body. Now the only thing left to do is plug the fridge back in."
Zh: 警督研究着冰箱。“这确实是个反常的选择……不过肯定能放下尸体。现在唯一要做的，就是把冰箱的电源插上。”

En: It's perfect -- big, with a powerful cooling system. Now if you could just plug in the fridge and get the lieutenant to help you...
Zh: 它很完美——不仅够大，还有强力的冷却系统。现在只要你能插上冰箱，把警督找来帮忙的话……

En: It's perfect -- big, with a powerful cooling system. Now if you could just get the lieutenant to help you with the body...
Zh: 它很完美——不仅够大，还有强力的冷却系统。现在只要你能把警督找来帮忙就可以了……

En: It's certainly an... eccentric choice. But it's more than big enough -- and cold enough. Honestly, it's perfect. All we have to do is plug the fridge back in.
Zh: “这确实是一个……反常的选择。不过它已经足够大了——而且也足够冷。老实说，它很完美。现在我们只要把冰箱插上就可以了。”

En: It's certainly an... eccentric choice. But it is capacious -- and cold enough, too...
Zh: “这确实是一个……反常的选择。不过它很宽敞——而且也足够冷……”

En: It's perfect -- big, with a powerful cooling system. Now if you could just get the lieutenant to help you with the body...
Zh: 它很完美——不仅够大，还有强力的冷却系统。现在只要你能把警督找来帮忙就可以了……

En: It's perfect -- big, with a powerful cooling system. Now if you could just plug in the fridge and get the lieutenant to help you...
Zh: 它很完美——不仅够大，还有强力的冷却系统。现在只要你能插上冰箱，把警督找来帮忙的话……

En: Of course. I'll take the head, you take the feet...
Zh: “当然。我来搬头，你去抬脚……”

En: Alright, time to freeze the body!
Zh: “好吧，是时候把尸体冰冻起来了！”

En: Hold on... The lieutenant inspects the dark interior of the refrigerator. "There's no use if the fridge isn't working. Let's plug it in first and then return here to move the body."
Zh: “等等……”警督检查着黑乎乎的冰箱内部。“如果冰箱没开的话，那就一点用也没有了。我们先把它插上，然后回这里搬尸体。”

En: Of course. I'll take the head, you take the feet...
Zh: “当然。我来搬头，你去抬脚……”

En: Try to remove the dead man's boots again.
Zh: 再次尝试脱掉死者的靴子。

En: Of course. A giant, ice bear shaped fridge... He relaxes his hand, his face bathed in the harsh light of the open fridge door.
Zh: “当然了。只是一台巨大的、冰熊形状的冰箱……”他放松双手，脸庞沐浴在开启的冰箱门发出的强光之中。

En: Let's take a look inside.
Zh: “我们看看里面吧。”

En: ... but the optics on this are awful, he thinks. We need to be as silent as we can.
Zh: ……但是这上面的光学很糟糕，他想到。我们需要尽可能安静一些。

En: Your visual confirms: you could fit *two* more bodies in the ice bear fridge.
Zh: 你的视觉确认了：这台冰熊冰箱里还可以再放*两具*尸体。

En: The two of you? Easily.
Zh: 你们俩？那很容易。

En: You've *definitely* earned a drink after this. Perhaps even some *pagan rites*.
Zh: 这件事完成之后，你*绝对*应该好好喝上一杯。或许可以再来些*异教徒仪式*。

En: You have created an *ice bear sarcophagus*.
Zh: 你创造了一具*冰熊石棺*。

En: Yes. We need to celebrate by performing pagan rites. Let's bring out the mead and set it on fire.
Zh: “没错。这可要举行异教徒仪式来庆祝一下。我们把蜂蜜酒拿出来，把它点燃吧。”

En: Behold! We have created an *ice bear sarcophagus*.
Zh: “看啦！我们创造了一具*冰熊石棺*。”

En: He nods gravely. "Talk of the *ice bear sarcophagus* must *not* leave this room."
Zh: 他严肃地点点头。“这个*冰熊石棺*的话题绝对*不能*离开这个房间。”

En: I *knew* you would say that! I *knew* this would lead to drinking... He shakes his head vigorously. "No. No more. This is *paganistic* enough -- and it does *not* leave this room."
Zh: “我就*知道*你会这么说！我*知道*这样下去肯定会提到酒……”他使劲摇摇头。“不。别再来了。这已经够*异教徒*的了——而且它绝对*不可以*离开这个房间。”

En: Inside the icy realm of the ice bear fridge, the corpse stands slumped, waiting...
Zh: 在冰熊冰箱的寒冰领域里，尸体跌坐着，等待着……

En: A friendly cartoon bear smiles back at you from a glossy cellophane wrapper. It looks nothing like the fridge -- or the decomposing body in it.
Zh: 光滑的玻璃包装纸后面，一只友好的卡通熊在朝你微笑。跟冰箱看起来一点都不像——跟里面那具腐烂的尸体也不像。

En: The paper still smells of vanilla and chocolate. Even under this stench you can feel it.
Zh: 包装纸还残留着香草和巧克力的味道。即使是在这样的恶臭中，你还是感觉的到。

En: Shake from the sudden cold sensation.
Zh: 突如其来的寒冷让你开始颤抖。

En: The fridge buzzes with energy. The electricity bill on this thing must be *catastrophic*.
Zh: 通上电的冰箱嗡嗡直响。这玩意的电费账单一定是*灾难性*的。

En: The electricity bill must be *catastrophic*. On the other hand, it did help us with a certain corpse situation...
Zh: “这玩意的电费账单一定是*灾难性*的。话又说回来，它确实帮我们解决了尸体的问题……”

En: He's right. His work would be much more formal.
Zh: 他说得对。他的工作肯定会更拘谨一些。

En: ... lucky us.
Zh: ……我们还真是走运。

En: Indeed. Refrigerated meat is much better for coroner's work.
Zh: 确实。对于验尸官的工作来说，冷藏肉要好多了。

En: Okay, Kim. Take him away. (The lieutenant takes the body away -- work alone for the rest of the day.)
Zh: “好吧，金。把他带走吧。”（警督把尸体带走了——今天剩下来的时间你将独自工作。）

En: This must be the giant ice bear fridge that Soona mentioned. Look inside.
Zh: 这肯定就是苏娜提到的那台巨型冰熊冰箱。看看里面吧。

En: So what do you think of this fridge, Kim? It looks big enough for *two* corpses.
Zh: “所以你觉得这台冰箱怎么样，金？看起来大的够放*两具*尸体了。”

En: Hm, the fridge looks big enough for the corpse...
Zh: 嗯，冰箱看起来大的够放尸体了……

En: Let's bag him, Kim. Take him away. (The lieutenant takes the body away -- you work alone for the rest of the day.)
Zh: “我们把他装起来带走吧，金。”（警督把尸体带走了——今天剩下来的时间你将独自工作。）

En: Let's drag him to the Kineema. (You're finished with the corpse.)
Zh: “我们把他搬到锐影里吧。”（尸体处理完毕。）

En: Bag him. Take him away, Kim. (Let the lieutenant take the body away without further examination.)
Zh: “把他装起来，然后带走吧，金。”（让警督把尸体带走，不做进一步检查。）

En: Search the body *one more time*, thoroughly.
Zh: *再次*彻底地调查一遍尸体。

En: Stop wasting time, you're 'smart'!
Zh: 别浪费时间了，你很‘聪明’！

En: Knows there's a bullet in there.
Zh: 知道里面有一颗子弹。

En: Need reason to pull head off.
Zh: 把脑袋扯下来是需要理由的。

En: Look in the mouth!
Zh: 看看嘴里！

En: Established probable cause.
Zh: 确认可能的原因。

En: Didn't establish probable cause.
Zh: 没有确认可能的原因。

En: Felt a little bump on the head.
Zh: 感到头上有一个小肿块。

En: Refrigerated the body.
Zh: 把尸体冻起来。

En: Titus mentioned a wound...
Zh: 提图斯提到一个伤口……

En: Two officers must be present to conduct a field autopsy.
Zh: 要想进行现场尸检，必须有两名警官同时在场。

En: Try to remove the dead man's boots.
Zh: 试着脱掉死者的靴子。

En: Shoot, Loonie Roonie!
Zh: 来吧，疯子罗尼！

En: Tell me something, dead man.
Zh: 跟我说点什么吧，死人。

En: Tell me, who are you, dead man?
Zh: “告诉我，你是谁，死人？”

En: Talked to him in your dream.
Zh: 在梦里跟他说话。

En: Amber Die with a Mosquito.
Zh: 带蚊子的琥珀骰子。

En: You see a terrifying ice bear with a strange compartment in its belly. The door is covered in frost and the bear's eyes are glowing red.
Zh: 你看见一只可怕的冰熊，肚子上有个奇怪的隔间。隔间门上布满冰霜，熊的眼睛正发出红色的光芒。

En: Crack open the door...
Zh: 把门撬开……

En: Back off. [Leave.]
Zh: 后退。[离开。]

En: This ice bear is a hypercarnivore. Be careful.
Zh: 这只冰熊可是超级食肉动物。要小心。

En: What *is* this thing? It looks like a giant ice bear...
Zh: “*这是*什么东西？看起来像是一只巨大的冰熊……”

En: A gust of freezing cold air rushes to greet you. You hear a low grumble, as the bear regulates itself... This is the inside of a refrigerator.
Zh: 一股冰冷的空气迎面朝你扑来。你听见一阵低沉的嘟囔声，就好像熊在自我调节一样……这是冰箱的内部。

En: The lieutenant doesn't answer. His eyes are glued to the animal. A sharp slice of light shines out from its mysterious belly-door...
Zh: 警督没有回话。他的眼睛还停留在那个动物身上。一道强烈的光线从它肚子上那道神秘的小门里透射出来……

En: Your words echo in the empty chamber, ringing against the wet floor tiles. The bear keeps staring. A sharp slice of light shines out from its mysterious belly-door.
Zh: 你的话语在空旷的房间里回荡，与潮湿的地板砖碰撞出声响。一道强烈的光线从它肚子上那道神秘的小门里透射出来……

En: The lieutenant takes a peek inside. His hand has found the holster of his gun.
Zh: 警督往里面瞥了一眼，手已经扶在腰间的枪套上。

En: Take the note from the door.
Zh: 把门上的便条取下来。

En: Close the door. [Leave.]
Zh: 关上门。[离开。]

En: What is a giant bear-shaped fridge doing in an abandoned cellar in the first place?
Zh: “首先，为什么一间废弃的地下室里会有这么大一尊冰熊形状的冰箱啊？”

En: What is even worse -- the bear is still costing them money, to this day... The lieutenant points at the red snaky cable running from the fridge.
Zh: “更糟糕的是——直到今天，这只熊还在花他们的钱……”警督指着冰箱外那条弯弯曲曲的红色电线。

En: This must be the fridge you were told about. Look inside, see if it's big enough for the corpse.
Zh: 这肯定就是他们告诉你的冰箱。看看里面的空间够不够大，能不能放尸体。

En: Beautiful, he says, wiping his hands in his handkerchief. "A dead body in an ice bear fridge -- this is some of the best police work I've ever done."
Zh: “漂亮，”他一边说着，一边用手帕擦拭着双手。“冰熊冰箱里的尸体——我当警察的职业生涯里，这是最棒的工作之一。”

En: Really? You think it's good work?
Zh: “真的吗？你觉得做的挺好的吗？”

En: I'm not sure I believe you.
Zh: “我不太相信你。”

En: No. Not really. Look at that! What have we done? We stuffed a *dead body in an ice bear fridge*. This story does not leave this room.
Zh: “不。并没有。你看看！我们都做了什么？把*一具尸体塞进一台冰熊冰箱了*。这个故事绝对不可以离开这个房间。”

En: Of course you don't. Look at that! What have we *done?* We stuffed a dead body in an ice bear fridge. This does *not* leave this room.
Zh: “当然没有了。你看看！我们都*做了*什么？我们把一具尸体塞进一台冰熊冰箱了。这件事绝对*不可以*离开这个房间。”

En: I think this is a glorious achievement and people need to hear about it.
Zh: “我觉得这是一个光荣的成就，大家都需要听到。”

En: As impressive as the fridge itself is, this is a rather small victory. He throws an exasperated look at your feet. "Among many defeats."
Zh: “冰箱本身挺让人印象深刻的，不过这只是一次小小的胜利。”他恼怒地看着你的脚。“在无数次失败之间。”

En: As impressive as the fridge is, this is a *small* victory. Among numerous defeats.
Zh: “冰箱是挺让人印象深刻的，这只是一次*小小的*胜利——在无数次失败之间。”

En: We did our best with the means at our disposal.
Zh: “在所有能用到的手段之间，我们已经做到最好了。”

En: Yeah, I'm not *proud* either, but we did find a fridge.
Zh: “是啊，我也没有很*得意*，不过我们确实找到冰箱了。”

En: This isn't police work, Kim. It's *art*. We're *artists* and this was our vision.
Zh: “这不是警察的工作，金。这是*艺术*。我们是*艺术家*，这是我们的幻想。”

En: At least we've stopped the body from decomposing further. Now you can conduct another inspection. Under controlled *circumstances*.
Zh: “至少我们阻止了尸体进一步腐烂。现在你可以进行其他检查——在可控的*环境*下。”

En: He throws a look at your feet. "And I'm guessing grave-robbery is also part of the performance?"
Zh: 他看了一眼你的脚。“那我猜盗墓也是表演的一部分了？”

En: If I were an artist, this would certainly *not* be my vision. I would be much, much more conservative in my work...
Zh: “如果我是一个艺术家，这肯定*不是*我的幻想。我的作品会更加保守一些……”

En: Did we, though? He throws an exasperated look at your feet and purses his lips.
Zh: “我们有吗？”他恼怒地看了一眼你的脚，紧闭双唇。

En: Did we, though? He sighs. "Okay, maybe we *did*"
Zh: “有吗？”他叹了口气。“好吧，也许我们*确实有*。”

En: At least we've stopped the body from decomposing further -- or will have, once you plug the fridge back in. Then you can conduct one more inspection.
Zh: “至少我们阻止了尸体进一步腐烂——或者应该说可以阻止，等你把冰箱插上之后。然后你可以再进行一次检查。”

En: I hope not. He throws an exasperated look at your feet and purses his lips.
Zh: “希望不是。”他恼怒地看了一眼你的脚，紧闭双唇。

En: No. They do *not* -- they *need* to hear about our uneventful, boring, downright *conservative* murder investigation.
Zh: “不。他们*不需要*——他们*需要*听到的是我们平淡、无聊、完全*保守*的凶杀案调查。”

En: He means it. He doesn't want to be the Ice Bear Cop.
Zh: 他是认真的。他不想当冰熊警察。

En: The fridge sits empty before you, an overindulgent wardrobe now that the cooling systems aren't active.
Zh: 你面前的冰箱空空如也，因为制冷系统没有生效，现在它不过是一个过度放纵的衣柜。

En: You've acquired an interactable item. Investigate this item further by going to the INTERACT tab in your INVENTORY.
Zh: 你得到了一个可互动物品。前往物品栏中与之互动吧。

En: Relax, Kim. It's the fridge, remember?
Zh: “放松，金，这只是一台冰箱，还记得吗？”

En: Relax, Kim, it's a fridge.
Zh: “放松，金，这只是一台冰箱。”

En: Of course. Just a giant, ice bear shaped fridge... He relaxes his hand, his face bathed in the harsh light of the open fridge door.
Zh: “当然了。只是一台巨大的、冰熊形状的冰箱……”他放松双手，脸庞沐浴在开启的冰箱门发出的强光之中。

En: You see the looming shadow of an ice bear with a strange compartment in its belly. The door is slightly open...
Zh: 你看见一只冰熊若隐若现的阴影，它的肚子上还有一个奇怪的隔间。隔间门微微敞开着……

En: Its eyes are dead, but it's still terrifying... Run before it wakes up!
Zh: 它的眼睛毫无生气，但还是很可怕……在它醒来之前，快点跑路吧！

En: The lieutenant doesn't answer, his eyes glued to the animal. It's dark inside its mysterious belly-compartment...
Zh: 警督没有回话。他的眼睛还停留在那个动物身上。那个神秘的肚子隔间里漆黑一片……

En: Your words echo in the empty chamber, ringing against the wet floor tiles... It's dark inside the bear's mysterious belly-compartment...
Zh: 你的话语在空旷的房间里回荡，与潮湿的地板砖碰撞出声响。那个神秘的肚子隔间里漆黑一片……

En: A faint waft of cool air rolls out. You hear the sound of dripping water. This is the inside of a defrosting refrigerator.
Zh: 一阵微弱的冷风吹了出来。你听见滴水的声响。这是正在解冻中的冰箱内部。

En: The bear's eyes are dead and empty, ice inside the fridge slowly melting... The power has been cut off.
Zh: 熊的眼睛死气沉沉，空洞无神，冰箱里的冰块正在慢慢融化……电源已经被切断了。

En: The bear's eyes are still glowing red, watching over all the ice cream wrappers hidden inside its belly.
Zh: 熊的眼睛仍然发着红光，照看着肚子里隐藏的那些冰淇淋包装纸。

En: You pocket the note -- *and* the little fridge magnets keeping it on the door.
Zh: 你把便条塞进口袋——*还有*把它固定在门上的那个小冰箱贴。

En: Shall we go and get the body, then? I'll take the head, you take the feet. The stairs won't be easy, but we'll manage.
Zh: “那我们把尸体搬过来吧？我搬头，你抬脚。上下楼梯不会太容易，但是我们应该能做到的。”

En: Okay, let's do this! (Clap your hands.)
Zh: “好吧，那就开工吧！”（拍手。）
