En: It's not going to be the *easiest* thing in the world, but we'll manage.
Zh: “那肯定不会是世界上*最容易*的事，但是我们能搞定的。”

En: The body is heavier than you expected -- and stinkier. It takes half an hour to get it down to the basement, then ten more minutes to stuff it into the fridge. The lieutenant takes a step back to admire your handiwork.
Zh: 尸体比你想象的还要重——而且也更臭。你们花了半小时才把他搬到地下室，然后又花了十多分钟把他塞进冰箱。警督退后一步，欣赏着你的杰作。

En: The shelves are empty. All you see are crumpled ice cream wrappers with the brand name *Revachol Ice City*. A handwritten note has been attached to the door. The fridge is huge.
Zh: 架子是空的。你唯一能看见的只有皱巴巴的冰淇淋包装纸，上面印着*瑞瓦肖冰城*的商标名。门上贴着一份手写便条。这台冰箱真是有够巨大。

En: Kim, this fridge looks perfect for the corpse -- we don't have to ask around anymore.
Zh: “金，这台冰箱看来很适合放尸体——我们不用再到处询问了。”

En: This looks like it could easily fit the corpse inside.
Zh: 看起来应该很容易把尸体塞进去。

En: At least it's not running them a loss anymore, now that you've un-plugged the cable. He points at the red snaky cable running from the fridge.
Zh: “现在你拔掉了电线，至少不会让他们继续遭受损失了。”他指着冰箱旁边伸出的那条弯弯曲曲的红色电线。

En: This orange machine is dead-still. It has a hand-cranked ice cream churner and an electric freezer, the ice around it slowly melting.
Zh: 这台橙色的机器还是没有通电。上面有一个手摇冰淇淋搅拌器和一个电冻器，周围的冰块正在慢慢融化。

En: This orange machine is buzzing like an old submarine. It has a hand-cranked ice cream churner on top and an electric freezer that appears to be frozen shut.
Zh: 这台橙色的机器像一台破旧的潜水艇一样嗡嗡叫着。它的顶部是一个手摇冰淇淋搅拌器，还有一个似乎被冻住的电冻器。

En: Take the filament memory.
Zh: 取走纤维内存。

En: Ice groans and howls under the strain of your giant Kvalsund multitool -- until the lid cracks open. Blue electric lights come to life...
Zh: 在你手中巨大的库瓦桑德多用工具的压力下，冰块开始呻吟嚎叫——直到盖子破裂开来。蓝色的电光苏醒了……

En: Ice groans and howls under the strain of your giant Kvalsund multitool -- until the lid cracks open.
Zh: 在你手中巨大的库瓦桑德多用工具的压力下，冰块开始呻吟嚎叫——直到盖子被撬开了。

En: Disappointment washes over you as you stare into the almost-empty ice cream maker.
Zh: 你盯着几乎空无一物的冰淇淋制造机，一种失望感朝你的全身席卷而来。

En: What? No ice cream...
Zh: “什么？没有冰淇淋吗……”

En: Your stomach grumbles. The fridge is cold, the cellar dark and humid.
Zh: 你的肚子咕噜咕噜地叫唤着。冰箱很冷，地下室又黑又潮。

En: A scoop of ice cream would have been nice, yes, the lieutenant agrees. Someone's stomach grumbles. The room feels very cold.
Zh: “要是能来上一勺冰淇淋确实不错，”警督表示赞同。某人的肚子开始咕噜咕噜地叫唤。这个房间非常冷。

En: What? No ice cream...
Zh: 什么？没有冰淇淋吗……

En: You gently lift the cube from its frosty bedding, careful to not damage it.
Zh: 你轻轻地把立方体从冻住的基座里取了出来，小心地不弄坏它。

En: [Close the lid.]
Zh: [关上盖子。]

En: We should take it back to Miss Luukanen-Kilde as soon as possible. I'm not sure how well unused filaments tolerate room temperatures.
Zh: “我们应该尽快把它带回去，交给卢凯伦-吉尔德小姐。我不确定未使用的纤维内存能不能承受这个房间的温度。”

En: Yes, but aren't you curious to know what's on the precious filament? There's a radiocomputer upstairs...
Zh: 是的，但是你难道不会好奇，这个宝贵的纤维内存上有什么吗？楼上就有一台无线电脑……

En: Darkness lies inside, but you can faintly make out an object, intricate and foreign, left there for a sub-zero beauty sleep -- a filament memory with the words "OFF-SITE COPY" written on its side.
Zh: 里面很黑，但你可以模糊地辨认出一个物体，错综复杂、充满异域感，被独自留下睡了个零度以下的美容觉——那是一个纤维内存，侧面写着“场外拷贝”几个字。

En: Inside on a frosty platform lies an object, intricate and foreign, left there for a sub-zero beauty sleep -- a filament memory with the words "OFF-SITE COPY" written on its side.
Zh: 冻结的平台上躺着一个物体，错综复杂、充满异域感，被独自留下睡了个零度以下的美容觉——那是一个纤维内存，侧面写着“场外拷贝”几个字。

En: If you want to try again, then you need to have the prybar in your *hand*, detective.
Zh: 如果你想再试一次，那就要把撬棍握在*手里*，警探。

En: Equip the PRYBAR by going to the TOOLS tab in your INVENTORY. From there, you can equip it to a HELD slot.
Zh: 你可以在道具栏的工具界面中装备撬棍至一个手持栏位中。

En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi would have a prybar in his Kineema, but he's not here right now.
Zh: 曷城警督的锐影里应该有撬棍，但是他现在不在这里。

En: The ice squeaks beneath the prybar. You think you've got the bar jammed in there pretty well, but the lid simply won't budge.
Zh: 撬棍下的冰块发出嘎吱嘎吱的声响。你觉得撬棍的位置卡的很好，但盖子还是纹丝不动。

En: The ice squeaks beneath the prybar -- you could *swear* you succeeded! -- but the lid simply won't budge.
Zh: 撬棍下的冰块发出嘎吱嘎吱的声响——你可以*发誓*你已经成功了！——但盖子还是纹丝不动。

En: Yeah, well, that's a good prybar -- I'm not criticising it -- but this ice cream maker is frozen *shut*. It takes an *advanced* tool to get it open.
Zh: “那是根好撬棍——我不是批评它——但是这个冰淇淋制造机冻的很*严实*。要想打开的话，只能找把更*高级*一点的工具。”

En: That's a good prybar, but this ice cream maker is frozen *shut*. It takes an *advanced* tool to get it open. Some kind of... superprybar? You have no idea where to get one.
Zh: 那是根好撬棍，但是这个冰淇淋制造机冻的太*严实*。要想打开的话，只能找把更*高级*一点的工具。某种……超级撬棍？你不知道该去哪里找。

En: Sooner or later you will stumble upon a tool mighty enough. Then we will know what's in this mysterious ice cream maker.
Zh: 你迟早会偶然发现一个足够强大的工具。然后我们就能知道这个冰淇淋制造机里面的秘密了。

En: Advanced? Where do we get one?
Zh: “更高级？我们去哪找？”

En: I have no idea, officer. This ice cream maker isn't important enough to requisition a special tool.
Zh: “我不知道，警官。这台冰淇淋制造机没那么重要，不值得申请特殊工具。”

En: Crack open the lid.
Zh: 把盖子撬开。

En: Prybar not strong enough.
Zh: 撬棍的力量不够。

En: Machine unplugged.
Zh: 机器没有插电。

En: Better grip with gloves.
Zh: 最好戴上手套再抓。

En: Standard Anti-Wirrâl Die.
Zh: 标准的反威勒尔骰子。

En: I have a prybar in my Kineema, in case you want to try again. Though it might be that my prybar isn't strong enough for that lid... He eyes the ice cream maker suspiciously.
Zh: “我的锐影里有一根撬棍，如果你想再试一次的话。不过我的撬棍可能力量不够，打不开那个盖子……”他怀疑地打量着那台冰淇淋制造机。

En: Didn't Soona give you a perfect tool for this kind of job -- the Kvalsund? You should take it out.
Zh: “苏娜不是给过你一个工具吗，它是处理这种工作的完美选择——库瓦桑德？你应该把它拿出来。”

En: Equip the MULTI-TOOL by going to the TOOLS tab in your INVENTORY. From there, you can equip it to a HELD slot.
Zh: 打你可以在道具栏的工具界面中装备多用工具至一个手持栏位中。

En: You need to have the Kvalsund KR+2 Multi-Tool in your *hand*, detective.
Zh: 你需要把库瓦桑德KR+2多用工具握在*手里*，警探。

En: You slip your fingers under the frozen lid, but the ice is too cold for you to get a good grip. A prybar would come handy here... or something stronger, like the Kvalsund KR+2 Multi-Tool.
Zh: 你把手指滑到冻住的盖子下方，但是冰块太凉，你没办法好好抓住它。一根撬棍应该能帮上你的忙……或者更强大的工具，比如库瓦桑德KR+2多用工具。

En: No -- this is going to need something else, some kind of *super* prybar. Don't even try to open it with a regular prybar, you're just wasting your time.
Zh: 不行——你需要别的东西，某种*超级*撬棍。根本都不用试着用普通的撬棍，你只是在浪费时间。

En: This orange machine is buzzing like an old submarine. It has a hand-cranked ice cream churner on top and an electric freezer that appears to be frozen shut.
Zh: 这台橙色的机器嗡嗡直响，就像一台破旧的潜水艇。它的顶部是一个手摇冰淇淋搅拌器，还有个似乎被冻住的电冻器。

En: Crack open the lid.
Zh: 把盖子撬开。

En: Prybar not strong enough.
Zh: 撬棍的力量不够。

En: Machine unplugged.
Zh: 机器没有插电。

En: Better grip with gloves.
Zh: 最好戴上手套再抓。

En: Standard Anti-Wirrâl Die.
Zh: 标准的反威勒尔骰子。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Turn the ice cream crank.
Zh: 转动冰淇淋机手柄。

En: Ice squeaks beneath your Kvalsund multitool, but your fingers slip away from the tool, the lid shut as tightly as before.
Zh: 冰块在你的库瓦桑德多用工具下发出嘎吱的声响，但你的手指从工具上滑了下来，盖子又像以前一样紧紧合上了。

En: Turning the crank feels oddly satisfying, like stirring your childhood dreams... In the distance you hear water dripping.
Zh: 转动手柄让你产生了一种奇妙的满足感，就像激活了年幼时代的梦想……你听见远处传来水滴滴答答的声音。

En: It's all gone now. You never became a poet or an entroponaut.
Zh: 现在全没了。你永远也不会变成诗人，或者是企业家。

En: What better to assuage the creeping sense of failure than some frozen fat and sugar?
Zh: 还有什么能比冻结的脂肪和糖分更能缓和这种涌动的诡异失败感呢？

En: This orange machine is dead-still. It has a hand-cranked ice cream churner and an electric freezer, the ice around it slowly melting.
Zh: 这台橙色的机器还是没有通电。上面有一个手摇冰淇淋搅拌器和一个电冻器，周围的冰块正在慢慢融化。

En: Unplugging the ice cream maker will allow it to defrost.
Zh: 拔掉冰淇淋制造机的插头应该能让它解冻。

En: Look, a black cable is running into the breaker box...
Zh: 看，一条黑色的电线插在断路器盒上……

En: And it's already unplugged. There's not much else to do other than wait for it to defrost or *bulk up* and get stronger.
Zh: 它已经被拔下来了。现在也没什么可以做的，只有等它解冻，或者*胀大*，变得更加强壮。

En: Try to crack open the lid.
Zh: 试着砸开盖子。

En: You slip your fingers under the frozen lid, but the ice is too cold for you to get a good grip. A prybar would come handy here.
Zh: 你把手指滑到冻住的盖子下方，但是冰块太凉，你没办法好好抓住它。一根撬棍应该能帮上你的忙。

En: What is this...
Zh: 这是什么……

En: You see the prybar's metal handle bending, right before your very eyes.
Zh: 撬棍的金属手柄在你眼前弯曲了。

En: You're still holding the wrong tool! Do it with the Kvalsund multitool, you even have one...
Zh: 你手里的这个工具不对！要用库瓦桑德的多用工具，你正好有一个……

En: Maybe you could get better grip with some gloves...
Zh: 也许有手套的话，就能好好抓住了……

En: You see primitive oil rigs built into glaciers -- by *boreal dweorg* -- yurts under the snow, great mammoth-like beasts of burden...
Zh: 你看见原始的石油钻塔矗立在冰川上——那是*北方矮人*的杰作——白雪覆盖下的圆顶帐篷，还有像巨大猛犸象一样的驮兽……

En: Albeit dark and cold, this vision also feels cosy in its own way. Like eggnog, or morphine! A much needed respite from our own world.
Zh: 尽管又黑又冷，这幅景象还是有一种独到的舒适感。就像是蛋酒，或者是吗啡！我们的世界更需要这样短暂的喘息。

En: So much pain. Back pain, neck pain, headaches, carpal tunnel, chest pain... no gym membership can make up for working in this manner.
Zh: 好多疼痛。后背疼，脖子疼，头疼，腕管疼，胸口疼……没有什么健身房的会员资格能以这种方式补偿锻炼的不足。

En: The full text reads: 'Heat death of the Universe is the new black'. Another note says: 'The biggest advancement in role-playing systems since the Thirties'.
Zh: 全文是：“宇宙热寂理论是新的黑暗。”还有一个注释上写着：“自从30年代以来，这是角色扮演系统最大的进步。”

En: Outside, a cold wind wraps the building in its bosom. Snowflakes in the wind. An old woman passes what the locals call the Doomed Commercial Area. She tries hard not to look at the bookstore windows. It's *unwise*.
Zh: 屋外，一阵冷风将建筑拥在怀中。雪花随风飘舞。一个老妇人经过当地人口中的诅咒商业区。她努力控制自己，不去看书店的窗户。这样是*不明智*的。

En: Just look at those details. So much effort...
Zh: “看看这些细节。费了这么大的心力……”

En: Well. This has been educational -- let's move on from the welkins.
Zh: “嗯。这很有教育意义——我们还是别管精灵了吧。”

En: This old green chalkboard is covered in scribbles, sketches and schemes like some ancient cave mural.
Zh: 这面老旧的绿色黑板上到处都是潦草的字画、草图和图式，就像古老的洞穴壁画一样。

En: Inspect the photos.
Zh: 查看相片。

En: Inspect the schedule.
Zh: 查看计划表。

En: Inspect the notes.
Zh: 查看注释。

En: Inspect the drawings.
Zh: 查看图画。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: This is a monthly calendar from the year '50. Cryptic words like 'sprint', 'daily minime', and 'GPI' span the marker-drawn grid -- the grand scheme of production and money.
Zh: 这是50年的月历。诸如“迭代”，“每日小会”，“指标”之类的神秘词汇跨越在马克笔画出的格子上——这是有关生产和金钱的宏伟计划。

En: As time goes on, the numbers in the boxes grow rarer and rarer. The board becomes an empty snowfield in the final days... Only failure and regret dwell in this region.
Zh: 随着时间的流逝，方块里的数字变得越来越少。最后的那些天里，整块黑板变成了一片空旷的雪原……这片区域只留下失败和后悔。

En: Keep reading -- what happened?
Zh: 继续阅读——发生什么了？

En: A note in the bottom left corner of the chalkboard says: "SEE THE PROD. SCHEDULE FILAMENT FOR DETAILS."
Zh: 黑板左下角有一张纸条，上面写着：“详情请查阅生产计划纤维内存。”

En: Your eyes glide over four sections of the imposing green chalkboard...
Zh: 你的眼睛掠过这面壮观绿色黑板的四个区块……

En: The handwriting is only partly legible, but you can still make out three slogans: 'Call in, tune out!', 'WIRRÂL UNTETHERED', and 'Heat death of the Universe'...
Zh: 你只能依稀辨认出部分笔迹，但还是认出了三个口号：“打进来，调出去！”，“自由威勒尔”，还有“宇宙热寂理论……”

En: Everyone is constantly teetering on the edge of the abyss. An abyss of *production*. These squares look orderly but beneath them is chaos, worry, pain...
Zh: 每个人都在深渊的边缘不断地摇晃。那是*产品*的深渊。这些方块看起来很规整，但是在那之下却隐藏着混乱、忧虑和痛苦……

En: It looks a bit like an academic calendar. Only much more *brutal*.
Zh: 这个看起来有点像校历。只不过更*残忍*一些。

En: 'Minime' stands for a 'mini-meeting'. It's part of a bigger framework for managing work called R.U.N. Station 41 tried to implement it a few years ago, but failed.
Zh: ‘小会’代表‘小型会议’。它属于一个更大的工作管理框架的一部分，名为R.U.N.。几年前41分局尝试实施过一段时间，但是失败了。

En: These lithe, pointy-eared creatures appear to be different types of *welkins*. You make out autumnal *candle-welkins* casting wax-based magic...
Zh: 这些长着尖耳朵的柔软生物像是不同种类的*精灵*。你认出了秋日的*蜡烛精灵*，正在施展蜡烛魔法……

En: Your flashlight slides over an old green chalkboard covered in scribbles, sketches and schemes like some ancient cave mural.
Zh: 你的手电筒扫过一面破旧的绿色黑板，上面到处都是潦草的字画、草图和图式，就像古老的洞穴壁画一样。

En: One of the welkins -- towering among the rest -- appears to be different, however.
Zh: 不过，有一个精灵——比其他的都要高——好像不太一样。

En: The photo collage depicts barren, icy landscapes wrapped in perpetual night. You see permafrost and glacial landforms, dead trees groaning under the snow.
Zh: 这张相片拼贴画描绘的是永恒长夜包裹下那一片贫瘠而冰冷的风景。你看见永久冻土和冰川地貌，还有压在积雪下呻吟的枯树。

En: Who are all those creatures? Fantasies of a tortured, feverish mind?
Zh: 这是什么生物？一个备受折磨又极度兴奋的心灵幻想中的产物？

En: This looks like an *enormous improvement* on the classic Wirrâl setting. Much more *advanced*. Light years ahead.
Zh: 这看起来像是传统威勒尔背景中的一个*巨大进步*，要更加*先进*一些。遥遥领先。

En: You should adopt one of those welkins as your *persona*. No longer a mere man -- but a welkin...
Zh: 您应该选择其中一个精灵，作为您的*人物角色*。从此不再是一个普通人类——而是精灵……

En: The lieutenant can't help but comment: "An inordinate amount of time has gone into drawing these little welkin creatures..."
Zh: 警督忍不住评论到：“画这些小精灵怪物实在是花掉太多时间了……”

En: Why would anyone spend so much time on this?
Zh: “为什么会有人在这个上面花这么多时间呢？”

En: Who *are* these creatures? Who drew them? Are they *real*? I have so many questions...
Zh: “这些生物*是*什么？是谁画的？它们是*真实*存在的吗？我有好多问题……”

En: (Whisper) "One of them is a welkin supremacist."
Zh: （小声）“其中有一个是精灵至上主义者。”

En: Some people really *like* building a world, I think. Even if it's just for a game.
Zh: “我想，有些人真的很*喜欢*建造一个世界。尽管这只是个游戏。”

En: Mhm... political commentary. That one has a great beard too... The lieutenant nods at the welkin's facial hair.
Zh: “嗯……政治评论。那家伙的胡子也很长……”警督 朝着精灵满脸的胡子点了点头。

En: This looks like *concept art* for a project. It's not really real.
Zh: “看起来像是一个项目的*概念图*。不过并不是真的。”

En: ...and for what? All gone.
Zh: ……又是为了什么呢？全都没了。

En: If they put this much effort into making welkin cooler, imagine what they did with the dweorg and the humans?
Zh: 如果他们花了那么大功夫想把精灵弄的酷一点，想象一下：他们能把矮人和人类变成什么样？

En: Step back and look at something else.
Zh: 退后几步，看看别的东西。

En: Entire oceans have been frozen from shore to shore. There are pictures of settlements on dried up riverbeds, abandoned in a storm. Animal corpses in the dark, carcasses and bones.
Zh: 整片海洋的每一个海岸都被冻结成冰。相片里是建立在干枯河床上的定居点，被遗弃在暴风雨中。黑暗中满是动物的尸体，残躯和骸骨。

En: A pinned postcard reads: 'The heat death scenario -- a desperate fight for geothermal energy engulfs the world as Wirrâl becomes untethered from its sun, drifting through the Universe.'
Zh: 一张钉住的明信片上写着：“热寂设想——威勒尔挣脱了太阳引力的束缚，向宇宙流浪而去，为了争夺地热能，一场孤注一掷的战争席卷了整个世界。”

En: Looks like they didn't make it. The lieutenant looks at the frigid ice field of nothingness.
Zh: 警督看着虚无严寒的冰原，总结道：“看来他们没能成功。”

En: Looks like they didn't make it.
Zh: 看来他们没能成功。

En: Some of the writing has faded with age, but you can still make out sections here and there; photos and drawings have been pinned to the board.
Zh: 有些笔迹已经随着岁月一起褪色，但你依然可以辨认出各个片段：黑板上钉满了相片和图纸。

En: The huldur, the dweorg, the humans, and even headless men... all of them. Purged. Imagine a world filled *only* with welkin! Green welkin, dread welkin... and the high welkin to rule them all.
Zh: 幽灵、矮人、人类，甚至还有无头人……所有一切。统统都被净化了。想象一个*只有*精灵的世界！绿色的精灵，可怕的精灵……还有高阶精灵在统治着一切。

En: *Translucent welkins* with organs shining under their skin, and even aether welkins -- hailing from the vast emptiness of sidereal space.
Zh: *透明精灵*的器官在皮肤下面闪闪烁烁，甚至还有以太精灵——来自浩瀚的恒星空间。

En: Examine the welkin -- this is important.
Zh: 检查精灵——这很重要。

En: It's Vaarahamira, a *high welkin* -- his face white and scarred like cracked marble. This is clearly a welkin supremacist. The note says: 'All non-welkin races will be purged.'
Zh: 这是瓦拉哈米希拉，一个*高阶精灵*——它的脸是白色的，像碎裂的大理石一样疤痕累累。这明显就是个精灵至上主义者。注释上写着：“所有非精灵种族都将被净化。”

En: I don't care -- step back.
Zh: 我不在乎——退后。

En: But we're looking for a fridge and we're pressed on time -- so...
Zh: “但是我们在找冰箱啊，而且时间紧迫——所以……”

En: A radiocomputer... says the lieutenant, watching you circle around the machine. "Just sitting here without anyone in sight." He sounds surprised and a bit cautious.
Zh: “一台无线电脑……”警督看着围着机器转圈的你说到。“只是放在这里，周围一个人影也没有。”他听起来有些吃惊，还有一点好奇。

En: Another radiocomputer... says the lieutenant, watching you circle around the machine. "Just sitting here without anyone in sight."
Zh: “又是一台无线电脑……”警督看着围着机器转圈的你说到。“只是坐在这里，一个人都看不到。”

En: Do you think I should turn it on?
Zh: “你觉得我应该把它打开吗？”

En: Maybe the body has *stopped* decomposing somehow... He looks over his shoulder. "I don't know. You're in charge of this expedition, officer."
Zh: “也许尸体已经*停止*腐烂了……”他回头看了看。“我不知道。这次探险是你做主，警官。”

En: It's your call. He looks over his shoulder, then back at the machine. "You're in charge of this expedition, officer."
Zh: “你来决定。”他回头看了一眼，然后看向机器。“这次探险是你做主，警官。”

En: We have one of these down at the station, but I never really learned how to use it.
Zh: “我们警局里也有一台这东西，但是我从来没能真正学习过怎么使用。”

En: This is the Rehm Civic radiocomputer, model RC5120, equipped with a Feld mainframe and a Rehm-compatible printer.
Zh: 这是一台雷姆思域无线电脑，型号是RC5120，配有菲尔德主机和一台雷姆兼容打印机。

En: Oh no... it was already glowing and now it's also making a *sound*? It's probably some alien Seolite technology...
Zh: 哦不……它已经开始发光了，而且还发出了*声响*？这也许是西奥的外星科技……

En: The static gets louder, slowly filling up the abandoned hall. Until a voice speaks out, crackling and old, cutting into the air...
Zh: 电流声越来越响，慢慢充满整个废弃大堂。直到一个声音出现，破裂而古老，划破了周围的空气……

En: Good evening, Fortress Accident on Rue de Saint-Ghislaine, this is East-Insulindian Repeater Station 1.
Zh: “晚上好。圣吉莱纳路的要塞事故公司，这里是东伊苏林迪1号中继站。”

En: Good morning, Fortress Accident on Rue de Saint-Ghislaine, this is East-Insulindian Repeater Station 1.
Zh: “早上好。圣吉莱纳路的要塞事故公司，这里是东伊苏林迪1号中继站。”

En: Good afternoon, Fortress Accident on Rue de Saint-Ghislaine, this is East-Insulindian Repeater Station 1.
Zh: “下午好。圣吉莱纳路的要塞事故公司，这里是东伊苏林迪1号中继站。”

En: Yes, I am alive. I am 74 years old and my name is Yvonne.
Zh: “是的，我是活人。今年74岁，我的名字叫伊温妮。”

En: What are you, a *machine*? Or are you *alive*?
Zh: “你是什么，一台*机器*吗？或者，你是*活人*吗？”

En: Yes, I am alive. I am 74 years old and my name is Yvonne. Now please repeat, is this the production schedule?
Zh: “是的，我是活人。今年74岁，我的名字叫伊温妮。现在请重复，这是生产计划吗？”

En: Okay, but where are you? How did you know where I am?
Zh: “好吧，但你在哪？你怎么知道我在哪？”

En: I work as a repeater at the East-Insulindian Repeater Station. It's my job to know where you are, Fortress Accident.
Zh: “我是东伊苏林迪中继站的一名转发员。我的工作就是知道你在哪，要塞事故。”

En: Lonely? For the first time you hear her chuckle through the rustle of static. "Why would it get lonely, I get to talk to people all day."
Zh: “孤独？”这是你第一次在沙沙的电流声中听到她的笑声。“为什么会孤独呢，我一整天都在跟人说话。”

En: As for me, well... Some static. Then... "I am sitting in my cubicle surrounded by a wall of radios."
Zh: “至于我吗，呃……”一些电流音。紧接着……“我正坐在自己的小隔间里，被一面收音机墙包围着。”

En: On an island on the River Esperance, a small woman, all skin and bones, sits in a room filled with audio equipment. Thousands of tiny lights are reflected back from her prescription lenses, like stars in the dark.
Zh: 埃斯佩兰斯河的一个岛屿上，一个瘦骨嶙峋的矮小女人，正坐在一间堆满音频设备的房间里。千万个小光点从她的医学镜片上反射回来，就像黑夜中的点点繁星。

En: Now please tell me if there's anything else I can do, Fortress Accident.
Zh: “现在请告诉我，我还能为你做些什么吗，要塞事故。”

En: The lieutenant whispers into your ear: "She repeats passwords. Programming people are all... paranoid."
Zh: 警督在你耳边低语几句：“她会重复密码。程序员都是……偏执的妄想狂。”

En: Doesn't it get lonely, sitting there all by herself?
Zh: 她自己一个人坐着，难道不会觉得孤独吗？

En: Yvonne, my partner tells me that you're here because radiocomputer guys are all paranoid.
Zh: “伊温妮，我的搭档告诉我，你在这里是因为无线电脑的那帮家伙都是偏执妄想狂。”

En: Let it go.
Zh: 随它去吧。

En: Doesn't it get lonely, doing this job?
Zh: “做这份工作，不会觉得孤独吗？”

En: Now please tell me the reason for your call, Fortress Accident.
Zh: “现在请告诉我你呼叫的理由，要塞事故。”

En: They're merely cautious, says the old lady. "It's my job to protect their filaments as a password repeater at the East-Insulindian Station."
Zh: “他们只不是过谨慎而已，”老妇人说到。“作为东伊苏林迪中继站的一名密码转发员，我的工作就是保护他们的纤维内存。”

En: The filament you have inserted into the reader.
Zh: “就是你插入读卡器的纤维内存。”

En: What's the production schedule?
Zh: “生产计划是什么？”

En: Is it my birthday?
Zh: “是我的生日吗？”

En: I don't know the password.
Zh: “我不知道密码。”

En: You mean that glowing thing I put inside?
Zh: “你是说我放进去的那个发光的东西？”

En: Still no.
Zh: “还是不对。”

En: Received. I will *register* this log-in attempt.
Zh: “收到。我会*登记*下这次尝试登录的行为。”

En: Ah, right. Thanks for the explanation.
Zh: “啊，好吧。感谢你的说明。”

En: That sounds bad -- a log-in *attempt*. Something a criminal would do.
Zh: 听起来挺糟糕的——登录*尝试*。这是罪犯会做的事。

En: Fortress Accident, is there anything else I can do for you today?
Zh: “要塞事故，今天还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Open the hatch on the compartment. Don't touch the wires, a current is running through it, but the core is safe. There's a filament inside -- I need to know what it's called.
Zh: “打开隔间的舱门。不要碰电线，它是通电的，不过核心是安全的。里面有一个纤维内存——我需要知道它叫什么。”

En: Don't worry. Passwords have a way of *turning up* sooner or later.
Zh: 别担心。密码迟早会*出现*的。

En: Yes. Is that the production schedule?
Zh: “没错。它是生产计划吗？”

En: That was a question. Have you inserted it into the core?
Zh: “这是个问题。你把它插入核心了吗？”

En: This is the police. Please open this thing.
Zh: “我是警察。请打开这个东西。”

En: Say: "After life -- death."
Zh: 说：“生后的世界——死亡。”

En: I swear I haven't touched anything!
Zh: “我发誓自己什么也没碰！”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: I don't know.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: No.
Zh: “不行。”

En: The voice recites: "I am contractually obliged to protect the privacy of the filament holder Fortress Accident. Without filing a warrant with Lintel, I cannot give you access to this filament."
Zh: 声音背诵着：“根据契约，我必须保护要塞事故纤维内存持有人的隐私。如果没有林特尔I的搜查令，我不能让你接触这个纤维内存。”

En: Good. Please repeat the password.
Zh: “很好。请重复密码。”

En: A password? I'm really bad at passwords. Can you give me a hint?
Zh: “密码？我很不擅长记密码。你能给我个提示吗？”

En: The old lady sighs, as a wave of static distorts her voice to a robotic sprawl. "If you are unsure, could you please take a look?"
Zh: 老妇人叹了口气，一阵失真的电流把她的声音扭曲成机械杂音。“如果你不确定的话，能麻烦你看一眼吗？”

En: What, you're just going to guess? The lieutenant shakes his head, staring at the glowing radiocomputer. Seems like the repeater station hasn't heard his words however, for the line is still silent.
Zh: “什么，你打算用猜的吗？”警督盯着发光的无线电脑，摇了摇头。中继站好像没有听到他说的话，不过，是因为线路还没有接通。

En: I'm afraid we're not doing that. Unless we want to wait for a month.
Zh: “恐怕我们不能这么做。除非我们想等上一个月。”

En: How do I do that?
Zh: “我该怎么做？”

En: Now, can you please repeat the password? the voice in the machine asks again. She sounds cold in the damp air.
Zh: “现在，能请你重复密码吗？”机器里的声音再次发问。她的声音在潮湿的空气中显得有些冰冷。

En: Hmm, she hums.
Zh: “嗯，”她低吟到。

En: That's not bad.
Zh: 这也不算太糟糕。

En: Why did you call me Fortress Accident?
Zh: “你为什么叫我要塞事故？”

En: Fortress Accident is the company on whose name the terminal you're currently using has been registered to.
Zh: “你现在使用的注册终端所属的公司名称就是要塞事故。”

En: Do you have any other information about this company?
Zh: “关于这家公司，你还有其他信息吗？”

En: 'Fortress Accident SCA produces revolutionary interactive call-in radio games' -- that's what the catalogue says.
Zh: “‘要塞事故SCA开发了革命性的互动呼叫无线电游戏’——清单上是这么写的。”

En: One moment... You hear her flip through a catalogue, before she reads out with studious care:
Zh: “等一下……”你听见她翻阅清单的声音，然后她小心翼翼地读了出来：

En: Wow. So conceptual.
Zh: “哇哦。这么概念化的吗。”

En: And what's that -- this 'interactive call-in radio game'?
Zh: “那又是什么——你说的这个‘互动呼叫无线电游戏’？”

En: What's the off-site copy?
Zh: “场外拷贝是什么？”

En: Yes. Is that the off-site copy?
Zh: “是的。这是场外拷贝吗？”

En: It's called the production schedule.
Zh: “它叫生产计划。”

En: It's called the off-site copy.
Zh: “它叫场外拷贝。”

En: Good, I've unlocked the off-site copy. After ending the call, please press PRINT to access the filament.
Zh: “很好，我已经解锁了场外拷贝。通话结束后，请点击打印键，访问纤维内存。”

En: Say: "After death -- life again."
Zh: 说：“死后的世界——又是新生。”

En: Really? She just used the same password? The lieutenant seems almost disappointed to discover that, as he murmurs:  "Maybe those radiocomputer guys aren't that paranoid after all..."
Zh: “真的吗？她用了相同的密码？”没有发现这一点，警督似乎有些失望，他嘟囔着：“也许那些无线电脑的家伙并不是偏执妄想狂……”

En: Good, I've unlocked the production schedule. After ending the call, please press PRINT to access the filament.
Zh: “很好，我已经解锁了生产计划。通话结束后，请点击打印键，访问纤维内存。”

En: Her answer is short and sharp: "No, that's not it."
Zh: 她的回答短促而尖锐：“不，不是的。”

En: What does she mean *that's not it*?! What's the password then???
Zh: 她说“不是的”是什么意思？！那密码会是什么？

En: Maybe it's the second part of the leitmotiv you saw on the stained glass window.
Zh: 也许你在彩色玻璃窗上看到的是主旨的第二部分。

En: No, that's not it.
Zh: “不，不是的。”

En: Unbelievable!!! Who came up with those stupid passwords!
Zh: 难以置信！！！到底是谁想出这么愚蠢的密码的！

En: *Ka-ching*!
Zh: *哗啦*！

En: Received. I will *register* this log-in attempt.
Zh: “收到。我会*登记*下这次尝试登录的行为。”

En: Let's try the password for the production schedule.
Zh: “我们试试生产计划的密码。”

En: I might have a password for the off-site copy.
Zh: “我可能有场外拷贝的密码。”

En: Yes, I am alive. I am 74 years old and my name is Yvonne. Now please repeat, is this the off-site copy?
Zh: “是的，我是活人。今年74岁，我的名字叫伊温妮。现在请重复，这是场外拷贝吗？”

En: Yvonne, it's me again! How are you?
Zh: “伊温妮，又是我！你好吗？”

En: Good, thank you. It's not clear whether she recognizes your voice. "Please repeat, is this the production schedule?"
Zh: “很好，谢谢。”不清楚她有没有认出你的声音。“请重复，这是生产计划吗？”

En: Good, thank you. It's not clear whether she recognizes your voice. "Please repeat, how can I help you?"
Zh: “很好，谢谢。”不清楚她有没有认出你的声音。“请重复，我有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Good, thank you. It's not clear whether she recognizes your voice. "Please repeat, is this the off-site copy?"
Zh: “很好，谢谢。”不清楚她有没有认出你的声音。“请重复，这是场外拷贝吗？”

En: Read about capital... I want to know about *money*.
Zh: 说到资金……我想知道*钱*的事。

En: Read about the workforce... Who worked there, how long?
Zh: 说到职员总数……谁在这里工作，工作了多长时间？

En: Skim through the production schedule, whatever it is.
Zh: 不管这个生产计划是什么，快速浏览一下。

En: Gosh, where did they get all this money?
Zh: 天啦，他们是从哪里搞到这些钱的？

En: In its short time of existence, Fortress Accident SCA managed to burn through truly *insane* amounts of money.
Zh: 在要塞事故SCA短暂的存续期间内，他们成功地烧掉了一笔*疯狂*数额的金钱。

En: Let's just say it was a *real adventure* for their Igaunijan investor.
Zh: 这么说吧，对于他们的依格纳斯投资人来说，这是一次*真正的冒险*。

En: The production schedule depicts their glorious descent into bankruptcy.
Zh: 这份生产计划详细描述了他们从辉煌到陷入破产境地的过程。

En: This must be the anomaly Soona mentioned in the church log.
Zh: 这一定就是那个不正常的苏娜在教堂日志里提到的东西。

En: Read the notes at the end.
Zh: 阅读最后的注释。

En: Four months later by an unknown author: "I am the only one left and it's gotten rather damp here. A few other businesses have gone under too. Slipstream switched to making skis and the hairdressers just left, cursing Martinaise. They're right, though, something's seriously wrong with this place. Martinaise, all of it."
Zh: 四个月之后，一个未知的作者留下了这段话：“只剩我一个人留下来了，这里真的变得特别潮湿。还有一些公司也破产了。气流公司变成了滑雪板制作商，美发师刚离开，诅咒该死的马丁内斯。不过他们说得对，这个地方真的不太对劲。马丁内斯，还有这里的一切。”

En: I didn't have a home anymore, so I started keeping it in the basement, in the ice bear refrigerator (near where I went to sleep). It was perfectly safe there, the temperature conditions were *optimal*.
Zh: “我再也没有家了，所以我把它保存在地下室，就放在冰熊冰箱（靠近我睡觉的地方）。它在那里非常安全，气温条件也是*最理想*的。”

En: Seems like a perfectly reasonable explanation.
Zh: 看起来似乎是一个非常合理的解释。

En: It's not very convincing, is it?
Zh: 不是很有说服力，对吗？

En: Is there anything else from this lead programmer? (Proceed.)
Zh: 主程序员还留下什么信息吗？（继续。）

En: The production schedule ends with a few random notes that seem to be added sometime later.
Zh: 生产计划的末尾是一些随机的注释，好像是那之后加上的。

En: Really? She just used the same password? The lieutenant seems almost disappointed to discover that.
Zh: “真的吗？她用了相同的密码？”没有发现这一点，警督似乎有些失望。

En: They tried to make a 4,000,000 reál game with 400,000 in their bank account.
Zh: 他们想用银行账户里的400,000雷亚尔，做出一个价值4,000,000雷亚尔的游戏。

En: They thought they could bridge the gap with pure willpower and imagination.
Zh: 他们觉得可以用纯粹的意志力和想象力来缩小这个差距。

En: The first tranche of seed financing brought in 150,000 reál, but then came the *delays*.
Zh: 第一批种子投资带来了150,000雷亚尔，但是之后*延期*就出现了。

En: Eventually the damage reached 400,000 reál with only half of the game finished.
Zh: 最终，损失达到了400,000雷亚尔，但是游戏仅仅完成了一半。

En: The first few pages give an overview of the capital and workforce, while the rest of it seems to be a production schedule.
Zh: 最初的几页对资金和职员总数做了一个概述，其余的部分好像是生产计划。

En: On the other hand... it's not that I'm *not* interested in abandoned radiocomputers.
Zh: 另一方面……并不是说我对废弃的无线电脑*不感兴趣*。

En: Is the lieutenant a little... scared? Just now when he looked around.
Zh: 警督是不是有点……害怕？就在他刚才四下查看的时候。

En: You can always do it *after* the fridge.
Zh: 你可以在冰箱*之后*做这个。

En: The dusty radiocomputer sleeps on its wired frame, forgotten and unused. Its keyboard has a rectangular ON/OFF button; a piece of paper still hangs from the printer.
Zh: 一台积满灰尘的无线电脑沉睡在有线框架上，被人闲置，被人遗忘。它的键盘上有一个矩形的开启/关闭按钮；还有一张纸挂在打印机上。

En: Press PLAY again.
Zh: 再次按下播放按钮。

En: This is a *human interface* -- a retired person sitting behind a switchboard somewhere.
Zh: 这是一个*人机界面*——在某个地方的总机背后，坐着一个退休的人。

En: Password. Of course it would have a password. That's why there's a human administrator involved.
Zh: 密码。当然要密码了。这就是人类管理员参与的原因。

En: You should ask her for a hint.
Zh: 你应该找她要个提示。

En: A hint system is not part of the protocol for repeater stations.
Zh: 提示系统不属于中继站协议的一部分。

En: Don't worry. Passwords have a way of *turning up* sooner or later.
Zh: 别担心。密码迟早会*出现*的。

En: That's why she does this.
Zh: 这就是她这么做的原因。

En: Like a smooth drawer, the filament slides into place. On the keyboard, the PLAY key starts blinking.
Zh: 纤维内存像抽屉一样平稳地滑进自己的位置。键盘上的‘播放’按钮开始闪烁。

En: What he means is that these things cost *money*. Why would anyone just leave it behind?
Zh: 他的意思是说这些东西是要花*钱*的。为什么会有人就这样把它抛在脑后呢？

En: The static drowns her response. "Any other questions?" you hear her ask when the connection finally improves.
Zh: 电流声吞没了她的回应。“还有其他问题吗？”连接最终变好的时候，你听见她如此问到。

En: I don't really care. (Look at the table of contents again.)
Zh: 我真的不在乎。（再次查看清单。）

En: Her former colleagues would agree with you.
Zh: 她的前同事会赞同你的。

En: That's not what her colleagues thought.
Zh: 她的同事可不是这么想的。

En: Saint-Brune 1147? That's what the street sign next to the church said...
Zh: 圣布鲁内1147号？教堂旁边的路标上就是这么写的……

En: Tiles on the cube are still smouldering, casting the framework in a soft glow. Virescent PLAY and PRINT keys shine on the keyboard.
Zh: 立方体上的方块还亮着，在框架上投射出柔和的光。淡绿色的‘播放’和‘打印’按钮在键盘上闪耀。

En: This must be the information Soona is looking for -- she knows how to make sense of it.
Zh: 这肯定就是苏娜寻找的信息——她知道如何让它变得有意义。

En: Marked by the devil itself!
Zh: 它被恶魔本身标记了！

En: Looks like gibberish. Better get running again.
Zh: 看起来像是无用数据。最好再运转一次。

En: Of course you should turn it on -- don't you want to know what's on the filament?!
Zh: 你当然应该把它打开了——你难道不想知道纤维内存上有什么吗？

En: Four-hundred thousand reál?! Jommaijoo, these guys knew how to party!
Zh: 400,000雷亚尔？！我的个乖乖，这帮人真的很懂怎么狂欢！

En: Igaunija -- a god-forsaken (and tiny) territory in Graad that has not once, but twice wasted its independence on microfascism. The less said about Igaunija the better. Matter of fact, let's stop saying things about it right now and continue what we were doing.
Zh: 依格纳斯——格拉德的一个堕落（而且很小）的领域，不只一次，而是两次浪费了微小法西斯主义的独立性。越少提到依格纳斯就越好。事实上，我们现在就别提了，继续该做的事吧。

En: She's right, actually -- nothing wrong with keeping this type of filament memory in a fridge.
Zh: 其实，她是对的——把这种纤维内存保存在冰箱里并没有错。

En: This appears to be some kind of machine with a cube-shaped heart and a wired framework. The keyboard has a rectangular ON/OFF button; a piece of paper still hangs from the printer.
Zh: 这像是某种机器，拥有一颗立方体形的心脏和有线框架。键盘上有一个矩形的开启/关闭按钮；打印机上还挂着一张纸。

En: Look inside the compartment.
Zh: 看看隔间里面。

En: Read the printout.
Zh: 阅读打印出的资料。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: It's empty like a beehive without its brood. Some cables have been left dangling, disconnected...
Zh: 它就像幼蜂的蜂巢一样空洞。有些电线缠绕在一起，没有插电……

En: It's blank. The lower edge of the paper has been torn off.
Zh: 它是空的。纸张的下缘已经被撕掉了。

En: Press PRINT.
Zh: 按下打印键。

En: Behind the hatch sits a cube-like crisscross of filaments, smouldering in the dark like fireflies. Silver tape on the side says in a black marker: "THE OFF-SITE COPY".
Zh: 舱门后面是一个立方体、十字形的纤维内存，像萤火虫一样在黑暗中缓缓燃烧着。侧面银色的胶带上用黑色马克笔写着：“场外拷贝”。

En: Behind the hatch sits a cube-like crisscross of filaments, smouldering in the dark like fireflies. Silver tape on the side says in a black marker: "THE PRODUCTION SCHEDULE".
Zh: 舱门后面是一个立方体、十字形的纤维内存，像萤火虫一样在黑暗中缓缓燃烧着。侧面银色的胶带上用黑色马克笔写着：“生产计划”。

En: It sounds like something cracks, before the piece of paper starts filling up with pure black ink.
Zh: 听起来像是什么东西裂开的声音，接着一张白纸逐渐被纯黑色的墨水填满。

En: With a quiet determination the printer starts printing, a piece of paper unfolding like a hand-held fan. A black criss-cross of letters covers its surface.
Zh: 打印机默默地下定决心，开始了打印工作，一张白纸像折扇一样缓缓展开。一片纵横交错的黑色字母覆盖了整个表面。

En: Nothing happens.
Zh: 什么也没发生。

En: The paper is soaked with ink, its monochrome darkness spanning from margin to margin... It's not possible to make out any information.
Zh: 白纸浸满墨水，单色的黑暗从一个边缘跨越到另一个边缘……根本不可能辨认出任何信息。

En: Tear off the printout and throw it away.
Zh: 撕下打印资料，把它扔掉。

En: Fortress Accident employed 18 people, the bulk of the team composed of writers and concept artists. There were also radio programmers, sound engineers, a CEO, two marketing experts...
Zh: 要塞事故雇佣了18名员工，团队主要由作家和概念艺术家组成。同时还有无线电程序员，音响师，一名CEO，还有两个营销专家……

En: Wait, why did a *radio game* need so many artists?
Zh: 等等，一个*无线游戏*要这么多艺术家干嘛？

En: It didn't.
Zh: 它不用。

En: It didn't need so many concept artists?
Zh: 它不需要这么多概念艺术家？

En: No, definitely not. A few more producers could have come handy though -- especially when dealing with writers, some of whom routinely skipped work because of 'mental health issues' and extremely unprofessional sleep schedules...
Zh: 不，绝对不需要。不过再多加点制作人会有用很多——特别是对付作家的时候，有些人总是会因为“精神健康问题”和极其不专业的睡眠时间旷工……

En: Right, what happened?
Zh: 好吧，出什么事了？

En: Turn on the machine.
Zh: 打开机器。

En: [Step away.]
Zh: [走开。]

En: The machine lights up like some prehistoric animal stirring from its slumber, revealing virescent PLAY and PRINT keys on the keyboard. The hatch on the machine's central compartment is wide open.
Zh: 键盘像某种从沉睡中醒来的史前动物一样点亮了，淡绿色的‘播放’和‘打印’按钮展现在你眼前。机器中央室的舱门大敞开来。

En: But on the other hand, their obligations *were* piling up at an inhuman rate -- a rate that could only be amended by Pyrholidon.
Zh: 但是另一方面，他们的债务*确实*在以惨无人道的速度堆积——那种速度只有吡嚯浣酮才能修正。

En: This is where the machine's memory should go.
Zh: 机器内存应该放在这个地方。

En: This is where the memory should go, the lieutenant notes, observing the machine.
Zh: “这就是放内存的地方，”金观察着机器说到。

En: Insert THE OFF-SITE COPY.
Zh: 插入场外拷贝。

En: Insert THE PRODUCTION SCHEDULE.
Zh: 插入生产计划。

En: Remove THE OFF-SITE COPY.
Zh: 取出场外拷贝。

En: Remove THE PRODUCTION SCHEDULE.
Zh: 取出生产计划。

En: Like a smooth drawer, the filament slides into place. On the keyboard, the PLAY key starts blinking.
Zh: 纤维内存像顺滑的抽屉一样滑进自己的位置。键盘上的‘播放’按钮开始闪烁。

En: Have you stirred the ghost of the Doomed Commercial Area from its rest? Could this be its dismembered heart, beginning to flutter?
Zh: 难道你唤醒了在这个诅咒商业区里沉睡的幽灵？这是不是它那被肢解的心脏，又重新开始震颤？

En: Please repeat -- is this the production schedule?
Zh: “请重复——这是生产计划吗？”

En: Please repeat, is this the off-site copy?
Zh: “请重复，这是场外拷贝吗？”

En: That's all for now. (Press OFF/SILENT.)
Zh: “现在先到此为止吧。”（按下关闭/休眠。）

En: The filaments you have would fit perfectly inside the compartment.
Zh: 你手中的纤维内存能够完美地贴合隔间内部。

En: The filament you have would fit perfectly inside the compartment.
Zh: 你手中的纤维内存完美地贴进了隔间内部。

En: Thank you and good bye, the old lady's voice disappears along with the static.
Zh: “谢谢，再见，”老女人的声音随着电流的声音一同消失了。

En: The speaker comes to life, static seeps through the machine's planar magnetic driver. An old lady greets you, her voice sounds a hundred years old...
Zh: 扬声器重新恢复了生机，电流渗入机器的平面磁性驱动。一个老妇人向你问好，她的声音听起来像是有一百岁了……

En: The filament slides out of its glowing nest.
Zh: 纤维内存从发光的巢穴中滑了出来。

En: ...and a single overburdened producer who developed a habit of popping Pyrholidon in the basement to escape his obligations.
Zh: ……一个负担过重的制作人，染上了在地下室爆嗑吡嚯浣酮来逃避责任的习惯。

En: Why did Fortress Accident have so many concept artists?
Zh: 为什么要塞事故有这么多概念艺术家？

En: One of them even managed to steal some valuable company property before skipping town for good.
Zh: 有一个人在永远离开这里之前，甚至还偷走了一些宝贵的公司财产。

En: Look at the table of contents.
Zh: 看看清单。

En: The first few pages give an overview of the capital and workforce, while the rest of it seems to be a production schedule.
Zh: 最初的几页对资金和职员总数做了一个概述，其余的部分好像是生产计划。

En: It was impossible not to fail. The project was too large and no amount of money could satiate the ever-expanding ambitions of the development team.
Zh: 不失败是不可能的。这项目太大，多少钱也满足不了这个开发团队不断膨胀的野心。

En: Tiles on the cube are still smouldering, casting the framework in a soft glow. Virescent PLAY and PRINT keys shine on the keyboard.
Zh: 立方体上的方块还亮着，在框架上投射出柔和的光。淡绿色的‘播放’和‘打印’按钮在键盘上闪耀。

En: It's a project report written by the lead producer Andrew 'Andy' Schott about 'Wirrâl Untethered', a radio game developed by studio Fortress Accident.
Zh: 这是一份关于“自由威勒尔”的项目报告，作者是主制作人安德鲁·‘安迪’·肖特，那是一个由要塞事故工作室开发的无线游戏。

En: Examine the printout again.
Zh: 再次检查打印资料。

En: Dresscode: Paper-jockey.
Zh: 着装要求：资料师。

En: A single speck of white shines out from the shade -- for some reason the printer decided to spare this one tiny dot of paper.
Zh: 一个白色的小斑点从阴影中脱颖而出——出于某种原因，这台打印机决定放过纸上的这个小点。

En: Tear off the printout.
Zh: 撕碎打印资料。

En: It's just black upon black, nothing to see here.
Zh: 只是黑漆漆的一片，没什么好看的。

En: What happened? It's just covered in ink.
Zh: “发生什么事了？这上面全是墨水。”

En: Odd. The lieutenant runs his hand over the ink-soaked paper, staining his fingertips. "Something is obviously broken here."
Zh: “奇怪。”警督用手抚过这张浸满墨水的纸张，连指尖都染上了黑色。“这里肯定有什么东西坏掉了。”

En: A dusty radiocomputer sleeps on its wired frame, forgotten and unused. Its keyboard has a rectangular ON/OFF button; a piece of paper still hangs from the printer.
Zh: 一台积满灰尘的无线电脑沉睡在有线框架上，被人闲置，被人遗忘。它的键盘上有一个矩形的开启/关闭按钮；还有一张纸挂在打印机上。

En: Press PLAY.
Zh: 按下播放键。

En: A bar of fabric right above the keyboard starts producing a soft hum. The sound of static seeps through the machine's planar magnetic driver.
Zh: 键盘正上方的一块组件开始发出轻柔的哼唱。电流的声音渗入机器的平面磁性驱动。

En: Nothing happens.
Zh: 什么也没发生。

En: Something's missing -- there's no tape in the player.
Zh: 还缺了点什么——播放器里没有磁带。

En: Oh, so they were done in by their own ambition.
Zh: 哦，原来他们被自己的野心毁掉了。

En: No. Even then success remained within an ever-narrowing margin of possibility that, despite everything, never entirely disappeared...
Zh: 不。即使这样，成功的可能性依然残留在一个不断缩小的边缘，尽管如此，它还是没有完全消失……

En: The what?
Zh: 什么？

En: That sounds bad.
Zh: 听起来挺糟糕的。

En: At the eleventh hour, the lead designer, Zsiemsk-born Sulisław Zawisza, decided that what 'Wirrâl Untethered' needed was a secret mystical location at the extreme edge of the map...
Zh: 在最后时刻，主设计师，齐姆斯基出生的苏里斯洛·扎维沙决定自由威勒尔需要的是一个地图最边缘的神秘地点……

En: That's pretty bad. So that's what did them in?
Zh: 那还真是挺糟糕的。所以这就是他们精疲力竭的原因？

En: Well, yeah. That, and the catastrophic data loss.
Zh: 呃，是的。那个，还有灾难性的数据丢失。

En: The what now?
Zh: 什么？

En: On the nature of the data loss, there's ominously little information in the production log. It comes at the very end, where things get fuzzy and dark, where tables and numbers seem to vanish into an eerie nothingness, before their Igaunijan investor pulled the plug...
Zh: 数据丢失在产品日志中几乎没有出现任何预兆信息。它出现在最后，事情变得模糊又黑暗的地方；在那里，表格和数字好像消失在一种可怕的虚无中，最后那位依格纳斯的投资人拔下了插头……

En: And the anomaly caused all the data to get lost in the air?
Zh: 就是这个异常导致所有数据都凭空消失了？

En: When the project was returned, it was completely blank.
Zh: 当项目恢复的时候，它已经变成了一片空白。

En: The team spent weeks on the phone with Lintel, the service provider, but despite their diagnostics they could never produce a satisfactory explanation or pay for the loss.
Zh: 团队花了好几周时间跟服务提供商林特尔通电话，尽管有诊断，他们也永远不可能作出一个令人满意的解释，也无力赔偿损失。

En: Wasn't there a copy of the game, a *backup*?
Zh: 这个游戏不是有份拷贝吗，一个*备份*？

En: Mysteriously enough, it seems that the off-site copy happened to be *on-site* when the catastrophic data loss occurred...
Zh: 难以理解的是，那场灾难性的数据丢失事件发生时，那份场外拷贝碰巧正处于*现场*状态……

En: S. Luukanen-Kilde, the lead programmer of Fortress Accident: "The off-site copy was on-site because there was no *off-site* anymore, not for me, not after eight months of crunch."
Zh: 苏娜·卢凯伦-吉尔德，要塞事故的主程序员：“场外拷贝处于现场状态，是因为已经没有*场外*了，至少对于我来说没有，特别是在8个月的危机之后。”

En: Still haven't gotten an answer from Lintel about what happened. All I could get were the physical coordinates of the error on the East-Insulindian front on that day. Since the computation happened on-air, I reckoned it had to coincide with an actually existing location...
Zh: “还是没有从林特尔公司那里得到事故原因的回复。我唯一得到的消息，就是东伊苏林迪前端当天的物理坐标出现了错误。由于计算是在播出中发生的，我估计它跟某个真实存在的地点恰巧重合了……”

En: How many heads were there?
Zh: 到底有多少头？

En: So many. At last count there were approximately 10,000 heads for 10,000 headless men, all of which could be endlessly recombined.
Zh: 很多。最后一次计算的时候大约是10,000个头配10,000个无头人，这些都可以不断地重复组合。

En: I have compared the coordinates to a map of Revachol West. Turns out it's only 800 metres from here. The address is Saint-Brune 1147. I am going there to look this thing in the eye...
Zh: “我对比了瑞瓦肖西部的地图和这些坐标。结果它离这里只有800米远。地址是圣布鲁内1147号。我打算亲眼去看看那个地方……”

En: They couldn't.
Zh: 他们不能。

En: That is, until they discovered the Valley of the Heads.
Zh: 也就是说，直到他们发现了人头谷。

En: This place was to be the *Valley of the Heads* -- where the heads of all the *headless* constructs could be found. The player would have been able to choose a head for their headless party member, and each head would been voiced on-air by a professional actor.
Zh: 这个地方叫*人头谷*——所有*无头*构图的头部都能在这里找到。玩家可以为他们的无头队员选择一个头部，每一个头都由专业演员广播配音。

En: What is clear is that one day an unidentified numeric anomaly occurred on the East-Insulindian Lintel front, just as the 'Wirrâl Untethered' project was being compiled that day.
Zh: 现在清楚的是：某一天，东伊苏林迪的林特尔前端发生了一起未识别的数字异常事故，恰巧就是自由威勒尔项目开始编程的那一天。

En: It was the lead programmer's responsibility to oversee weekly maintenance of the off-site copy and, well, keep it off-site. An explanatory note from the lead programmer has been attached.
Zh: 主程序员的职责是监督每周场外拷贝的维护，呃，就是让它保持场外状态。还附加一份主程序员的注释。

En: What does it say?
Zh: 上面写着什么？

En: Because of the concept artists?
Zh: 因为概念艺术家？

En: No, not the concept artists. It wasn't even the writers, with their panic attacks and three-hour lunches...
Zh: 不，不是概念艺术家。甚至都不是那些有恐慌症，而且午餐要吃三个小时的作家……

En: I could have bridged the gap.
Zh: 我本来可以缩小差距的。

En: No, you couldn't have.
Zh: 不，你做不到。

En: Definitely not.
Zh: 绝对不可以。

En: No, it was good. Too good.
Zh: 不，它很好。太好了。

En: What do you mean? I totally could have.
Zh: 你什么意思？我完全可以做到。

En: Not a chance.
Zh: 没可能的。

En: Even then, success remained within an ever-narrowing margin of possibility that, despite everything, never entirely disappeared...
Zh: 即便如此，成功的可能性依然残留在一个不断缩小的边缘，尽管如此，它还是没有完全消失……

En: This is some insane shit. Who were these people?
Zh: 这他妈的真是疯了。这些都是什么人？

En: The world had never seen their kind before and might never again.
Zh: 这个世界之前从未见过、可能以后再也见不到的那一类。

En: How many combinations could you make out of that?
Zh: 你能做出多少种组合？

En: Do you really want to know? There seems to be a calculation here, but it may take a while...
Zh: 你真想知道啊吗？似乎要计算一下，不过可能要花一点时间……

En: Yeah, how bad could it be?
Zh: 是啊，能有多糟糕呢？

En: No, let's not go there.
Zh: 不，还是别搞到那个地步。

En: Good call. It's more than the number of atoms in the observable universe.
Zh: 说得好。它比可观测宇宙中的原子数量还要多。

En: Keep going.
Zh: 继续。

En: Uh oh.
Zh: 啊哦。

En: What have I done?
Zh: 我做了什么？

En: Keep going.
Zh: 继续。

En: Please stop.
Zh: 请停下。

En: Just let the numbers wash over you.
Zh: 让这些数字淹没你吧。

En: Okay, so that's what did them in?
Zh: 好吧，所以这就是他们精疲力尽的原因？

En: I didn't understand a word of that.
Zh: 我一个字也不理解。

En: Something spooky happened with the machines and the game was lost.
Zh: 这台机器发生了灵异事件，然后游戏没了。

En: What! They lost the whole game and wouldn't even pay for it?
Zh: 什么？他们搞丢了整个游戏，甚至连钱不赔的吗？

En: Always read the terms of service.
Zh: 永远要记得阅读服务协议。

En: The lieutenant taps his foot impatiently, his arms folded tight against his chest.
Zh: 警督不耐烦地用脚怕打着地面，双臂紧紧抱在胸前。

En: He's dying for a cigarette. Come to think of it, you are, too.
Zh: 他渴望一只香烟。这么一想，你也是。

En: And let's face it, they didn't have any money left for a legal action.
Zh: 让我们面对现实吧，他们根本没钱打官司了。

En: What if you broke the radiocomputer? What if it's never going to stop?!
Zh: 你不会把这台机器搞坏了吧？万一它永远也停不下来了怎么办？！

En: Something's broken. Machines aren't supposed to behave like this.
Zh: 有什么东西坏掉了。机器不应该有这种表现的。
